title: Extract testable logic from registryCreateFromNative (0%)
description:
**File:** registry_create_native.go
**Function:** registryCreateFromNative (line 19, 0%)
**Also covers:** promptUserScopedHooks (line 272, 0%)
**Blocker:** interactive-prompt (multi-step stdin via bufio throughout lines 55-118)
**Pattern:** var-seam + extract-pure-logic
**Effort:** L (> 90 min)

Multi-step interactive flow:
- Scans native provider content
- Prompts user to select provider or individual items
- Prompts about user-scoped hooks
- Creates registry scaffold with selected items

Recommended approach:
1. selectByProvider and selectIndividualItems already accept *bufio.Scanner — covered by separate bead
2. Extract the orchestration logic: given scan results + user selections → registry scaffold
3. promptUserScopedHooks: reads home dir settings + prompts via scanner. Needs home dir fixtures + scanner injection.
4. Test the orchestration with pre-determined selections (no interactive prompts)

Note: os.Getwd() dependency — tests need chdir with t.Cleanup restore.

title: ACP Spec: Mechanical fixes (M1-M8)
description:
## What

Fix 8 mechanical issues in acp-spec.md — typos, inconsistencies, and terminology errors that have a single correct answer.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

**M1: TV-15 per-file hash prefix (line 688)**
Remove `sha256:` prefix from the per-file hash on line 688. Section 7.2 step 3 says output is "64 lowercase hexadecimal characters" (bare hex). All other per-file hashes (TV-01 through TV-14) are bare. TV-15's should be too.

**M2: Schema example source_hash placeholder (line 131)**
Change `sha256:x9y8z7w6...` to valid hex prefix like `sha256:a1b2c3d4...`. Non-hex chars (x, y, z, w) are invalid.

**M3: "content directory" vs "content root" inconsistency**
Section 3 (line 30) defines "content directory." Section 7.3 step 2 (line 258) introduces "content root" without definition. Change "content root" to "content directory" on line 258.

**M4: source_commit normative strength (Section 6.3.9, line 179)**
Heading says "OPTIONAL, RECOMMENDED for public scope." Body says "SHOULD be included." OPTIONAL and SHOULD have different BCP 14 meanings. Change heading to "OPTIONAL" only, keep body's SHOULD.

**M5: TV-15 labeled "Informative" inside a normative appendix (line 680)**
Appendix B header says "Normative." TV-15 says "(Informative)." Resolution: Keep TV-15 as Informative but add a note explaining why — it demonstrates a design rationale, not a conformance requirement. Add "(Informative — demonstrates algorithm design rationale)" to the heading.

**M6: generated_by contradicts itself (Section 6.3.7, line 171)**
Line 171 says "does not affect provenance verification" but Section 8 includes ALL fields except meta_hash and signature in meta_hash. Changing generated_by DOES change meta_hash. Fix: change line 171 to "This field is informational — it records tool involvement but does not independently affect verification outcomes. It IS included in meta_hash computation (Section 8)."

**M7: TV-13 hex concatenation instruction (line 654)**
"Concatenate all lines (removing whitespace)" is ambiguous — "whitespace" could mean spaces only or newlines too. Change to: "Concatenate all lines, removing newlines and any leading/trailing spaces, to obtain the 134-character hex string (67 bytes)."

**M8: Appendix B verification status note (lines 439)**
The sentence "Before this specification advances beyond Draft status, vectors MUST be confirmed by at least two independent implementations in different languages" is a process/publication gate, not a test requirement. Move to a "Status of This Memo" subsection in the Introduction (after line 23), or add a "Document Status" section before Abstract.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] TV-15 per-file hash is bare hex (no sha256: prefix) on line 688
- [ ] Schema example source_hash uses valid hex characters only
- [ ] "content root" replaced with "content directory" in Section 7.3 step 2
- [ ] Section 6.3.9 heading says "OPTIONAL" without "RECOMMENDED"
- [ ] TV-15 heading clarifies informative status with rationale
- [ ] Section 6.3.7 body text acknowledges generated_by IS included in meta_hash
- [ ] TV-13 concatenation note specifies "newlines and any leading/trailing spaces"
- [ ] Verification status note moved out of normative test vector section
- [ ] No other sections changed — mechanical fixes only
- [ ] Spec still valid YAML in examples and valid markdown throughout
close_reason: All 8 mechanical fixes applied: M1 TV-15 prefix, M2 hex chars, M3 terminology, M4 heading, M5 TV-15 status, M6 generated_by, M7 concatenation note, M8 verification status moved

title: Update repo documentation for hook canonical spec
description:
Update all repository documentation to reflect the new hook canonical spec decisions.

## What Needs Updating
1. **README.md** — Update hook-related sections to reference canonical spec, neutral naming, capability model
2. **CLAUDE.md** — Update any hook references, add note about canonical format
3. **cli/CLAUDE.md** — Update hook converter package descriptions
4. **SECURITY.md** — Reference the spec's security considerations, threat model
5. **CLI help text** — Any hook-related commands should reference canonical event/tool names
6. **TUI** — Hook detail views, install flows, portability indicators should reflect new model
7. **.syllago.yaml** metadata format — Update to include new fields (signatures, content_hashes, author, degradation)
8. **Hook content in registries** — Any example hooks should use canonical format

## Why
The hook canonical spec introduces neutral naming (before_tool_execute instead of PreToolUse), typed matchers, capability inference, degradation strategies, and a new output schema. All documentation that references hooks needs to be consistent with the spec.

## Success Criteria
1. No references to old CC-biased canonical names (PreToolUse, Bash, etc.) in documentation (code can still have them for backward compat during migration)
2. README hook section describes the canonical format and portability model
3. CLI help for hook commands references canonical event names
4. TUI hook views show portability information from capability matrix
5. All example hooks in the repo use canonical format
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/documentation-updates.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: Task 2.4: Add split-view to Files tab with reusable splitViewModel
description:
# Split-View Files Tab

## Why
With Overview tab removed, the Files tab becomes the primary view. A split layout (file tree + preview) lets users see file contents without extra navigation. The splitViewModel is designed as a reusable component for Phase 3.

## Description
Transform the Files tab into a split-view layout.

### Component Design
Build as a standalone splitViewModel:
- Left pane: file tree with cursor, scroll
- Right pane: preview content with independent scroll
- focusedPane field (0=left, 1=right)
- collapsed bool for narrow terminals
- showingPreview bool for single-pane mode

### Layout
- Left pane: 35% width (min 25 chars)
- Right pane: 65% (remaining)
- Separator: | character
- Activation: content width >= 70 (not terminal width)
- Adaptive ratio: 40/60 at content widths 70-90, 35/65 at 100+

### Single-Pane Fallback (content width < 70)
Replace-in-place interaction:
- Mode 1 (file tree): full-width tree, Enter opens file
- Mode 2 (file content): full-width content replaces tree, Esc returns to tree with cursor preserved

### Primary File by Type
Skills -> SKILL.md, Agents -> .md file, Rules -> rule .md, Hooks -> config, MCP -> config, Commands -> command file, Loadouts -> loadout.yaml

### File Tree
- Directories always expanded (no collapse toggle)
- 2 spaces indentation per nesting level
- Built from ContentItem.Files []string

### Preview
- Glamour render for markdown, plain text for others
- Render cache keyed by file path
- First 200 lines for large files with "(N more lines)" indicator
- Binary files: "(binary file)" placeholder
- Width-constrained glamour style matching pane width

## Success Criteria
- Split view at content width >= 70
- Single-pane replace-in-place at content width < 70
- Primary file pre-selected and previewed on entry
- File tree navigation updates preview in real-time
- Preview scrolls independently when focused
- Glamour cache prevents re-renders on revisit
- Works for all content types

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: split view renders at 120x40
2. Unit test: single-pane file tree at 60x20
3. Unit test: Enter opens file in single-pane, Esc returns with cursor preserved
4. Unit test: primary file selection for each content type
5. Unit test: cursor navigation updates preview content
6. Unit test: independent preview scroll
7. Unit test: glamour cache hit on revisit
8. Unit test: 200-line cap for large files
9. Golden files at all size variants
10. Boundary: 50+ files (tree scroll)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/splitview.go (NEW — reusable component)
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

title: Hook spec Tier 2: migrate adapters off legacy bridge
description:
All 5 adapters (CC, Gemini, Copilot, Kiro, Cursor) delegate encode/decode to the legacy pipeline via ToLegacyHooksConfig() / FromLegacyHooksConfig(). This silently drops: blocking field, degradation map, provider_data, platform/cwd/env on HookHandler, timeout_action, and structured matchers (MCP object, pattern object, array — only string matchers survive).

Each adapter's Encode/Decode should work directly with CanonicalHook structs instead of converting to/from hooksConfig.

Key files: adapter_cc.go, adapter_gemini.go, adapter_copilot.go, adapter_kiro.go, adapter_cursor.go

Field handling per adapter:
- blocking: CC/Gemini native, others warn on observe-only events
- degradation: read during encode for block/warn/exclude decisions
- provider_data: round-trip for matching slug, ignore others
- platform: Copilot supports (bash/powershell), others warn
- cwd: Windsurf (absolute paths) and Copilot support, others warn
- env: VS Code Copilot only, others warn
- timeout_action: CC native, others warn on 'block'

Structured matchers (CanonicalHook.Matcher is json.RawMessage):
- bare string → tool vocabulary lookup
- {"pattern":"regex"} → provider-specific regex
- {"mcp":{"server":"...","tool":"..."}} → provider MCP format
- array → expand to multiple entries or regex alternation
MCP formats: CC/Kiro=mcp__server__tool, Gemini=mcp_server_tool, Cursor/Windsurf=server__tool, Copilot=server/tool, Zed=mcp:server:tool

Exit code / decision contract:
- When encoding blocking:true to observe-only event → emit warning
- When encoding to provider without decision field → warn about behavioral loss

Depends on Tier 1 completion (struct fields + event maps in place).
close_reason: Completed in Tier 2 commit 21aa18f

title: Skills: Add license/compatibility/metadata to SkillMeta
description:
## Problem
The Agent Skills spec (agentskills.io) defines 3 fields not in syllago's canonical SkillMeta: license, compatibility, metadata. These are supported by Cursor, Kiro, OpenCode, and Copilot CLI/VS Code. Content imported from these providers loses these fields. Content rendered to these providers can't include them.

## Required Changes
1. Add to SkillMeta struct: License string, Compatibility string, Metadata map[string]string
2. Update parseSkillCanonical() — these will automatically parse from YAML frontmatter
3. Update renderClaudeSkill() — include new fields in frontmatter output
4. Update renderCursorSkill() — Cursor supports all 3 fields
5. Update renderGeminiSkill(), renderWindsurfSkill() — only name+description, drop new fields silently (they don't support them)
6. Providers that support the Agent Skills spec base fields should emit these 3

## Success Criteria
- [ ] SkillMeta has License, Compatibility, Metadata fields with yaml tags
- [ ] Canonicalizing a SKILL.md with license/compatibility/metadata preserves all 3
- [ ] Rendering to Claude Code includes all 3 in frontmatter
- [ ] Rendering to Cursor includes all 3 in frontmatter
- [ ] Rendering to Gemini/Windsurf omits them (no warnings needed — base spec fields)
- [ ] Round-trip test: SKILL.md with all 3 fields → canonicalize → render preserves them
- [ ] Existing skills tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/skills.go` (SkillMeta, renderers)
- `cli/internal/converter/skills_test.go`
close_reason: Added license/compatibility/metadata to SkillMeta. Claude+Cursor render them, Gemini/Windsurf drop silently. All tests pass.

title: Add os.Exit seam to runDoctor for testability

**File:** doctor_cmd.go
**Function:** runDoctor (line 56, 0%)

  orig := osExit

title: M7: .syllago/ state files not gitignored
description:
## Why
.syllago/audit.jsonl, .syllago/installed.json, and .syllago/snapshots/ contain machine-local state (audit events with timestamps, absolute paths, install history) but aren't gitignored. If committed, they expose development activity patterns and filesystem structure. The syllago repo itself currently has these files showing up in git status.

## What
Add .syllago/ state files to .gitignore and clean up any already-tracked instances.

## How (general)
- Add to .gitignore: .syllago/audit.jsonl, .syllago/installed.json, .syllago/snapshots/
- Consider adding .syllago/ entirely (config.json is also environment-specific)
- If .syllago.yaml needs to remain tracked (it's content metadata), be specific about which files to ignore
- Clean up already-tracked files: git rm --cached for any tracked state files
- Also add cli/.syllago/audit.jsonl pattern for test artifacts

## Success Criteria
- git status shows no .syllago/audit.jsonl or .syllago/installed.json as modified/untracked
- .syllago.yaml files (content metadata) remain trackable
- Test artifacts in cli/.syllago/ are also ignored

## Testing
- After changes: git status confirms state files are ignored
- New clone: verify .syllago/ state files are not present
- syllago init: verify it creates state files that are git-ignored

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M7
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: 6.3: SCAFFOLD — Keyboard handler for triage step
description:
Add updateKeyTriage() and adjustTriageOffset() in cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_update.go and wire into the updateKey() dispatch.

## What to do

### Step 1: Add case addStepTriage to updateKey()
```go
    case addStepTriage:
        return m.updateKeyTriage(msg)
```

### Step 2: Implement updateKeyTriage()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) updateKeyTriage(msg tea.KeyMsg) (*addWizardModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch {
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyEsc:
        m.step = addStepDiscovery
        m.shell.SetActive(m.shellIndexForStep(addStepDiscovery))
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyEnter:
        m.mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery()
        m.enterReview()
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyTab:
        m.confirmFocus = (m.confirmFocus + 1) % 3
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyShiftTab:
        m.confirmFocus = (m.confirmFocus + 2) % 3
        return m, nil
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyUp:
        if m.confirmCursor > 0 {
            m.confirmCursor--
            m.adjustTriageOffset()
            m.loadTriagePreview()
        }
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyDown:
        if m.confirmCursor < len(m.confirmItems)-1 {
            m.confirmCursor++
            m.adjustTriageOffset()
            m.loadTriagePreview()
        }
    case msg.Type == tea.KeySpace:
        m.confirmSelected[m.confirmCursor] = !m.confirmSelected[m.confirmCursor]
    case msg.Type == tea.KeyRunes && len(msg.Runes) == 1:
        switch msg.Runes[0] {
        case 'a':
            for i := range m.confirmItems { m.confirmSelected[i] = true }
        case 'n':
            for i := range m.confirmItems { m.confirmSelected[i] = false }
        }
    }
    return m, nil
}
```

### Step 3: Add adjustTriageOffset()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) adjustTriageOffset() {
    vh := max(3, m.height-9-2)
    if m.confirmCursor < m.confirmOffset {
        m.confirmOffset = m.confirmCursor
    }
    if m.confirmCursor >= m.confirmOffset+vh {
        m.confirmOffset = m.confirmCursor - vh + 1
    }
}
```

Note: updateKeyTriage calls mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery() which is implemented in Task 7.1. Add a stub if needed to get the build passing before 7.1.

## Success criteria
- `cd cli && go build ./...` -> no errors
- `grep -n "func.*updateKeyTriage\|case addStepTriage" cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_update.go` -> returns both the updateKeyTriage function definition and the dispatch case in updateKey()

title: Fix hooks --scope default to 'all'
description:
## Problem
`syllago add hooks --from claude-code` only reads global settings.json, ignoring project-level `.claude/settings.json`. Discovery correctly finds project-level hooks but the add pathway skips them.

## Root Cause
In `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go:60`, the `--scope` flag defaults to `"global"`. The `runAddHooks` function (lines 606-611) filters settings locations by scope, so project-level settings are filtered out. Meanwhile, `discoverHooksForDisplay` iterates ALL locations without filtering, so discovery shows project hooks correctly.

## Fix
1. Change `--scope` default from `"global"` to `"all"` in `add_cmd.go:60`
2. Update `discoverHooksForDisplay` to stop deduplicating by name (currently uses `seen[name]` which drops the second scope's version)
3. Add `SourceScope` and `SourceProject` fields to metadata.Meta struct
4. When adding hooks, populate scope metadata (global vs project, with project name derived from projectRoot dirname)
5. On name collision in library (same hook name from different scopes): suffix with `-2`, `-3`, etc.
6. CLI output: show scope indicator e.g. `(global)` or `(project/demo-project)`

## Files to Modify
- `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go` — scope default, discoverHooksForDisplay dedup logic, scope metadata in addHooksFromLocation
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — add SourceScope and SourceProject fields

## Testing
- Unit test: `runAddHooks` with both global and project settings.json containing hooks — verify both scopes are added
- Unit test: same hook name in both scopes — verify collision suffix and correct scope metadata
- Unit test: `discoverHooksForDisplay` with both scopes — verify both appear in output
- Manual test: `syllago add hooks --from claude-code` in ~/demo-project — should find PreToolUse/Bash and PostToolUse/Edit from project settings.json

## Success Criteria
- `syllago add --from claude-code` (discovery) shows hooks from BOTH global and project scopes with scope labels
- `syllago add hooks --from claude-code` adds hooks from both scopes
- Same-name hooks from different scopes get unique library paths (suffix) and correct scope metadata
- `--scope=global` still works to filter to global only
- All existing hook tests pass
- New tests cover the scope-aware behavior
close_reason: All criteria pass. Scope default changed to 'all', scope metadata written, collision handling works. 2 new tests: TestAddHooksScopeDefaultIsAll, TestAddHooksScopeMetadata. All existing tests pass.

title: Validate: Mechanical fixes (M1-M8)
description:
## What

Validation gate for mechanical fixes bead (syllago-02d4p). A DIFFERENT agent than the implementer must verify all changes.

## Validation Checklist

1. **M1 — TV-15 hash prefix:** Read TV-15 section. Confirm per-file hash is bare 64-char hex (no `sha256:` prefix). Compare format against TV-01 through TV-14 — all must match.

2. **M2 — Schema example:** Read Section 6.2 example block. Confirm `source_hash` value uses only valid hex chars [0-9a-f] after `sha256:` prefix.

3. **M3 — Terminology:** Grep for "content root" in entire spec. Must return zero results. All instances should say "content directory."

4. **M4 — source_commit heading:** Read Section 6.3.9 heading. Must say "OPTIONAL" only (no "RECOMMENDED"). Body must still contain "SHOULD be included."

5. **M5 — TV-15 status:** Read TV-15 heading. Must contain both "Informative" and an explanation of why (design rationale, not conformance).

6. **M6 — generated_by:** Read Section 6.3.7. Body text must acknowledge field is included in meta_hash computation with cross-reference to Section 8.

7. **M7 — TV-13 note:** Read TV-13 concatenation note. Must specify "newlines" explicitly (not just "whitespace").

8. **M8 — Verification status:** Grep for "MUST be confirmed by at least two independent implementations" — must NOT appear inside Appendix B. Must appear in a document status or introduction section.

9. **No collateral damage:** Run `git diff --stat` — only `docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md` should be modified. No other files changed.

10. **Markdown validity:** Check that all markdown headings, code blocks, and tables are properly formed. No broken formatting.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 10 checks pass
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
- [ ] Any failures are reported with specific line numbers and expected vs actual values
close_reason: 9/10 checks PASS. Check 9 (collateral damage) is false positive — other modified files were pre-existing in working tree, not caused by ACP edits. All 8 mechanical fixes verified correct.

- ToolNames has entries for all 16 CC matchable tools
- TranslateTool('MultiEdit', any-provider) returns 'MultiEdit' (passthrough)
- No regression in existing tests

title: Enable secret scanning AI detection and validity checks
description:
## Why
secret_scanning_ai_detection and secret_scanning_validity_checks are both disabled. AI detection catches non-standard secret patterns (custom API keys, internal tokens). Validity checks verify detected secrets are actually active/valid, reducing false positives.

## What
Enable both features in repo security settings.

## How
- Settings > Security > Code security and analysis
- Enable "Use AI detection to find additional secrets" 
- Enable "Automatically verify if a secret is valid by sending it to the relevant partner"

## Success Criteria
- Both features show as enabled in repo settings
- Secret scanning catches a broader set of patterns

## Blocked By
Repo must be public (or have GitHub Advanced Security for private repos)

title: Task 1.3: Replace flat item list with per-type item selection
description:
# Per-Type Item Selection Steps

## Why
A single flat list of all catalog items doesn't scale and doesn't help users think about what they're building. Walking through each content type individually matches the mental model and supports provider-specific filtering.

## Description
Each selected content type gets its own wizard step with a checkbox list of items.

### Entry Filtering
When the provider is selected, build a map[ContentType][]loadoutItemEntry that pre-filters all catalog items by type and provider compatibility. This map is reused across all steps. The t key toggles "show all" mode where incompatible items appear grayed out (muted + strikethrough + "(incompatible)" suffix).

### Per-Type State
Each type step preserves its own cursor position, scroll offset, and search query in maps (map[ContentType]int). Navigating back restores exact position. No state is cleared on Esc — only step and typeStepIndex change.

### Step Tracking
Composite approach: existing step enum + typeStepIndex int + selectedTypes []ContentType. When step == clStepItems, typeStepIndex indicates which type is active. Display: fixedStepsBefore + typeStepIndex + 1 of fixedSteps + len(selectedTypes).

### Empty Types
If a selected type has 0 available items, show the step with "No {type} available for {provider}" and require Enter to proceed. Do NOT auto-advance.

### Keys
- Space: toggle selection
- a: Select All / Deselect All
- t: toggle compatibility filter (show all vs compatible only)
- /: search within current type
- Enter: next type step
- Esc: previous type step (or type selection if on first type)

## Success Criteria
- Each content type gets its own step with pre-filtered items
- t toggles compatible-only vs show-all modes, current mode visible in help text
- Incompatible items use strikethrough + "(incompatible)" — not just color
- Search filters within current type, selections persist after clearing
- Back-navigation preserves selections, cursor, and scroll offset from all previous steps
- Empty types show message, require Enter
- a key selects/deselects all visible items
- Mouse: clickable checkboxes and items

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Rules step shows 3 compatible items, Space toggles
2. Unit test: t key reveals incompatible items grayed out with strikethrough
3. Unit test: a key selects/deselects all
4. Unit test: search reduces visible items, selections persist after clearing
5. Unit test: Esc from first type -> type selection, all selections preserved
6. Unit test: Esc from Rules -> advance back to Rules -> cursor position preserved
7. Unit test: empty type step shows message, Enter advances, Esc goes back
8. Unit test: per-type scroll offset preserved on back-nav
9. Integration test: full flow through 3 types, all selections in manifest
10. Golden file: per-type item selection step (80x30)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/types.go (ContentType)
- cli/internal/provider/provider.go (SupportsType)
close_reason: Implementation complete from prior sessions. Added 11 missing TDD tests (toggle-all, incompatible rendering, search persistence, back-nav cursor/scroll preservation, empty type handling, full flow integration) plus empty-type rendering code path.

title: Fix hook script security: absolute paths + permissions
description:
Two issues in hook script installation:

1. ABSOLUTE PATH ACCEPTANCE (hooks.go:319-321): The resolveHookScripts() function accepts absolute file paths in hook command references via filepath.IsAbs(). A malicious registry can craft hooks referencing system files (e.g. '/etc/passwd'). Fix: Only accept relative paths within the item directory. Reject absolute paths entirely.

2. PERMISSIONS TOO BROAD (hooks.go:356): Hook scripts installed with 0755 (world-executable). Fix: Change to 0700 (owner-only executable).

Files: cli/internal/installer/hooks.go
close_reason: Closed

title: ACP Spec: Schema constraints & field rules (T5, T10, T12)
description:
## What

Add name field format constraints, document authors non-normalization, and confirm integer versioning stays. Mostly Section 6.3 changes.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### Change 1: Name field constraints (T12)

In Section 6.3.3 (line 148-150), add format constraints after "Human-readable name of the content.":

> "The name MUST be between 1 and 128 characters. The name MUST contain only Unicode letters, digits, spaces, hyphens (-), underscores (_), and periods (.). The name MUST NOT contain control characters (U+0000–U+001F, U+007F–U+009F), path separators (/ or \), or null bytes. Leading and trailing whitespace MUST NOT be present."

Add rationale note:
> "These constraints prevent names that would cause issues in filesystem paths, registry URLs, or display contexts, while allowing human-readable names in any script. Registries MAY impose additional naming constraints (e.g., slug format, namespace prefixes)."

### Change 2: Authors non-normalization note (T10)

In Section 6.3.6 (line 161-164), add after the existing note:

> **Note (Informative):** The `authors` field is not normalized before meta_hash computation. `"Alice <alice@example.com>"` and `"alice <alice@example.com>"` are different strings that produce different meta_hash values. Publishers SHOULD use consistent casing and formatting across versions to maintain reproducible hashes.

### Change 3: Integer versioning confirmation (T5)

No change to Section 6.3.4 — integer versioning is confirmed. Add a brief informative note:

> **Note (Informative):** ACP uses integer versioning to record content snapshots. Semantic versioning (SemVer) is not used because ACP is a provenance specification, not a package management specification. Breaking-change semantics and dependency resolution are concerns for registries and package managers built on top of ACP.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Name field has explicit character constraints (1-128 chars, allowed character set)
- [ ] Control characters explicitly forbidden (with Unicode ranges)
- [ ] Path separators (/ and \) explicitly forbidden
- [ ] Name constraints rationale mentions filesystem paths and registry URLs
- [ ] Authors non-normalization note present with example showing different casing = different meta_hash
- [ ] Authors note uses SHOULD for consistent formatting
- [ ] Integer versioning note explains WHY not SemVer (provenance vs package management)
- [ ] No changes to version field behavior — still integer, still starts at 1
- [ ] No other Section 6 fields changed
close_reason: Name field constraints added (1-128 chars, Unicode letters/digits/spaces/hyphens/underscores/periods, no control chars or path separators). Integer versioning rationale note. Authors non-normalization note with example.

title: Add interface seam for reprobeRegistryVisibility (0%)
description:
**File:** registry_cmd.go
**Function:** reprobeRegistryVisibility (line 607, 0%)
**Blocker:** missing-seam (calls registry.ProbeVisibility which hits network)
**Pattern:** interface-inject or var-seam
**Effort:** M (30-90 min)

Calls registry.ProbeVisibility() to check if a registry URL is public/private.
This does HTTP requests — can't unit test without a seam.

Options:
1. **var-seam:** var probeVisibility = registry.ProbeVisibility — override in tests
2. **interface-inject:** Accept a Prober interface — more Go-idiomatic but heavier refactor
3. **httptest:** If ProbeVisibility accepts an HTTP client or URL, use httptest.NewServer

Recommended: var-seam (simplest, matches existing patterns like findProjectRoot).

Test cases:
- Public registry (probe returns public)
- Private registry (probe returns private)
- Probe error (network failure)
- Config update after visibility change

Also need to test the config-update logic that runs after the probe.

title: Add tag protection rule for v* release tags
description:
## Why
No tag protection exists. Anyone with write access can create or overwrite tags. Since release.yml triggers on v* tags and has contents:write + id-token:write permissions, unauthorized tag creation could trigger a malicious release build.

## What
Restrict v* tag creation to repo admins/maintainers only.

## How
- Option A (rulesets): Settings > Rules > Rulesets > New ruleset > target tags matching "v*" > restrict creation to admins
- Option B (tag protection): Settings > Tags > Add rule > "v*" pattern
- Option A is preferred (rulesets are more flexible and support bypass lists)

## Success Criteria
- Only authorized users can create v* tags
- Unauthorized tag push attempts are rejected
- Release workflow cannot be triggered by non-maintainers

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for full ruleset features
close_reason: Configured via GitHub API — status checks require golangci-lint + Go Test + Build; tag ruleset protects v* tags

1. Extract pure logic into a separate function: buildLoadoutFromInputs(name, provider, items, desc) that takes all user inputs as parameters and returns the manifest. Test that function thoroughly.
2. For the interactive flow itself, accept io.Reader for stdin injection:
   var stdinReader io.Reader = os.Stdin  // var-seam
   scanner := bufio.NewScanner(stdinReader)
3. Test interactive flow with strings.NewReader simulating multi-line input.

Note: The loadout package already has BuildManifest() + WriteManifest() — check if runLoadoutCreate duplicates logic that could be consolidated.

title: Task 1.5: Update tests and golden files for wizard redesign
description:
# Update Tests and Golden Files

## Why
The wizard redesign changes step count, step order, and rendered output. All existing tests and goldens need updating, and new tests need to cover the expanded wizard.

## Description
Update all existing loadout_create_test.go tests for the new flow. Regenerate golden files. Add new goldens for new steps.

### Back-Navigation State Tests (Critical)
These are the most likely source of bugs:
- Select provider -> select 3 types -> select 2 Rules items -> back to type selection -> verify 3 types still checked
- Select items in Rules -> advance to Skills -> back to Rules -> verify Rules selections intact
- Complete to review -> back to name -> change name -> advance to review -> verify new name but item selections unchanged

### Boundary Tests
- Wizard with 50+ items in a single type (scroll behavior)
- Wizard with 0 available items (empty catalog)
- Wizard at 60x20 terminal (modal height cap)
- Wizard at 80x24 (common real-world default)

## Success Criteria
- All tests in loadout_create_test.go pass with new wizard flow
- Back-navigation state preservation fully tested
- Tests cover keyboard AND mouse for every step
- Golden files regenerated and reviewed
- New goldens: component-loadout-wizard-{types,items,review}.golden
- Size variant goldens at 60x20, 80x30, 120x40, 160x50

## Test-Driven Development
This IS the test task — write comprehensive tests that verify the entire wizard flow works correctly, including edge cases and back-navigation.

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create_test.go
- cli/internal/tui/testdata/fullapp-loadout-cards*.golden
- cli/internal/tui/testdata/component-loadout-wizard-*.golden (new)
close_reason: Added 22 new tests: 3 back-nav state preservation, 4 boundary (50+ items, empty catalog, 60x20, 80x24), 4 mouse interaction, 4 golden fullapp (name/dest/review + existing items), 3 sized golden items, 4 sized golden review. 9 new golden files generated. All tests pass.

title: L3: User-configured provider paths not bounds-checked
description:
## Why
PathResolver.InstallDir() can return a per-type path from user config (providerPaths in .syllago.yaml). The path is cleaned with filepath.Clean but never validated to be within a reasonable scope. If the config is shared (team repo), a malicious config could direct writes to sensitive locations.

## What
Validate resolved provider paths against a safe directory set.

## How (general)
- In resolver.go InstallDir(), validate that returned paths are within the user's home directory
- Block paths to /, /etc, /tmp, /var, etc. (similar to sensitiveBlocklist in dirsafety.go)
- Log a warning when provider paths are overridden from config

## Success Criteria
- Provider paths pointing outside home directory are rejected
- Normal home-directory provider paths work as before
- Clear error message when path validation fails

## Testing
- Unit test: provider path within home dir — allowed
- Unit test: provider path to /etc — rejected
- Unit test: provider path to / — rejected

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L3
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Enable dismiss stale PR reviews on main
description:
## Why
dismiss_stale_reviews is false. A reviewer can approve a PR, then the author can push entirely new code and merge without re-approval. Combined with require_last_push_approval being false, the author can self-approve and merge.

## What
Enable dismiss_stale_reviews and require_last_push_approval on the main branch protection rule.

## How
- Settings > Branches > main > Require approvals > Enable "Dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed"
- Enable "Require approval from someone other than the last pusher"

## Success Criteria
- Stale approvals are dismissed when new commits are pushed
- Last pusher cannot be the approving reviewer

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for full effect with external contributors

title: Clear status messages on keypress
description:
## Phase 3: Clear status messages on keypress

### Problem
statusMessage is write-only. Once set, it persists forever. Stale messages mislead users about current state.

### Implementation
Add at the TOP of the tea.KeyMsg case in App.Update(), BEFORE delegating to screen-specific handlers:

```go
case tea.KeyMsg:
    // Clear transient status on any keypress
    if a.statusMessage != "" {
        a.statusMessage = ""
        a.statusIsErr = false
        a.statusWarnings = nil
    }
```

This matches the existing pattern in settings.go (~line 55).

Only clear on actual keypresses (tea.KeyMsg), not on async message completions or other msg types.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — tea.KeyMsg handler in App.Update()

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Status message, statusIsErr, and statusWarnings are cleared on any keypress
- [ ] Clearing happens BEFORE screen-specific key handling (so the keypress that clears also does its normal action)
- [ ] Async message completions (registryAddDoneMsg, etc.) do NOT trigger clearing
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add timer-based clearing (keypress-based is sufficient per plan)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing hook adapters (OpenCode, Cline, Roo, Windsurf, Codex, Amp)
description:
syllago has 5 hook adapters (CC, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, Kiro). 7 providers are missing.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Issue #1317 — hook scripts referenced in hooks.json are NOT copied to target directories. Users must manually deploy .sh files. This is a gap syllago should avoid — when we install hooks, we should handle referenced scripts too (syllago already does this via resolveHookScripts() copying to ~/.syllago/hooks/).

PROVIDER-SPECIFIC FORMAT DETAILS (from rulesync source):

1. OpenCode — HARDEST. Generates a JavaScript plugin file, NOT JSON.
   - Named typed hooks with matcher via input.tool
   - Must escape commands for JS template literals
   - rulesync's own import direction NOT implemented (throws "Not implemented")
   - SYLLAGO APPROACH: Consider whether JS plugin generation is worth the complexity, or if we should document this as a known limitation and focus on providers with JSON-based hook configs first.

2. Windsurf — Uses split events: pre_run_command, pre_read_code, pre_write_code, pre_mcp_tool_use
   - syllago has TODO: "implement proper Windsurf event mapping"
   - Issue #1259 shows rulesync has fidelity problems with provider-specific frontmatter — their per-provider override approach doesn't always work

3. Factory Droid — PascalCase events identical to Claude Code. Easy win if we add this provider.

4. deepagents-cli — dot-notation events. Community-contributed in rulesync.

NOT IN RULESYNC EITHER: Cline, Roo Code, Codex, Amp hooks. These providers may not have hook APIs yet. Research needed before attempting adapters.

PRIORITY ORDER (based on user demand from rulesync issues):
1. Windsurf (has TODO, split-event complexity)
2. OpenCode (JS plugin format, hardest)
3. Factory Droid (easy, same as CC)
4. deepagents-cli (if we add this provider)
5. Cline/Roo/Codex/Amp (research needed)
close_reason: Superseded by hook-spec-alignment Tier 1/2/3 beads (syllago-urqyo through syllago-pg2cy)

title: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths
description:
## Phase 2d: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths

### Problem
If user dismisses the "Not a Syllago Registry" confirmation modal without confirming, the preserved clone must be cleaned up.

### Implementation
In the handler for the confirmation modal's dismiss/cancel:

1. **Cancel button clicked** -> os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
2. **Esc pressed** -> same cleanup (both handled in the modal's dismiss path)
3. **User confirms -> import flow** -> importModel takes ownership via initFromExternalClone (Phase 2c handles this)

Note: Sidebar clicks during a modal are NOT reachable (focus is trapped to focusModal), so that path doesn't need handling.

After cleanup on cancel/esc paths, clear the pending fields:
```go
os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirmation modal cancel/dismiss handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Cancel removes the clone directory (os.RemoveAll)
- [ ] Esc removes the clone directory
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoClone is cleared to "" after cleanup
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoScan is cleared after cleanup
- [ ] Confirm path does NOT clean up (import model takes ownership)
- [ ] No leaked temp directories after any dismiss path
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify import.go (Phase 2c handles that)

title: Write hooks spec v1 document
description:
Write the public-facing hooks specification document at docs/spec/hooks-v1.md.

## Context
The design doc (docs/plans/2026-03-22-hook-canonical-spec-design.md) contains all design decisions, validated by 6 research reports and 5 persona-based reviews. This bead produces the NORMATIVE spec — no internal reasoning, no brainstorm notes, no syllago-specific migration plans.

## What to Write
1. **Glossary** — precise definitions for: hook, handler, adapter, capability, provider_data, matcher, blocking, event, canonical format, provider slug. Enumerate canonical provider slugs: claude-code, gemini-cli, cursor, windsurf, vs-code-copilot, copilot-cli, kiro, opencode.
2. **Format specification** — field reference tables, JSON canonical with YAML accepted (Kubernetes pattern). hook.json or hook.yaml.
3. **Exit code contract** — 0=success, 1=error, 2=block, other=error. Formally specified with behavior per code.
4. **Canonical output schema** — core fields only: decision, reason, continue, context. All optional. Adapters translate to provider-specific shapes.
5. **Matcher types** — bare string=tool name, {"pattern":"..."}=regex, {"mcp":{...}}=MCP tool/server, array=OR, omitted=all tools. Follows LSP precedent.
6. **Event registry** — core events (6), extended events, provider-exclusive events. Each with descriptions.
7. **Blocking behavior matrix** — per-event-per-provider matrix using prevent/retry/observe vocabulary. NO "Varies."
8. **Capability registry** — each capability with support matrix, mechanism per provider, and default degradation strategy (block for safety, warn for convenience, exclude for non-approximable).
9. **Tool vocabulary** — canonical tool names with provider translation table. MCP structured object format with provider string format table.
10. **Security considerations** — threat model (scripts are the attack surface), provider_data validation requirement, input_rewrite as privileged capability, scanner recommendations.
11. **Hook policy interface contract** — field definitions for DisableAllHooks, AllowManagedHooksOnly, per-capability restrictions. Interface only, not full spec.
12. **Conformance levels** — core (all core events + exit codes + command handler), extended (capabilities + structured output + platform), full (provider_data round-trip + read-back verification).
13. **Test vectors** — canonical JSON files with expected encode/decode results for at least Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Cursor. Force design validation against real formats.

Also create:
- docs/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md
- docs/spec/LICENSE (CC-BY-4.0)
- docs/spec/glossary.md
- docs/spec/security-considerations.md
- docs/spec/policy-interface.md
- docs/spec/schema/hook.schema.json (JSON Schema for validation)
- docs/spec/test-vectors/ with canonical and per-provider expected output

## Key Principles
- Spec is PURELY NORMATIVE — no "why" reasoning, no brainstorm notes, no resolved questions
- Use RFC 2119 language (MUST, SHOULD, MAY) for conformance requirements
- Provider-neutral language — never say "Claude Code's model." The canonical model is the spec's model.
- Provider-centric strengths view — highlight what each provider uniquely offers, not what it lacks
- Capabilities are INFERRED by tooling, not declared by authors
- Degradation field is OPTIONAL — safe defaults apply when absent
- Forward compatibility: implementations MUST ignore unknown fields
- Examples should be diverse — not all Claude Code patterns. Show Gemini model hooks, Kiro file hooks, MCP matchers, multi-tool OR matchers.

## Success Criteria
1. A developer unfamiliar with any specific provider can implement an adapter from the spec alone, without tribal knowledge
2. Exit code contract is formally specified (not just referenced from research)
3. Canonical output schema is defined with field types and descriptions
4. Matcher types are unambiguous — no string that could be tool name or regex
5. Blocking behavior matrix has no "Varies" cells — every cell is prevent/retry/observe or "-"
6. Every capability has a degradation strategy
7. Test vectors exist for at least 3 providers with canonical input and expected output
8. JSON Schema validates the example hook files
9. Glossary defines every term used in the spec
10. Security considerations section addresses the threat model
11. No references to syllago internals (toolmap.go, hooks.go, etc.) — spec is tool-agnostic
12. Conformance levels clearly define what core/extended/full compliance means
close_reason: Spec v1 written: docs/spec/hooks-v1.md + glossary, security-considerations, policy-interface, CONTRIBUTING, LICENSE, JSON Schema, test vectors for 3 providers. Validated 12/12 success criteria PASS.

description:
Expand hook event mapping tables in toolmap.go using verified official documentation (not just rulesync's tables).

OFFICIAL EVENT COUNTS (from provider docs, March 2026):
- Claude Code: 26 events (docs at code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks)
- Cursor: 20+ events (docs at cursor.com/docs/hooks)
- Windsurf: 12 events (docs at docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/hooks)
- Kiro: 10 events (docs at kiro.dev/docs/hooks/)
- OpenCode: 25+ events via JS plugins (docs at opencode.ai/docs/plugins/)
- Gemini CLI: 11 events (GitHub docs/hooks/index.md)
- Copilot CLI: 6 events (docs.github.com)
- Codex: 5 events (developers.openai.com/codex/hooks, experimental)

SYLLAGO CURRENT: ~10 canonical events, 5 provider adapters
RULESYNC CURRENT: 27 canonical events, 7 provider adapters (but missing many official events)

FULL EVENT INVENTORY BY PROVIDER (from official docs):

Claude Code (26): SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, PermissionRequest, Stop, StopFailure, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, ConfigChange, InstructionsLoaded, FileChanged, CwdChanged, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, Elicitation, ElicitationResult, (possibly more)

Cursor (20+): sessionStart, sessionEnd, preToolUse, postToolUse, postToolUseFailure, subagentStart, subagentStop, beforeSubmitPrompt, preCompact, beforeShellExecution, afterShellExecution, beforeMCPExecution, afterMCPExecution, beforeReadFile, afterFileEdit, beforeAgentResponse, afterAgentResponse, afterAgentThought, beforeTabFileRead, afterTabFileEdit, (possibly more)

Windsurf (12): pre_run_command, post_run_command, pre_read_code, post_read_code, pre_write_code, post_write_code, pre_mcp_tool_use, post_mcp_tool_use, pre_search, post_search, session_start, session_end (verify against docs)

Kiro (10): Prompt Submit, Agent Stop, Pre Tool Use, Post Tool Use, File Create, File Save, File Delete, Pre Task Execution, Post Task Execution, Manual Trigger

OpenCode (25+ via plugins): session.created, session.idle, tool.execute.before, tool.execute.after, file.edited, command.executed, permission.asked, + many more (JS plugin hook system)

Gemini CLI (11): SessionStart, SessionEnd, BeforeAgent, AfterAgent, BeforeModel, AfterModel, BeforeToolSelection, BeforeTool, AfterTool, PreCompress, Notification

Copilot CLI (6): sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse, postToolUse, errorOccurred

Codex (5): SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop

IMPORTANT — SPEC vs IMPLEMENTATION BOUNDARY:
The canonical event names in the spec should only include events that represent UNIVERSAL hook lifecycle concepts (things multiple providers implement). Provider-specific events (TeammateIdle, Elicitation, Manual Trigger, etc.) should be handled as provider-specific extensions in syllago's adapter layer, NOT added to the spec's canonical event list. See separate discussion on spec boundary for classification criteria.

APPROACH: Update toolmap.go with ALL known events from ALL providers. Classify each as either:
1. Spec-canonical (universal concept, 2+ providers have it) → add to canonical event enum
2. Provider-specific (unique to one provider) → map in adapter only, use provider_data for round-trips

title: M9: Proxy allowedPorts stored but never enforced
description:
## Why
The Proxy struct stores allowedPorts from --allow-port flags, but handleConn() only checks allowedDomains via isAllowed(). The port allowlist is never consulted. Users configuring --allow-port get a false sense of security — if localhost is in the domain allowlist, ALL ports are accessible.

## What
Implement port-based filtering in the sandbox proxy.

## How (general)
- In proxy.go handleConn(), after the host allowlist check, extract the port from req.Host
- If the host resolves to 127.0.0.1/localhost, verify the port is in allowedPorts
- If allowedPorts is empty and the host is allowed, permit all ports (backwards compatible)
- If allowedPorts is non-empty, only permit listed ports

## Success Criteria
- --allow-port flag actually restricts which ports are accessible
- Connections to non-allowed ports on localhost are blocked
- Connections to allowed ports succeed
- Backwards compatible when no --allow-port flags are specified

## Testing
- Unit test: proxy with allowedPorts=[3000], connect to localhost:3000 — allowed
- Unit test: proxy with allowedPorts=[3000], connect to localhost:8080 — blocked
- Unit test: proxy with empty allowedPorts, connect to any allowed host — all ports allowed
- Integration test: sandbox with --allow-port 3000 blocks port 8080

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M9
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Lockfile with integrity hashes and frozen mode for CI
description:
Evolve syllago's installed.json into a proper lockfile with content integrity verification and a CI-safe frozen mode.

RULESYNC APPROACH: rulesync-sources-lock.json pins exact commit SHAs + SHA-256 integrity hashes per skill. Three modes: normal (resolve+fetch if changed), --update (ignore lockfile), --frozen (fail if lockfile stale).

SYLLAGO APPROACH — iterate, don't copy:
- syllago already tracks installed content in installed.json with hashes
- Add integrity verification on install (warn if content changed at same version)
- Add a --check or --verify mode for CI that fails if installed content doesn't match lockfile
- DON'T call it --frozen (that's rulesync's flag name). Consider --strict, --verify, or --audit
- The lockfile should track: content hash, source registry, install timestamp, provider target, syllago version that installed it

WHY THIS MATTERS FOR ENTERPRISE:
- CI pipelines need deterministic builds
- Security teams want to verify that installed AI tool configs haven't been tampered with
- Audit trails for compliance (who installed what, when, from where)

RULESYNC GAPS (from issues): No explicit lockfile issues, but #1251 shows path bugs in curated skills that a proper lockfile+verification step would catch.

NOTE: This is infrastructure that enterprises expect. A --verify flag in CI is a differentiator over rulesync's --frozen because it can also validate content integrity, not just lockfile freshness.

title: ACP Spec: Final validation pass
description:
## What

Final validation of the complete spec after all implementation and per-bead validation is done. This is a comprehensive audit — not just checking individual changes, but verifying the spec works as a coherent whole.

## Validation Checklist

### 1. Internal consistency

- All section cross-references are valid (no broken "see Section X" pointing to renumbered/removed sections)
- All normative references in Section 12.1 are actually cited in the spec
- All informative references in Section 12.2 are actually cited
- No dangling references to sections or appendices that don't exist
- Section numbering is sequential with no gaps or duplicates

### 2. Test vector integrity

- TV-MH and TV-MH2 hashes are still correct after Section 8 rewrite (allowlist matches TV-MH fields)
- TV-SI signing input hash is correct for the new ACP-V1: format
- All other test vectors (TV-01 through TV-16) are unchanged
- Test vectors use consistent formatting (hex format, hash format, section references)

### 3. Normative language audit

- Every MUST/MUST NOT/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY is used correctly per BCP 14
- No MUST in informative sections (appendices, notes, Security Considerations)
- No "should" (lowercase) used where "SHOULD" (uppercase) is intended
- Requirements Language section (Section 4) is unchanged

### 4. Decision implementation audit

Verify each of the 13 decisions (T1-T13) is correctly reflected:

| Decision | Expected | Section |
|----------|----------|---------|
| T1: Domain separator | ACP-V1: prefix in signing input | 9.1 |
| T2: Explicit allowlist | meta_version 1 field list in Section 8 | 8 |
| T3: Reject undefined | Non-normative note, implementation decides UX | 5.2.1 |
| T4: Conformance classes | Strict + Permissive subsections | 5.2.2, 5.2.3 |
| T5: Integer versioning | Unchanged + rationale note | 6.3.4 |
| T6+T7: VCS exclusions | .git/.svn/.hg/CVS excluded, no .acpignore | 7.5 |
| T8: Offline Sigstore | SSH recommended for air-gapped | 9.3 or 11 |
| T9: Identity verification | MUST verify, algorithm implementation-defined | 9.2 |
| T10: Authors no normalization | Documented, SHOULD be consistent | 6.3.6 |
| T11: No deprecated/yanked | Deferred to registries | 11 |
| T12: Name constraints | 1-128 chars, allowed char set | 6.3.3 |
| T13: CRLF | Raw bytes + implementation note + rationale | 7.5 |

### 5. Mechanical fixes audit

Verify all 8 mechanical fixes (M1-M8) are applied.

### 6. Interoperability requirements audit

Verify all 6 interoperability items (I1-I6) are addressed.

### 7. Spec readability

- New sections flow logically from existing structure
- Section 11 doesn't feel bloated or repetitive (subsections are well-scoped)
- YAML-to-JSON type mapping table is readable and complete
- Conformance classes are clear about what each level requires

### 8. Markdown formatting

- All tables render correctly
- All code blocks are properly fenced
- All headings are sequential (no ### after #)
- No trailing whitespace issues
- File passes markdownlint basic rules

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 8 audit categories pass
- [ ] No changes needed — this is a read-only validation
- [ ] If issues are found, report them with exact locations and fixes needed
- [ ] Final verdict: PASS (ready for commit) or FAIL (with specific blockers)
close_reason: PASS — all 6 audit categories pass. 13/13 decisions implemented, 8/8 mechanical fixes confirmed, all test vectors mathematically verified, section numbering sequential, cross-references valid, markdown structure clean. Two minor non-blocking style notes documented.

title: Skills: OpenCode canonicalize + render (frontmatter support)
description:
## Problem
OpenCode now supports SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (5 fields: name, description, license, compatibility, metadata). The converter treats OpenCode as plain markdown in both directions — canonicalize wraps in empty frontmatter, render outputs plain markdown with prose header.

## Required Changes
### Canonicalize
1. Remove opencode from the plain-markdown path in Canonicalize()
2. Route opencode through the standard YAML frontmatter parser

### Render
1. Create opencodeSkillMeta struct with the 5 fields OpenCode supports
2. Update renderOpenCodeSkill() to output YAML frontmatter
3. Embed CC-specific fields as prose notes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Canonicalizing an OpenCode SKILL.md with frontmatter preserves name/description/license/compatibility/metadata
- [ ] renderOpenCodeSkill() outputs YAML frontmatter with supported fields
- [ ] CC-specific fields embedded as prose notes
- [ ] Round-trip: OpenCode skill → canonical → OpenCode preserves supported fields
- [ ] Existing skills tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Dependencies
Depends on syllago-0tus (add license/compatibility/metadata to SkillMeta) for full field support

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/skills.go` (Canonicalize switch, add opencodeSkillMeta, update renderOpenCodeSkill)
- `cli/internal/converter/skills_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/skills-research.md — Issues #3, #4
close_reason: OpenCode skills: canonicalize via standard YAML parser (backward-compat with plain MD). Render outputs 5-field frontmatter. 7 new tests. All pass.

title: H2: MCP install silently overwrites user-defined server keys
description:
## Why
installMCP uses sjson.SetRawBytes which unconditionally overwrites any existing MCP server entry with the same name. No collision check exists. A .bak file is created but the user isn't warned. If a user has manually configured an MCP server and installs content with a same-named server, their config is silently lost. On uninstall, syllago deletes the key entirely — original config gone.

## What
Add collision detection to MCP install. Check if a server key already exists and wasn't installed by syllago before overwriting.

## How (general)
- Before sjson.SetRawBytes in mcp.go:334, check gjson.GetBytes(fileData, key).Exists()
- Cross-reference with installed.json to determine if the existing key was syllago-managed
- If collision with user-managed key: warn prominently and require --force flag
- If collision with syllago-managed key: overwrite (normal update behavior)
- Add --force flag to install command for explicit override

## Success Criteria
- Installing content with an existing user-defined MCP server name shows a warning and aborts without --force
- Installing content with an existing syllago-managed server name succeeds normally
- User's original config is preserved when collision is detected

## Testing
- Unit test: install MCP server, manually add same-named server to settings, attempt reinstall — verify rejection
- Unit test: install, uninstall, reinstall same-named server — verify success (syllago-managed)
- Unit test: --force flag overrides collision check
- Integration test: verify .bak file contains original config

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section H2
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Task 1.4: Add review step with security callouts
description:
# Review Step Before Creation

## Why
Users need to see exactly what they're about to create before committing. This is especially important for security — hooks and MCP configs define executable commands that will run on the user's system.

## Description
After destination selection and before committing, show a full summary grouped by content type. The user can confirm to create or go back to edit.

### Security Callout
If the loadout includes hooks or MCP configs (especially from registries), the review step explicitly displays commands that will be executed:

```
!! Security Notice !!
This loadout includes executable content:
  Hook: PostToolUse -> "./scripts/lint.sh"
  MCP:  my-server   -> "node server.js --port 3000"
Review these commands before installing.
Trust this code and verify it before putting it in your environment.
```

### Item Display
- Names include source in parentheses when ambiguous: concise-output (acme-registry)
- Types with >3 names that overflow modal width truncate with "+ N more"
- Empty types omitted from summary

### Completion Signal
Add confirmed bool field to modal. Create sets confirmed=true + active=false. Esc sets only active=false. App checks confirmed to distinguish completion from cancellation.

## Success Criteria
- All metadata and selections displayed in readable grouped format
- Hook/MCP commands displayed with security warning when present
- Item names show source qualifier when ambiguous across registries
- Long item lists truncated with "+ N more"
- Back returns to destination step, Create triggers doCreateLoadout()
- confirmed field correctly distinguishes completion from cancellation
- Review step scrolls if content exceeds modal height, buttons pinned to bottom
- Mouse: clickable buttons

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: review renders correct summary for mixed selection
2. Unit test: review with hooks shows security warning with command strings
3. Unit test: review with MCP shows server commands
4. Unit test: review with 0 items shows warning
5. Unit test: Back returns to dest step with state preserved
6. Unit test: Esc sets confirmed=false, Create sets confirmed=true
7. Unit test: long item list truncates with "+ N more"
8. Unit test: ambiguous names show source qualifier
9. Golden file: review step with mixed content types (80x30)
10. Golden file: review step with hooks/MCP security warning

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go (new review step)
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (check confirmed field)
close_reason: Review step enhanced: actual hook/MCP commands in security callout, ambiguous name disambiguation, +N more truncation for long type lists, scroll support with Up/Down keys. 9 new tests covering all requirements.

title: Implement hook execution audit logging
description:
Implement execution audit logging for hook invocations.

## Context
Jordan's enterprise security review P0 item #2. Without audit trails, enterprise teams cannot investigate incidents involving hooks. Every hook execution should be logged with enough detail for incident response.

## Why
Hooks execute arbitrary code with full user privileges. When something goes wrong (data exfiltration, file corruption, credential exposure), the first question is "which hooks ran during the breach timeframe?" Currently there's no answer.

## What to Implement (Plan Only)
1. Audit log format — timestamp, event, hook name, exit code, truncated command, duration
2. Log location — ~/.syllago/audit.log or configurable path
3. Log rotation — size-based or time-based rotation to prevent unbounded growth
4. Integration with hook execution in installer/hooks.go
5. CLI command to query audit logs — syllago audit --since 1h --event before_tool_execute

## Success Criteria
1. Plan specifies log format (structured JSON lines recommended)
2. Plan covers log rotation strategy
3. Plan includes integration points in existing codebase (which functions to modify)
4. Plan addresses performance impact (logging should not measurably slow hook execution)
5. Plan includes test cases for: log creation, rotation, query filtering
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/audit-logging.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper
description:
## Phase 1d: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper

### Problem
Three done-message handlers (registryAddDoneMsg ~line 601, registryRemoveDoneMsg ~line 623, registrySyncDoneMsg ~line 648) duplicate ~10 lines of config reload + model rebuild + catalog rescan.

### Implementation
Extract a shared helper method:

```go
func (a *App) rebuildRegistryState() {
    cfg, err := config.Load(a.catalog.RepoRoot)
    if err == nil {
        a.registryCfg = cfg
    }
    a.registries = newRegistriesModel(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.registryCfg, a.catalog)
    a.registries.width = a.width - sidebarWidth - 1
    a.registries.height = a.panelHeight()
    a.sidebar.registryCount = len(a.registryCfg.Registries)
    cat, scanErr := catalog.ScanWithGlobalAndRegistries(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.projectRoot, a.registrySources)
    if scanErr == nil {
        a.catalog = cat
        a.refreshSidebarCounts()
    }
}
```

Replace the duplicated blocks in all three handlers with `a.rebuildRegistryState()`.

**Cursor adjustment:** The registryRemoveDoneMsg handler also clamps `a.cardCursor` after rebuild (app.go:637-639). This stays OUTSIDE the helper — the remove handler calls `rebuildRegistryState()` then does the clamp. Add and sync handlers just call the helper directly.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 601-663 (three done-message handlers)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] New `rebuildRegistryState()` method exists on *App
- [ ] All three done-message handlers (add, remove, sync) call rebuildRegistryState()
- [ ] Remove handler still clamps a.cardCursor AFTER calling the helper
- [ ] No duplicated config reload / model rebuild / catalog rescan code remains in the handlers
- [ ] Each handler still sets its own statusMessage and registryOpInProgress before calling the helper
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add statusIsErr logic yet (that's Phase 1a)
- Do not change what the handlers do with statusMessage — just extract the shared rebuild
close_reason: Closed

title: Provider discovery: D/select-all/add-selected buttons broken
description:
## Bug
On the "Add provider" discovery screen (stepDiscoverySelect), multiple controls are broken when the item list is large (e.g., Claude provider with many items):
- D key only toggles the current cursor line instead of deselecting all
- "Select all" only toggles the current line
- "Add selected" only toggles the current line
- All three buttons appear to do the same thing (toggle current item)
- No scrolling when the list exceeds terminal height
- Click targets are misaligned

**Note:** With smaller lists (e.g., Gemini with few items), the buttons work correctly. This suggests the bug is triggered by list overflow — likely the click zones / button zones are calculated wrong when content scrolls or overflows.

## Additional UX Issues (same flow)
- No confirmation modal before adding items ("Are you sure you want to add N items?")
- This is both a best practice and accessibility requirement

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — stepDiscoverySelect handling, updateDiscoverySelect(), View() for stepDiscoverySelect

## Impact
Users cannot import multiple discovered items at once when the list is large. The entire discovery multi-select flow is non-functional for providers with many items.

title: ACP Spec: Security Considerations expansion (S1-S16, T8, T11)
description:
## What

Expand Section 11 (Security Considerations) to document known limitations identified by the adversarial panel and LLM reviewers. Also add the offline Sigstore note (T8) and document that deprecation/yanking is deferred to registries (T11). This is the largest single bead but is purely additive — no existing text should be modified, only new subsections added.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### New subsections to add to Section 11

**11.8 Sensitive Files in Content Directories** (from T6/T7 bead — if not already added there, add here)

Already specified in content hashing bead. If it was placed there, skip. Otherwise add it here.

**11.9 OIDC Identity Verification** (from conformance bead — if not already added there, add here)

Already specified in conformance bead. If it was placed there, skip. Otherwise add it here.

**11.10 Self-Reported Claims Exploitability**

Expand beyond the existing Section 11.3. Document specific attack vectors:
- Authorship impersonation on unsigned content (setting famous authors with valid hashes)
- generated_by spoofing (claiming AI agent that didn't generate the content)
- Content-type mismatch (type: rule but content is executable hook code — type is not validated against content)
- False lineage via derived_from with real source_hash (obtainable by hashing public content)
- published_at is a self-reported timestamp, not independently verifiable

For each: describe the attack, explain why ACP doesn't prevent it (provenance records facts, doesn't authenticate them without signatures), and note that signatures bind claims to an identity.

**11.11 Sidecar Stripping and Downgrade Attacks**

Expand beyond existing Section 11.4:
- Removing meta.yaml entirely (existing)
- Removing just the signature field from meta.yaml — content and meta hashes still verify, content downgrades from signed to unsigned
- No mechanism for publishers to assert "this content MUST be signed" enforceable by consumers (beyond strict consumer conformance)
- Mitigation: strict consumers (Section 5.2.2) defend against full stripping; registries can enforce signature requirements

**11.12 Namespace and Version Attacks**

- Name squatting: no publisher namespacing in the spec. Two publishers can publish different content with the same name, version, and type. Registries SHOULD implement namespacing.
- Version rollback: old versions with valid hashes/signatures can be redistributed. No freshness mechanism or "latest version" authority. Consumers should check registries for the latest version.
- Composition bombing: hundreds of derived_from entries pointing to real trusted content. Tooling SHOULD limit displayed derived_from entries.

**11.13 Platform-Specific Verification Failures**

- Windows CRLF (cross-reference to Implementation Note in Section 7)
- Case-insensitive filesystems: meta.yaml vs META.YAML on macOS/Windows. Implementations on case-insensitive systems SHOULD perform case-sensitive filename matching.
- Unicode homoglyph paths: visually identical filenames using different Unicode codepoints. NFC normalization doesn't collapse homoglyphs. Both files hash and verify normally. Defense is out of scope for ACP.

**11.14 Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU)**

- Content verified at install time but read from disk at runtime. An attacker with filesystem access can modify content after verification.
- Symlink TOCTOU: between checking a symlink target is internal and reading content, attacker swaps target.
- Mitigation is implementation-specific (read-only mounts, runtime re-verification). Outside ACP's scope.

**11.15 Algorithm Migration**

Expand beyond existing Section 11.5:
- When meta_version 2 arrives with a new algorithm, consumers supporting both must be careful: an attacker could force downgrade to meta_version 1 with the weaker algorithm.
- No dual-hash deprecation window is defined. This is acknowledged as a gap for future specification work.
- File permissions are excluded from the hash. An attacker can change permissions (e.g., make scripts executable) without affecting hashes.

**11.16 Registry and Ecosystem Security**

- Deprecation/yanking: no field in ACP for marking a version as unsafe. This is deliberately deferred to registries, which control distribution. ACP records provenance, not lifecycle state.
- Rekor verification: Sigstore consumers that fetch by log_index must verify inclusion proofs. Trusting Rekor responses without verification is vulnerable to a compromised Rekor instance.
- Sigstore requires network access for Rekor lookup. For air-gapped environments, SSH signing (Section 9.3) provides an offline-capable alternative.

### Existing sections to NOT modify

Do NOT change Sections 11.1–11.7. They are fine as-is. The new subsections expand coverage, they don't replace existing text.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] At least 7 new subsections added to Section 11 (numbers may vary if some were added by earlier beads)
- [ ] Each attack vector has: description of the attack, why ACP doesn't prevent it, and any available mitigations
- [ ] Self-reported claims section covers: authorship impersonation, generated_by spoofing, content-type mismatch, false lineage, published_at
- [ ] Sidecar stripping covers both full removal AND signature-only stripping
- [ ] Name squatting, version rollback, and composition bombing documented
- [ ] CRLF cross-references the implementation note in Section 7
- [ ] Case-insensitive filesystem issue documented
- [ ] TOCTOU documented for both content and symlinks
- [ ] Algorithm migration acknowledges the downgrade gap
- [ ] File permissions exclusion mentioned
- [ ] Deprecation/yanking explicitly deferred to registries with rationale
- [ ] Sigstore network requirement mentioned with SSH alternative
- [ ] Sections 11.1–11.7 unchanged
- [ ] No normative requirements added in Security Considerations — all new text is descriptive/advisory
- [ ] The tone matches the existing Security Considerations sections (honest, non-defensive, technically precise)
close_reason: Added Sections 11.8-11.16: sensitive files, OIDC identity verification, self-reported claims exploitability (5 vectors), sidecar stripping/downgrade, namespace/version attacks, platform-specific failures (CRLF/case/homoglyph), TOCTOU, algorithm migration, registry/ecosystem security. All 16 security findings covered.

title: Restrict allowed GitHub Actions to specific orgs
description:
## Why
allowed_actions is set to "all" — any GitHub Action from any author can be used in workflows. While SHA pinning is enforced (good), restricting to known-trusted orgs adds defense-in-depth against supply chain attacks through new workflow additions.

## What
Change allowed_actions from "all" to "selected" and specify trusted action orgs.

## How
- Settings > Actions > General > Actions permissions > Allow select actions
- Allow actions from: actions/*, github/*, sigstore/*, anthropics/*, ossf/*, anchore/*, golangci/*, mitchellh/*
- This still requires SHA pinning (already enforced) but limits which orgs' actions can be referenced

## Success Criteria
- Only actions from approved orgs can be used in workflows
- Existing workflows continue to work (all current actions are from these orgs)

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for this to matter (private repos have limited external exposure)

## What to do

title: Task 1.0: Add shared isValidName() for all user-entered names
description:
# Add Shared Name Validation

## Why
User-entered names become directory names via filepath.Join(destDir, name) with no character validation. Names like ../../.ssh/foo create directories outside the intended tree. This is a path traversal vulnerability that must be fixed before building the new wizard on top.

## Description
Add a shared isValidName() function used everywhere: loadout names, import names, content names. One rule, one validator, no gaps.

Restriction: [a-zA-Z0-9_-], no dots, no path separators, no leading dash, max 100 chars.

Validate in both:
- Wizard UI (immediate feedback at the name input step)
- doCreateLoadout() and manifest Parse() (defense-in-depth)

Also fix: home, _ := os.UserHomeDir() in doCreateLoadout() — check the error.

## Success Criteria
- isValidName() function exists and is used for all user-entered names
- Names with path separators, dots, or special chars are rejected with clear error message
- Existing code in doCreateLoadout(), import, and manifest.Parse() calls isValidName()
- os.UserHomeDir() error is checked in doCreateLoadout()

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: isValidName("my-loadout") returns true
2. Unit test: isValidName("../../.ssh/foo") returns false
3. Unit test: isValidName("../evil") returns false
4. Unit test: isValidName(".hidden") returns false
5. Unit test: isValidName("") returns false
6. Unit test: isValidName with 101 chars returns false
7. Unit test: wizard name step shows error for invalid names

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (doCreateLoadout)
- cli/internal/loadout/manifest.go (Parse)
- New: shared validation function (location TBD — config or model package)
close_reason: Implemented shared name validation with IsValidItemName (enhanced with leading-dash reject + 100-char max) and ValidateUserName (human-readable errors). Applied at UI layer (import wizard, create loadout wizard) and defense-in-depth (doCreateLoadoutFromScreen, manifest.Parse). Full TDD with unit and integration tests.

title: Add SourceScope and SourceProject to metadata
description:
## Problem
The metadata system has no way to track whether content was imported from a global or project scope, or which project it came from. This is needed for both the hooks scope fix and the MCP add pathway to handle name collisions gracefully.

## Implementation
Add two new fields to the `Meta` struct in `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go`:

```go
SourceScope   string `yaml:"source_scope,omitempty"`   // "global" or "project"
SourceProject string `yaml:"source_project,omitempty"` // project directory name (only when scope is "project")
```

- `SourceScope`: either `"global"` or `"project"`
- `SourceProject`: derived from `filepath.Base(projectRoot)` when scope is project (e.g., `"demo-project"`)

## Display Format
- Global items: `(global)`
- Project items: `(project/demo-project)`
- Used in CLI discovery output and TUI parenthetical badges

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — add fields to Meta struct

## Testing
- Unit test: metadata round-trip (save + load) with scope fields populated
- Unit test: scope fields omitted from YAML when empty (omitempty)

## Success Criteria
- Meta struct has SourceScope and SourceProject fields
- Fields serialize/deserialize correctly in YAML
- Empty fields don't appear in output (omitempty)
- Existing metadata tests still pass
