title: ACP Spec: Signing changes (T1, I4, I6)
description:
## What

Add domain separator prefix to signing input, clarify signing input encoding, and add a signing input test vector. All changes are in Section 9 and Appendix B.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### Change 1: Domain separator prefix (T1)

Rewrite Section 9.1 (line 298-299). Current text:
> "The signature covers the concatenation of both hashes: the content_hash value, a newline character (0x0A), and the meta_hash value."

New text:
> "The signature covers a domain-separated concatenation of both hashes. The signing input is the byte string: `ACP-V1:` followed by the content_hash value, a newline character (0x0A), and the meta_hash value."

Format: `ACP-V1:<content_hash_value>\n<meta_hash_value>`

Where:
- `ACP-V1:` is the literal ASCII string (7 bytes: 0x41 0x43 0x50 0x2D 0x56 0x31 0x3A)
- `<content_hash_value>` is the full content_hash string including `sha256:` prefix (e.g., `sha256:9c9c3591...`)
- `\n` is a single newline byte (0x0A)
- `<meta_hash_value>` is the full meta_hash string including `sha256:` prefix
- There is NO trailing newline after meta_hash_value

Add rationale note:
> "The `ACP-V1:` prefix is a domain separator that prevents cross-protocol signature replay. Without it, a signature from another protocol using the same `hash\nhash` format could be misinterpreted as an ACP signature. The prefix is versioned to allow future signing format changes without ambiguity."

### Change 2: Signing input encoding clarity (I6)

Add explicit encoding statement to Section 9.1:
> "All components of the signing input are UTF-8 encoded. The content_hash and meta_hash values include their `sha256:` prefix — the signing input contains the full string as it appears in meta.yaml (e.g., `sha256:9c9c3591...`), not just the bare hex."

### Change 3: Signing input test vector (I4)

Add a new test vector TV-SI after TV-MH2 in Appendix B:

Title: "TV-SI: Signing input construction (Section 9.1)"

Use TV-MH's content_hash and meta_hash values:
- content_hash: sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40
- meta_hash: sha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9

Signing input string:
`ACP-V1:sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40\nsha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9`

Show:
- The exact byte sequence in hex (for unambiguous implementation)
- Total byte count
- SHA-256 of the signing input (so implementations can verify they constructed it correctly, even without a private key)

Generate the actual hex and SHA-256 using: `printf 'ACP-V1:sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40\nsha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9' | sha256sum`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Section 9.1 signing input format includes `ACP-V1:` prefix
- [ ] Rationale for domain separator is present (cross-protocol replay prevention)
- [ ] Encoding is explicit: UTF-8, full sha256: prefixed strings, no trailing newline
- [ ] TV-SI test vector exists with hex bytes, byte count, and SHA-256 of signing input
- [ ] TV-SI uses TV-MH's real hash values (provenance chain continuity)
- [ ] No trailing newline after meta_hash in signing input (explicitly stated)
- [ ] SHA-256 in TV-SI is computed from actual bytes (verified via shell command)
- [ ] Sections 9.2 and 9.3 verification steps reference updated 9.1 format (no stale references)
close_reason: Section 9.1 rewritten with ACP-V1: domain separator, explicit encoding (UTF-8, full sha256: prefixed strings, no trailing newline), rationale note. TV-SI added with verified hex (150 bytes) and SHA-256 (d0ca7069...).

title: Add SigningPublicKey to release workflow LDFLAGS
description:
## Why
The release workflow builds binaries with -X main.version but doesn't pass the Ed25519 SigningPublicKey added in the H1 security fix. Without this, release binaries warn "no signing key configured" during self-update instead of verifying signatures. The cosign signing of checksums.txt already exists in the workflow but the Ed25519 verification in the binary isn't wired up.

## What
Generate an Ed25519 key pair, embed the public key in release builds, and sign checksums.txt with the private key during CI.

## How
1. Generate key pair: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 (or Go crypto/ed25519)
2. Store private key hex as GitHub secret: SYLLAGO_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
3. In release.yml, add a step after "Generate checksums" that signs checksums.txt with the private key, producing checksums.txt.sig
4. Add the public key hex to LDFLAGS: -X github.com/OpenScribbler/syllago/cli/internal/updater.SigningPublicKey=<hex>
5. Upload checksums.txt.sig as a release artifact alongside checksums.txt

Note: This is separate from the existing cosign signing (which signs checksums.txt.bundle for external verification). The Ed25519 signature is for the binary's built-in self-update verification.

## Success Criteria
- Release binaries have the signing key embedded
- Self-update downloads and verifies checksums.txt.sig
- Tampered checksums are rejected
- Dev builds still work (gracefully skip verification)

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for the release workflow to run against public GitHub Releases

title: Explode nested MCP configs into per-server catalog items
description:
## Context

MCP config items are currently discovered as one ContentItem per directory. A single config.json can contain multiple MCP servers in nested format:

\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "server-a": { "command": "npx", "args": [...] },
    "server-b": { "url": "https://..." }
  }
}
\`\`\`

This produces ONE item "example-kitchen-sink-mcp". Users cannot see or install individual servers.

## Goal

At catalog scan time, explode nested MCP configs so each server entry becomes its own ContentItem. This enables individual server visibility, selection, and installation.

## Current Architecture

### Scanner flow (catalog/scanner.go)

MCP is a "universal" content type, scanned by \`scanUniversal()\` (line 350-421). The function:
1. Iterates subdirectories under \`mcp/\`
2. Validates directory names (\`IsValidItemName\`)
3. Creates one ContentItem per directory
4. For Skills/Agents: extracts name/description from frontmatter
5. **For MCP (falls into \`default\` case, line 404): does nothing extra** — no description, no server parsing
6. Collects file listing, loads metadata, appends to catalog

### ContentItem struct (catalog/types.go, line 62-75)

\`\`\`go
type ContentItem struct {
    Name        string
    DisplayName string
    Description string
    Type        ContentType
    Path        string         // absolute path to item directory
    Provider    string         // for provider-specific content
    Files       []string
    Meta        *metadata.Meta
    Library     bool
    Registry    string
    Source      string
}
\`\`\`

No \`ServerKey\` field exists yet.

### Import dependencies

- \`catalog\` imports: metadata, gjson, yaml (and stdlib)
- \`catalog\` does NOT import \`installer\`
- \`installer\` imports \`catalog\`
- **Cannot call installer.ExtractServerEntries from catalog** — would create circular import

### config.json formats (two variants)

**Nested format** (multiple servers):
\`\`\`json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": { "command": "npx", "args": [...] },
    "http-server": { "url": "https://..." }
  }
}
\`\`\`

**Flat format** (single server):
\`\`\`json
{
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem"]
}
\`\`\`

### JSON key variations by provider

The nested wrapper key varies by provider (see installer/mcp.go MCPConfigKey):
- Most providers: \`"mcpServers"\`
- Zed: \`"context_servers"\`
- OpenCode: \`"mcp"\`
- Amp: \`"amp.mcpServers"\`

**Important for scanner**: The scanner reads the CONTENT REPO's config.json, which uses the canonical format (\`"mcpServers"\` wrapper). Provider-specific keys are only relevant at install time. The scanner should check for \`"mcpServers"\` as the canonical wrapper key.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Add ServerKey field to ContentItem (catalog/types.go)

\`\`\`go
type ContentItem struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    ServerKey   string  // For MCP: which server entry in config.json this item represents
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Modify scanUniversal (catalog/scanner.go, line 377-406)

Replace the \`default\` case with MCP-specific logic:

\`\`\`go
case MCP:
    // Read config.json and check for nested format
    configPath := filepath.Join(itemDir, "config.json")
    data, err := os.ReadFile(configPath)
    if err != nil {
        // No config.json — emit single item with directory name (graceful fallback)
        break
    }

    // Check for nested format: {"mcpServers": {"name": {...}, ...}}
    servers := gjson.GetBytes(data, "mcpServers")
    if servers.Exists() && servers.IsObject() {
        // Explode: emit one ContentItem per server entry
        servers.ForEach(func(key, value gjson.Result) bool {
            serverName := key.String()
            if !IsValidItemName(serverName) {
                cat.Warnings = append(cat.Warnings, fmt.Sprintf(
                    "skipping MCP server %q in %s — name unsafe for JSON keys", serverName, entry.Name()))
                return true
            }
            serverItem := ContentItem{
                Name:        serverName,
                Type:        MCP,
                Path:        itemDir,  // still points to parent dir (needed for install)
                ServerKey:   serverName,
                Library:     local,
                Description: mcpServerDescription(value),
                Files:       item.Files,
                Meta:        item.Meta,
            }
            cat.Items = append(cat.Items, serverItem)
            return true
        })
        // Skip appending the parent item — individual servers replace it
        continue  // skip the cat.Items = append at end of loop
    }

    // Flat format — single server, use directory name
    item.ServerKey = entry.Name()
    item.Description = mcpFlatDescription(data)
\`\`\`

**Loop control note**: The \`continue\` after exploding nested servers skips the \`cat.Items = append(cat.Items, item)\` at line 418 (which would add the parent directory as an item). For flat format, the item falls through to the normal append.

### 3. Add description helpers (catalog/scanner.go)

\`\`\`go
// mcpServerDescription generates a short description from a server config value.
func mcpServerDescription(value gjson.Result) string {
    if cmd := value.Get("command").String(); cmd != "" {
        args := value.Get("args").String()
        if args != "" {
            // Show first meaningful arg (skip flags)
            // e.g., "npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem"
        }
        return cmd  // truncate to ~60 chars
    }
    if url := value.Get("url").String(); url != "" {
        return url  // truncate to ~60 chars
    }
    return ""
}

// mcpFlatDescription generates a description from a flat config.json.
func mcpFlatDescription(data []byte) string {
    if cmd := gjson.GetBytes(data, "command").String(); cmd != "" {
        return cmd
    }
    if url := gjson.GetBytes(data, "url").String(); url != "" {
        return url
    }
    return ""
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Handle metadata and files for exploded items

When exploding nested configs, each server item shares:
- \`Path\`: same parent directory (needed to find config.json at install time)
- \`Files\`: same file listing (config.json, README, etc.)
- \`Meta\`: same .syllago.yaml metadata
- \`Library\`: same as parent
- \`Registry\`: set later by the caller (same as parent)

### 5. Naming collision handling

If two MCP config directories contain servers with the same name (e.g., both have a "filesystem" server), they'll appear as two items with the same Name but different Paths. The catalog already handles this via its override/shadowing logic (project > library > registry precedence). No special handling needed.

## Files to modify

- \`cli/internal/catalog/types.go\` — add \`ServerKey\` field to ContentItem
- \`cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go\` — MCP case in scanUniversal, add description helpers
- \`cli/internal/catalog/scanner_test.go\` — new tests for MCP explosion

## Testing

- Unit test: nested config.json with 2 servers → produces 2 ContentItems with correct Names=server keys, ServerKeys=server keys, Paths=parent dir
- Unit test: nested config.json with 1 server → produces 1 ContentItem with server key as Name
- Unit test: flat config.json → produces 1 ContentItem with Name=directory name, ServerKey=directory name
- Unit test: no config.json → produces 1 ContentItem with Name=directory name, ServerKey empty (graceful fallback)
- Unit test: nested config with invalid server name → warning emitted, server skipped, other servers still emitted
- Unit test: description shows command for stdio servers, URL for HTTP servers
- Unit test: description is empty string for configs with neither command nor URL
- Unit test: Files and Meta are shared across exploded items
- Verify \`syllago list mcp\` shows individual server names (integration-level confirmation)
- Existing catalog tests still pass (Skills, Agents, Rules, Hooks, Commands unaffected)

## Success criteria

- \`catalog.ByType(MCP)\` returns individual server items, not directory-level items, for nested configs
- Each server item has ServerKey populated
- Flat-format configs still work (one item per directory)
- No circular imports between catalog and installer
- Descriptions are auto-generated from command/URL
- No regression in non-MCP content type scanning
close_reason: Implemented and verified. ServerKey field added to ContentItem, scanUniversal explodes nested MCP configs into per-server items, description helpers generate from command/URL. 7 test functions cover all cases. Coverage 80.9%.

title: L7: installed.json/settings.json crash window between writes
description:
## Why
For hooks, settings.json is written first, then installed.json. If the process crashes between these two writes, the hook is in settings.json but not tracked in installed.json — an orphan that syllago can't uninstall. Same pattern in MCP install.

## What
Document the recovery procedure. A full fix (write-ahead log) is over-engineering for this narrow crash window.

## How (general)
- Add a recovery note to syllago doctor output: detect hooks/MCP entries in settings.json not tracked by installed.json
- doctor command already exists — add an orphan detection check
- Optionally: offer to clean up orphaned entries

## Success Criteria
- syllago doctor detects orphaned hooks/MCP entries
- User guidance on how to resolve orphans (manual removal or doctor --fix)

## Testing
- Unit test: create orphaned hook entry in settings.json (not in installed.json), run doctor — detected
- Unit test: all entries tracked — doctor reports clean

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L7
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: ACP Spec: Meta hash & field handling (T2, I1, I3)
description:
## What

Rewrite Section 8 (Meta Hash Algorithm) to use an explicit field allowlist per meta_version, and add a normative YAML-to-JSON type mapping table. These are the two most impactful interoperability changes — every panelist and both LLM reviewers flagged them.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### Change 1: Explicit field allowlist (T2 + I3)

Replace Section 8's current step 1:
> "Extract all fields from meta.yaml EXCEPT meta_hash and signature."

With an explicit allowlist:

meta_version 1 hashed fields (in JCS sort order):
- authors, content_hash, derived_from, description, generated_by, meta_version, name, published_at, source_commit, source_repo, type, version

Add normative text:
- "Publishers MUST include exactly the fields listed for the applicable meta_version. Consumers MUST hash exactly these fields."
- "Fields not in the allowlist for the applicable meta_version MUST NOT be included in meta_hash computation."
- "Unknown fields (not in the allowlist) MAY be present in meta.yaml for forward-compatibility but MUST NOT affect meta_hash."
- This replaces the current "extract all except..." approach.

### Change 2: YAML-to-JSON type mapping table (I1)

Add a new subsection (8.1 or between steps 2 and 3) titled "YAML-to-JSON Type Mapping" with a normative table:

| meta.yaml field | YAML type | JSON type | Notes |
|----------------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| meta_version | integer | number (integer) | MUST serialize as integer, not float |
| type | string | string | |
| name | string | string | |
| version | integer | number (integer) | MUST serialize as integer, not float |
| description | string | string | |
| authors | sequence of strings | array of strings | |
| generated_by | string | string | |
| source_repo | string | string | |
| source_commit | string | string | |
| published_at | string | string | MUST be parsed as string, NOT as YAML timestamp. Implementations MUST quote or tag to prevent implicit datetime parsing. |
| content_hash | string | string | |
| derived_from | sequence of objects | array of objects | Each object: source(string), relation(string), source_hash(string) |
| signature.method | string | string | |
| signature.identity | string | string | |
| signature.log_index | integer | number (integer) | |
| signature.value | string | string | |
| signature.algorithm | string | string | |
| signature.key_id | string | string | |

Add normative requirements:
- "YAML boolean coercion MUST NOT be applied to any field. Values like yes, no, on, off MUST be treated as strings if they appear."
- "YAML anchors and aliases MUST be fully resolved before serialization."
- "Multi-line YAML strings (folded/literal block scalars) MUST be resolved to their final string value, with trailing newlines preserved as-is."
- "Absent optional fields MUST be omitted from the JSON object — they MUST NOT be serialized as null."

### Impact on test vectors

TV-MH and TV-MH2 should NOT change — they already use the correct fields. But add a note confirming the allowlist matches the fields present in TV-MH.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Section 8 uses explicit field allowlist (not "extract all except...")
- [ ] All 12 hashed fields for meta_version 1 are listed
- [ ] signature and meta_hash are explicitly NOT in the allowlist
- [ ] Unknown fields handling is explicit: MAY be present, MUST NOT affect meta_hash
- [ ] YAML-to-JSON type mapping table exists with all fields
- [ ] published_at explicitly requires string parsing (not YAML timestamp)
- [ ] YAML boolean coercion prohibition is normative (MUST NOT)
- [ ] Absent optional fields rule is normative (MUST be omitted, not null)
- [ ] YAML anchors/aliases resolution requirement is stated
- [ ] TV-MH and TV-MH2 are unchanged (hashes still valid)
- [ ] A note confirms TV-MH fields match the meta_version 1 allowlist
- [ ] No other sections changed except Section 8 and its new subsections
close_reason: Section 8 rewritten with explicit field allowlist (8.1) and YAML-to-JSON type mapping table (8.2). 12 hashed fields listed for meta_version 1. Unknown fields handling explicit. TV-MH/TV-MH2 unchanged.

title: ACP Spec: Conformance & verification (T4, T3, T9)
description:
## What

Add strict/permissive conformance classes to Section 5, add non-normative reject recommendations, and add OIDC identity verification requirements. These changes affect Sections 5, 9.2, and 9.4.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### Change 1: Conformance classes (T4)

Restructure Section 5.2 (Consumers) into two conformance levels. The current text becomes the baseline, with two levels layered on top.

New structure for Section 5.2:

**5.2 Consumers**

"A conforming consumer is any tool or process that reads and verifies meta.yaml files. This specification defines two consumer conformance levels: strict and permissive."

**5.2.1 All Consumers (Baseline)**

Keep existing requirements that apply to both levels:
- MUST verify content_hash by recomputing it per Section 7
- MUST verify meta_hash by recomputing it per Section 8
- MUST reject content where content_hash or meta_hash verification fails
- MUST verify signature when present, per Section 9
- MUST reject content where signature verification fails. No override.

**5.2.2 Strict Consumer**

A strict consumer enforces provenance as a security boundary:
- MUST NOT accept content without a valid meta.yaml.
- MUST NOT accept content where meta.yaml is present but signature is absent, when the consumer's policy requires signatures.
- MUST clearly indicate to the user when content is rejected and why.

Add rationale note:
> "Strict conformance is designed for security-conscious deployments — enterprise environments, CI/CD pipelines, and registries — where provenance is a gate, not advisory. By requiring meta.yaml, strict consumers defend against sidecar stripping (Section 11.4), where an attacker removes provenance from content to bypass integrity checks."

**5.2.3 Permissive Consumer**

A permissive consumer treats provenance as informational:
- SHOULD surface the absence of meta.yaml visibly to users.
- SHOULD NOT treat content without meta.yaml identically to content with provenance.
- SHOULD distinguish unsigned content from signed content in user-facing displays.
- MUST clearly indicate provenance status (verified, unsigned, absent) in user-facing displays.

Add rationale note:
> "Permissive conformance supports ecosystem adoption — content management tools, personal workflows, and early-stage registries where requiring provenance for all content would be impractical. Permissive consumers still verify provenance when present; the distinction is in how they handle its absence."

Add a note about choosing a level:
> "Consumers SHOULD document which conformance level they implement. Registries that mandate strict consumer behavior MAY reject content that would be accepted by a permissive consumer."

### Change 2: Non-normative reject recommendations (T3)

Add a note after Section 5.2.1 (after the MUST reject requirements):

> **Note (Informative):** This specification defines verification requirements but does not prescribe the user experience of rejection. Implementations may reject content by refusing installation, displaying a warning with user override, blocking execution, or other mechanisms appropriate to their context. The normative requirement is that content failing verification MUST NOT be treated as integrity-verified — the mechanism for communicating this to the user is an implementation decision.

### Change 3: OIDC identity verification requirement (T9)

In Section 9.2 verification step 5 (line 314), change:
> "Verify the OIDC identity in the certificate matches signature.identity."

To:
> "Verify that the OIDC identity in the certificate is authorized for this content. Consumers MUST perform identity verification — accepting signatures from any OIDC identity without checking authorization is a security vulnerability (see Section 11.9). The matching algorithm (exact string match, prefix matching, regular expression, or policy-based lookup) is an implementation decision."

Add new Section 11.9 (or appropriate subsection):
> **11.9 OIDC Identity Verification**
>
> Sigstore verification proves that content was signed by someone with a valid OIDC token — it does not prove it was signed by the right person. Consumers MUST verify that the signer's identity is authorized, not merely that a valid signature exists.
>
> Without identity verification, an attacker can sign content with their own valid OIDC identity and the signature will verify successfully. This was a known vulnerability in early Sigstore tooling (cosign prior to v1.14.0), which was fixed by making identity verification mandatory.
>
> The identity matching algorithm is deliberately unspecified — it is a consumer policy decision. Common approaches include exact string matching, prefix matching for organizational trust (e.g., trusting all identities under a GitHub organization), and regular expression patterns. Consumers SHOULD document their identity matching behavior.

### Change 4: SSH trust note (existing T8 applies here too)

In Section 9.3 verification step 3 (line 326), add after "or other trust mechanism":
> "The trust mechanism for SSH keys is implementation-defined. Unlike Sigstore, SSH verification does not require network access and is suitable for air-gapped environments."

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Section 5.2 has three subsections: Baseline (5.2.1), Strict (5.2.2), Permissive (5.2.3)
- [ ] Strict requires MUST NOT accept without meta.yaml
- [ ] Permissive uses SHOULD/SHOULD NOT language (current behavior)
- [ ] Both levels share baseline verification requirements (content_hash, meta_hash, signature when present)
- [ ] Rationale notes explain WHY each level exists and WHEN to use each
- [ ] A note recommends consumers document which level they implement
- [ ] Non-normative reject note explains verification != UX decision
- [ ] Section 9.2 step 5 requires identity verification with MUST language
- [ ] Identity matching algorithm is explicitly stated as implementation-defined
- [ ] Section 11.9 references cosign v1.14.0 vulnerability as precedent
- [ ] SSH air-gapped note present in Section 9.3
- [ ] No changes to Section 5.1 (Publishers) or 5.3 (Registries)
- [ ] Existing MUST reject requirements preserved in baseline (not weakened)
close_reason: Section 5.2 restructured into 5.2.1 (baseline), 5.2.2 (strict), 5.2.3 (permissive) with rationale notes. Non-normative reject note added. OIDC identity verification MUST in Section 9.2 step 5. SSH air-gapped note in Section 9.3.

- func (ci *ContentItem) IsBuiltin() bool

title: Agents: OpenCode canonicalize + fix renderer
description:
## Problem
OpenCode agents have no inbound conversion and the renderer is too minimal. Specifically:
- No canonicalize path for OpenCode agents
- Renderer uses maxTurns but OpenCode calls it steps
- Renderer outputs tools as []string but OpenCode uses map[string]bool
- Missing color, temperature fields that have canonical equivalents
- Missing mode (primary/subagent) and granular permission.* fields

## Required Changes
### Add Canonicalize
1. Add canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent() function
2. Parse OpenCode frontmatter: steps → maxTurns, permission.edit:deny → permissionMode:plan, temperature/color → canonical fields
3. Handle tools as map[string]bool → extract enabled tool names to []string
4. Register in Canonicalize switch

### Fix Renderer
1. Map maxTurns → steps field name in output
2. Output tools as map[string]bool format (all true)
3. Add color and temperature to output
4. Map permissionMode to OpenCode's permission.* structure where possible

## Success Criteria
- [ ] canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent() parses steps/permission/temperature/color
- [ ] OpenCode tools map[string]bool converted to canonical []string
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() outputs steps (not maxTurns)
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() outputs tools as map format
- [ ] renderOpenCodeAgent() includes color and temperature
- [ ] Round-trip: OpenCode agent → canonical → OpenCode preserves key fields
- [ ] Existing agent tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/agents.go` (add canonicalizeOpenCodeAgent, fix renderOpenCodeAgent, update Canonicalize switch)
- `cli/internal/converter/agents_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/agents-research.md — Issues #3, #4
close_reason: OpenCode agents: canonicalize added (steps→maxTurns, tools map→array, color, temperature). Renderer fixed with correct field names/formats. 5 tests. All pass.

title: Add registryAddNonSyllagoMsg type
description:
## Phase 2a: Add registryAddNonSyllagoMsg type

### Problem
When doRegistryAdd detects a non-syllago repo with provider content (app.go:237-246), it deletes the clone and returns an error. Should instead preserve the clone and send a new message type.

### Implementation
1. Add new message type to app.go:
```go
type registryAddNonSyllagoMsg struct {
    name      string
    clonePath string
    scan      catalog.NativeScanResult
}
```

2. In doRegistryAdd (~line 237-246), where it currently detects non-syllago content and returns an error:
   - Do NOT call os.RemoveAll on the clone
   - Return a tea.Cmd that sends registryAddNonSyllagoMsg with the name, clone path, and scan result
   - Remove the error message about "use syllago add <path> instead"

3. The registryAddNonSyllagoMsg should also set registryOpInProgress = false (like other done messages do)

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — message type definition and doRegistryAdd function

### Success Criteria
- [ ] registryAddNonSyllagoMsg type exists with name, clonePath, scan fields
- [ ] doRegistryAdd sends this message instead of an error when non-syllago content detected
- [ ] Clone directory is NOT deleted when non-syllago content is detected
- [ ] registryOpInProgress is set to false when the message is handled
- [ ] The old error message referencing "syllago add <path>" is removed
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add the handler for registryAddNonSyllagoMsg yet (that's Phase 2b)
- Do not modify import.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase B sanity check: Review spec + plan for quality and correctness
description: Sub-agent reviews the Phase B spec and implementation plan for: (1) Clarity — are instructions unambiguous enough for an impl agent? (2) Security — any injection vectors, path traversal risks, unsafe patterns? (3) Go best practices — proper error handling, no panics in non-invariant code, correct BubbleTea patterns? (4) Glaring problems — missing edge cases, race conditions, impossible states? (5) Architectural fit — consistent with existing modal patterns (remove.go, confirm.go, modal.go)? (6) Test quality — planned tests exercise real behavior, not superficial assertions. No tests that pass but prove nothing (e.g. checking a string isn't empty instead of checking it contains the right value). Every test must justify its existence by catching a real failure mode. If issues found, they must be fixed in the spec/plan before implementation beads are created.

- "does not support format conversion" → ErrConvertNotSupported
- "cannot locate content file" → ErrItemNotFound
- "reading content: %w" → ErrSystemIO (wrapped)
- "canonicalizing content: %w" → ErrConvertParseFailed (wrapped)
- "rendering to format: %w" → ErrConvertRenderFailed (wrapped)
- "not compatible with format" → ErrConvertNotSupported
- "writing output: %w" → ErrSystemIO (wrapped)

title: Competitive landscape research: ai-rulez, block/ai-rules, rulesync
description:
Research completed 2026-03-27. Captures the competitive landscape for AI coding tool content managers.

KEY COMPETITORS:
1. rulesync (dyoshikawa) — 936 stars, 608K npm/mo, v7.23.0, TypeScript
   - Hub-and-spoke (same arch as syllago), 27 providers, 8 content types
   - DOES convert hooks (27 canonical events, 7 providers) and MCP (16 providers)
   - Has permissions content type, simulated features for non-native providers
   - Missing: loadouts, registry ecosystem, TUI, binary distribution (needs Node 22)
   
2. ai-rulez (Goldziher) — 95 stars, 404 npm/wk, v3.11.2, Go
   - Fan-out generator (NOT hub-and-spoke), 13 providers, 6 content types
   - NO hook conversion. MCP is simple pass-through.
   - Has: built-in content library (23 domains), token compression, MCP server mode, profiles
   - Missing: bidirectional conversion, hooks, loadouts, registry

3. block/ai-rules (Block/Square) — 83 stars, v1.5.1, Rust
   - Symlink farm (simplest arch), 11 providers, 4 content types
   - NO hooks, MCP is file copy only
   - Has: monorepo nested traversal, status/sync checking, enterprise backing
   - Missing: real conversion, hooks, MCP handling, loadouts, registry

SYLLAGO'S DURABLE ADVANTAGES:
- Loadouts (curated installable bundles) — no competitor has this
- Registry ecosystem (community content sharing) — no competitor has this
- TUI (interactive browsing/management) — no competitor has this
- Go binary (zero runtime deps) — rulesync needs Node 22, ai-rulez is Go too
- Hook conversion — shared with rulesync but syllago's spec is more formal

SYLLAGO'S GAPS VS RULESYNC:
- Provider count: 12 vs 27
- Hook event coverage: ~10 vs 27 canonical events
- Hook adapter count: 5 vs 7 providers
- No permissions content type
- No simulated features for non-native providers
- No shared+per-provider hook override pattern
- No Cursor env var format handling for MCP

STATUS: Research complete. Actionable items broken into separate beads.

title: M2: Hook security scanner not called during install
description:
## Why
hook_security.go has a regex-based scanner for dangerous commands (curl, wget, nc, rm -rf) but installHook() never calls it. The scanner only runs during import/convert. Registry content can change between import and install (registry updates). The last chance to warn the user is at install time, and it's skipped.

## What
Wire ScanHookSecurity into the install path so users get warnings before hooks are written to provider settings.

## How (general)
- In installer/hooks.go installHook(), call converter.ScanHookSecurity on the hook command before writing to settings
- Display warnings to the user with finding details
- Consider requiring --allow-unsafe flag for high-severity findings
- Don't block on info-level findings, only warn

## Success Criteria
- Installing a hook with dangerous commands (curl, wget, etc.) shows security warnings
- Warnings include the specific dangerous command found and its severity
- Installation proceeds with user acknowledgment or --allow-unsafe flag
- Normal hooks install without interruption

## Testing
- Unit test: install hook with curl command, verify warning is emitted
- Unit test: install hook with safe command, verify no warning
- Unit test: --allow-unsafe flag suppresses blocking on warnings
- Table-driven tests for each pattern in the scanner

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M2
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: L5: CleanStale removes dirs by name prefix without uid check
description:
## Why
CleanStale iterates /tmp for directories named syllago-sandbox-* and removes them with os.RemoveAll. On shared systems, another user's legitimately-named directory could be deleted. Inner symlinks within a real directory would be followed by RemoveAll.

## What
Verify directory ownership before removal in CleanStale.

## How (general)
- After Lstat, check that the directory is owned by the current uid (os.Getuid())
- Use stat.Sys().(*syscall.Stat_t).Uid on Linux to get owner
- Skip directories not owned by current user

## Success Criteria
- Only directories owned by the current user are cleaned
- Other users' syllago-sandbox-* directories are left alone

## Testing
- Unit test: directory owned by current user — removed
- Unit test: directory owned by different user — skipped (may need root to test, or mock)

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L5
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

7. Add test cases for the new permissionMode values and effort:max

title: Fail-fast name validation before clone
description:
## Phase 0b: Fail-fast name validation before clone

### Problem
Name derivation (registry.NameFromURL) and duplicate/invalid checks happen inside the async command (app.go:218-228), after the modal closes and clone begins. These can be checked synchronously before the expensive network operation.

### Implementation
At app.go:1160-1171, in the handler that detects `!a.registryAddModal.active`:

1. After modal confirms (`a.registryAddModal.confirmed`), derive name from URL (or use override)
2. Check `catalog.IsValidRegistryName(name)` — if invalid, set modal error, re-open modal
3. Check for duplicates in `a.registryCfg.Registries` — if duplicate, set modal error, re-open modal
4. Only call `doRegistryAdd` if all validation passes

**Re-opening the modal on failure:**
```go
a.registryAddModal.message = fmt.Sprintf("Invalid registry name: %s", name)
a.registryAddModal.messageIsErr = true
a.registryAddModal.active = true
a.registryAddModal.confirmed = false
a.focus = focusModal  // keep input routing consistent
```
This works because modal's View() checks `if !m.active { return "" }`.

**Remove the duplicate checks from inside doRegistryAdd** (app.go:218-228) since they now happen synchronously. The async command can assume the name is valid and unique.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 1160-1171 (modal confirm handler) and 218-228 (remove redundant checks)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Invalid name shows error in modal and keeps it open (user can edit and retry)
- [ ] Duplicate name shows error in modal and keeps it open
- [ ] a.focus is set to focusModal when re-activating the modal
- [ ] doRegistryAdd no longer does name validation (moved to synchronous path)
- [ ] Empty URL validation still works (already handled by modal.go)
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change the modal.go Update method (it already sets confirmed=true, active=false on Enter)
- Do not touch doRegistryAdd's clone/scan logic — only remove the name validation from it
close_reason: Closed

title: 3.1: Wire structured errors — add_cmd.go + install_cmd.go
description:
Files: cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go (~18 errors), cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go (~8 errors)

Convert all fmt.Errorf calls to structured errors using the conversion patterns from the design doc.

add_cmd.go error mapping:
- "missing --from flag" → ErrInputMissing
- "cannot specify both a target and --all" → ErrInputConflict
- "unknown provider" → ErrProviderNotFound (already structured — convert from Pattern A to Pattern B)
- "loading global/project config: %w" → ErrConfigNotFound (wrapped)
- "expanding paths: %w" → ErrConfigPath (wrapped)
- "cannot determine home directory" → ErrSystemHomedir
- "discovering content: %w" → ErrCatalogScanFailed (wrapped)
- "unknown content type" → ErrItemTypeUnknown
- "no %s named %q found" → ErrItemNotFound
- "reading/splitting/marshaling %w" → ErrSystemIO (wrapped)

install_cmd.go error mapping:
- Provider not found → ErrProviderNotFound (already structured — convert to Pattern B)
- "loading config: %w" → ErrConfigNotFound (wrapped)
- "scanning library: %w" → ErrCatalogScanFailed (wrapped)
- "cannot determine home directory" → ErrSystemHomedir
- "no item named" → ErrItemNotFound

Important: Convert existing Pattern A (PrintStructuredError + SilentError) to Pattern B (just return the StructuredError). The 3-line pattern becomes 1 line.

Success criteria:
- No plain fmt.Errorf for user-facing errors remains in either file
- go vet ./cmd/syllago/ passes
- go test ./cmd/syllago/ passes
- Existing tests that check error messages still pass (StructuredError.Error() includes the message)

Commit: feat(cli): wire structured errors in add and install commands

Depends on: Phase 1 infrastructure complete

title: MCP: Fix Cline SSE support (data loss bug)
description:
## Problem
The MCP converter incorrectly blocks SSE/HTTP servers when rendering to Cline. `renderClineMCP()` in mcp.go:564-568 skips any server with a URL, saying "Cline only supports stdio, not HTTP." Research confirms Cline supports SSE transport with `url` and `headers` fields.

Additionally, `clineMCPServerConfig` struct is missing `url` and `headers` fields, so importing a Cline config with SSE servers silently loses them.

## Root Cause
The converter was written when Cline only supported stdio. Cline has since added SSE support.

## Required Changes
1. Add `url` and `headers` fields to `clineMCPServerConfig` struct
2. Update `canonicalizeClineMCP()` to map url/headers to canonical fields
3. Remove the URL-based server skip logic in `renderClineMCP()`
4. Emit url and headers in rendered Cline config for SSE servers
5. Set type appropriately (SSE vs stdio based on presence of url)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `clineMCPServerConfig` has `URL string` and `Headers map[string]string` fields
- [ ] Canonicalizing a Cline config with SSE servers preserves url+headers in canonical output
- [ ] Rendering to Cline with SSE servers emits url+headers (no skip)
- [ ] Rendering to Cline with stdio servers still works correctly
- [ ] Existing MCP tests pass
- [ ] New test: round-trip Cline SSE config through canonicalize+render
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp.go` (clineMCPServerConfig, canonicalizeClineMCP, renderClineMCP)
- `cli/internal/converter/mcp_test.go` (new test cases)

## Research Reference
docs/research/mcp-research.md — Converter Coverage Audit, Issue #1 and #2
close_reason: Fixed. Cline SSE support added to canonicalize+render. All tests pass.

title: Bridge registry redirect to import flow
description:
## Phase 2c: Bridge registry redirect to import flow

### Problem
When user confirms "Browse and import?" modal, need to hand off the preserved clone to the import model and switch to the import screen.

### Implementation

**1. New method on importModel (import.go):**
```go
func (m *importModel) initFromExternalClone(path string) {
    m.clonedPath = path  // takes ownership for cleanup
    m.fromRegistryRedirect = true
    m.message = ""
    m.messageIsErr = false
    m.pickCursor = 0

    cat, err := catalog.Scan(path, path)
    if err != nil || len(cat.Items) == 0 {
        // Content detected by native scan but not importable via catalog.Scan
        m.step = stepGitURL
        m.message = "Detected provider content but no importable items. Use CLI: syllago add <path>"
        m.messageIsErr = true
        m.cleanup()
        return
    }
    m.clonedItems = cat.Items
    m.step = stepGitPick
}
```

**2. Add fromRegistryRedirect field to importModel (import.go):**
```go
fromRegistryRedirect bool
```

**3. Modify Esc handler in updateGitPick (import.go ~line 709-713):**
When m.fromRegistryRedirect is true, instead of going to stepGitURL:
- Call m.cleanup()
- Clear m.fromRegistryRedirect
- Return a message (e.g., importBackToRegistriesMsg{}) that tells App.Update to switch to screenRegistries

**4. Handle confirm in App.Update (app.go):**
When the "Browse" confirmation is accepted:
```go
a.importer.cleanup()  // clean up any previous clone
a.importer.initFromExternalClone(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""  // import model owns it now
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
a.screen = screenImport
```

**5. Handle importBackToRegistriesMsg in App.Update:**
```go
a.screen = screenRegistries
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — initFromExternalClone method, fromRegistryRedirect field, updateGitPick Esc handler
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirm handler, importBackToRegistriesMsg handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] initFromExternalClone method exists on importModel
- [ ] fromRegistryRedirect bool field exists on importModel
- [ ] Confirming "Browse" switches to screenImport with items populated
- [ ] Esc from stepGitPick when fromRegistryRedirect returns to screenRegistries (not stepGitURL)
- [ ] Clone cleanup happens via import model's cleanup() on Esc
- [ ] Scan strategy mismatch handled: if catalog.Scan finds nothing, shows explanatory message and cleans up
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify native_scan.go
- Do not add new import wizard steps — reuse existing stepGitPick

title: H1: Self-update binary download without signature verification
description:
## Why
syllago update downloads a binary from GitHub Releases and replaces the running executable. SHA-256 checksum verification exists, but checksums.txt is downloaded from the same source as the binary. If GitHub Releases is compromised (account takeover, CI compromise) or MITM'd, an attacker controls both the binary and the checksums. The signing package has interfaces defined but is unimplemented.

Same-origin checksums only catch accidental corruption, not deliberate tampering. Real supply-chain security requires the verification key to come from a different trust root than the artifact.

## What
Implement cryptographic signature verification for self-update binaries. The signing package at cli/internal/signing/signing.go already has interfaces defined — implement them.

## How (general)
- Implement the signing package (cosign, GPG, or minisign)
- Sign checksums.txt during CI release builds with a key NOT stored alongside the release
- Hardcode the public verification key in the binary itself
- Verify signature before applying update
- Reject updates that fail signature verification
- Alternative for initial release: disable auto-update and document manual verification

## Success Criteria
- Binary updates are verified against a signature from a different trust root than GitHub Releases
- Tampered binaries or checksums are rejected with a clear error message
- Update flow works end-to-end with signature verification

## Testing
- Unit tests: verify signature check passes with valid signature, fails with tampered binary, fails with tampered checksum
- Integration test: mock GitHub API returning modified binary, verify rejection
- Test with expired/revoked key handling

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section H1
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Hook security warnings during import
description:
WHY: The hooks deep-dive (docs/providers/audit/hooks.md lines 126-131) highlights that project-level hooks are an attack surface. A malicious .claude/settings.json in a cloned repo could execute arbitrary commands. syllago should warn when importing hooks that contain potentially dangerous commands.

RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/audit/hooks.md (Security section, lines 126-131)
RESEARCH REF: Community projects: lasso-security/claude-hooks (prompt injection defense), affaan-m/everything-claude-code (config protection hooks)

WHAT TO BUILD:
- During syllago add of hooks content, scan hook commands for:
  - Shell commands that access network (curl, wget, nc)
  - Commands that modify system state (rm, chmod, chown)
  - Commands that read secrets (cat ~/.ssh/*, env | grep)
  - Overly broad matchers (match everything)
- Produce warnings (not blocks) with explanations
- Optional --security-scan flag for deeper analysis

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Importing hooks with 'curl' commands produces a security warning
- Importing hooks with narrow matchers and safe commands produces no warning
- Warnings are informational, not blocking

TESTING: Unit tests with various hook commands
close_reason: ScanHookSecurity scanner: HIGH (network/destructive), MEDIUM (broad matchers/permissions), LOW (system paths). 20 test cases. All tests pass.

title: Fix status rendering on welcome screen
description:
## Phase 1c: Fix status rendering on welcome screen

### Problem
app.go:2103-2108 always renders statusMessage with successMsgStyle and "Done:" prefix — even for errors. In-progress messages also get the "Done:" prefix.

### Implementation
Replace the existing status rendering block (~lines 2103-2108) with:
```go
if a.statusMessage != "" {
    if a.statusIsErr {
        s += errorMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    } else {
        s += successMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    }
    for _, w := range a.statusWarnings {
        s += warningStyle.Render("Warning: " + w) + "\n"
    }
    s += "\n"
}
```

Key changes:
- Use statusIsErr to select error vs success style
- Drop the "Done:" prefix entirely (past tense messages like "Added registry: foo" already communicate completion)
- Keep warning rendering for statusWarnings

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` around lines 2103-2108 (renderContentWelcome)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Error messages render with errorMsgStyle (not green successMsgStyle)
- [ ] Success messages render with successMsgStyle
- [ ] No "Done:" prefix on any status message
- [ ] statusWarnings still render with warningStyle
- [ ] In-progress messages don't get "Done:" prefix
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes (golden files may need updating)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change how statusMessage is set — only how it's rendered
close_reason: Closed

title: Enable GitHub Code Security for CodeQL scanning
description:
## Why
CodeQL workflow runs on every push but fails with: "Code Security must be enabled for this repository to use code scanning." The analysis completes but results can't be uploaded. This will resolve once the repo is public, but needs explicit enablement.

## What
Enable Code Security features in GitHub repo settings so CodeQL can upload scan results.

## How (general)
- Go to repo Settings > Security > Code security and analysis
- Enable "Code scanning" (requires public repo or GitHub Advanced Security license)
- Alternatively: remove the CodeQL workflow until the repo is public, then re-enable

## Success Criteria
- CodeQL workflow completes without errors
- Code scanning results appear in the Security tab

## Blocked By
Repo must be public (or have GitHub Advanced Security enabled for private repos)

title: TUI: git fetch on Init() blocks terminal input when credentials unavailable
description:
## Problem

The TUI's `Init()` method (app.go:178) calls `checkForUpdate()` which runs `git fetch origin main --quiet --tags` as an async Bubble Tea Cmd. When git credentials are not available (private repo, expired token, VHS recording, CI), git prompts for `Username for 'https://github.com':` on the terminal.

This prompt **steals stdin from the TUI**, making all keyboard navigation impossible. The TUI renders but is completely frozen — no keystrokes reach Bubble Tea because the git subprocess owns the pty input.

## Root Cause

`update.go:609` — the `exec.Command("git", ...)` inherits the process's stdin/stdout/stderr. When git needs credentials, it prompts on the same terminal the TUI is using.

```go
// update.go:606-610
func checkForUpdate(repoRoot, localVersion string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        fetchCmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
        fetchCmd.Run() // ← inherits stdin, blocks on credential prompt
```

## Impact

- TUI is completely unusable when credentials aren't cached
- Affects VHS/demo recordings (no credential helper available)
- Affects CI/CD environments
- Affects users who cloned via HTTPS without a credential helper
- No timeout — hangs indefinitely

## Recommended Fix

Three changes in `update.go`, `checkForUpdate()`:

### 1. Suppress credential prompts (one-line fix)
```go
fetchCmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0")
```
This makes git fail silently instead of prompting. The existing `fetchCmd.Run()` already ignores errors, so this is safe.

### 2. Detach from terminal
```go
fetchCmd.Stdin = nil
fetchCmd.Stdout = nil  
fetchCmd.Stderr = nil
```
Prevents git from ever touching the TUI's terminal.

### 3. Add a timeout (defense in depth)
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
defer cancel()
fetchCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
```

## Additional Consideration

The `registryAutoSync` setting exists in settings.go but registry sync is user-triggered (press `s`). The `Init()` update check is NOT registry sync — it's a version check against the syllago repo itself. Consider:
- Throttling to once per day (cache last check timestamp)
- Making it a setting (like `registryAutoSync`) that users can disable
- Showing a non-blocking notification instead of blocking launch

## Files

- `cli/internal/tui/update.go` — lines 606-610 (`checkForUpdate`)
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — line 178 (`Init()`)

## How to Reproduce

```bash
# Record with VHS (no credential helper in pty)
HOME=/tmp/clean-home vhs demo.tape

# Or run with expired/missing credentials
GIT_ASKPASS=false syl
# TUI renders but keyboard input is frozen
```
close_reason: Fixed git fetch stdin steal + added Update check setting

title: Validate: Signing changes (T1, I4, I6)
description:
## What

Validation gate for signing changes bead (syllago-86k1x).

## Validation Checklist

1. **Domain separator present:** Section 9.1 must contain `ACP-V1:` as part of the signing input format. The prefix must be described as literal ASCII bytes.

2. **No trailing newline:** Section 9.1 must explicitly state there is no trailing newline after the meta_hash value.

3. **Encoding explicit:** Section 9.1 must state: UTF-8 encoding, full sha256: prefixed strings (not bare hex).

4. **Rationale present:** A note explaining WHY the domain separator exists (cross-protocol replay prevention) must be present.

5. **TV-SI exists:** A signing input test vector must exist in Appendix B.

6. **TV-SI hex verification:** Independently compute the signing input hex. The string is:
   `ACP-V1:sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40\nsha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9`
   Run: `printf 'ACP-V1:sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40\nsha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9' | sha256sum`
   Compare result against TV-SI's stated SHA-256. Must match exactly.

7. **TV-SI uses real hashes:** Confirm content_hash matches TV-03 (9c9c3591...) and meta_hash matches TV-MH (ff9c3684...).

8. **Verification steps updated:** Sections 9.2 step 3 ("Construct the signing input per Section 9.1") and 9.3 step 2 must still reference Section 9.1 — no stale text about old signing format.

9. **No other test vectors changed:** TV-01 through TV-MH2 must be unchanged.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 9 checks pass
- [ ] TV-SI SHA-256 independently verified via shell command
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: 9/9 checks PASS. Domain separator, encoding, rationale, TV-SI hex and SHA-256 all verified.

title: Add 'syllago add --from shared' for bundled content
description:
WHY: Syllago bundles skills in content/skills/ (syllago-guide, syllago-import, syllago-provider-audit, etc.) but there's no workflow to get them into the user's library. syllago add requires --from <provider>, and shared content isn't a provider. Users who clone a repo with bundled content can see it in syllago list ([shared] tag) but can't install or convert it.

USE CASE: User clones syllago, runs make build, wants to use the built-in syllago-provider-audit skill. Today they're stuck — no command path from shared content to library to installed.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- syllago add --from shared: discovers content in the repo's content/ directory and adds to library
- syllago add <name> --from shared: adds a specific item
- syllago add --from shared --all: adds all bundled content
- The shared content dir is already scanned by catalog (shows as [shared] in list) — the gap is in the add command's provider requirement

AFFECTS: Any repo that bundles syllago content, not just the syllago repo itself. Teams could put skills in their project's content/ dir.

IMPLEMENTATION HINTS:
- cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go: the --from flag validates against provider.AllProviders — need to also accept "shared"
- When --from shared, scan the project's content/ dir instead of a provider's config dir
- The rest of the add flow (copy to library, metadata creation) should work as-is
close_reason: Implemented in 62da6d4

title: Agents: Kiro canonicalize warnings for dropped fields
description:
## Problem
Kiro agent canonicalize silently drops 7 provider-specific fields: toolAliases, toolsSettings, resources, includeMcpJson, includePowers, keyboardShortcut, welcomeMessage. These have no canonical equivalent so dropping is correct, but the user should be warned about data loss.

## Required Changes
1. In canonicalizeKiroAgent(): after parsing kiroAgentMeta, check each unique field
2. If any are non-zero, append warnings to the Result
3. The Kiro agent canonicalize currently returns (*Result, error) — the Result.Warnings slice should carry these

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Importing a Kiro agent with toolAliases emits a warning mentioning the dropped field
- [ ] Same for toolsSettings, resources, includeMcpJson, includePowers, keyboardShortcut, welcomeMessage
- [ ] Importing a Kiro agent without these fields produces no warnings
- [ ] Existing agent tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/agents.go` (canonicalizeKiroAgent)
- `cli/internal/converter/agents_test.go`
close_reason: Kiro agent canonicalize now emits warnings for 7 dropped fields. 3 new tests. All pass.

title: Harden git wrapper subcommand parsing
description:
The sandbox git wrapper only checks $1 for blocked subcommands, making it bypassable via git flags preceding the subcommand (e.g. 'git -c user.name=x push'). Additionally, dangerous git env vars (GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_EXEC_PATH, GIT_PROTOCOL_HANDLER_*) are not blocked, and git aliases could bypass the subcommand check entirely.

Fix:
1. Scan all args for subcommands, not just $1
2. Block dangerous git env vars in envfilter.go or the wrapper itself
3. Disable alias expansion (GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 + unset GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL or set to sandbox-safe config)
4. Consider using '--' to separate flags from subcommands

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/gitwrapper.go, cli/internal/sandbox/envfilter.go
close_reason: Closed

title: Validate: Conformance & verification (T4, T3, T9)
description:
## What

Validation gate for conformance & verification bead (syllago-8wtys).

## Validation Checklist

1. **Section structure:** Section 5.2 must have subsections 5.2.1, 5.2.2, 5.2.3. Verify markdown headings.

2. **Baseline preserved:** All existing MUST requirements from original Section 5.2 must appear in 5.2.1. Compare against: verify content_hash, verify meta_hash, MUST reject on hash failure, MUST verify signature, MUST reject on signature failure.

3. **Strict level:** 5.2.2 must contain MUST NOT for accepting content without meta.yaml. Must contain rationale mentioning enterprise and sidecar stripping.

4. **Permissive level:** 5.2.3 must use SHOULD/SHOULD NOT language. Must contain MUST for indicating provenance status in displays. Must contain rationale mentioning adoption and practicality.

5. **No weakening:** The baseline MUST reject requirements must not be conditional or weakened. Grep for "MUST reject" in Section 5 — must appear at least twice (hash failure, signature failure).

6. **Reject note:** Non-normative note about reject UX must exist. Must say "does not prescribe the user experience" or equivalent.

7. **Identity verification:** Section 9.2 must contain MUST language for identity verification. Must state matching algorithm is implementation-defined.

8. **Section 11.9:** Must exist. Must reference cosign v1.14.0. Must list common matching approaches (exact, prefix, regex).

9. **SSH air-gapped note:** Section 9.3 must mention air-gapped environments and that SSH doesn't require network access.

10. **Consumers document level:** A note recommending consumers document their conformance level must exist.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 10 checks pass
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: 9/10 PASS. Check 7 FAIL is expected — Section 11.9 is a forward reference that will be created by the Security Considerations bead (syllago-7c550). The reference itself is correct.

title: Handle global vs project content name collisions in add
description:
When syllago add --from <provider> discovers content, it merges global and project-level sources. If both have content with the same derived name (e.g., a global hook and project hook both named 'before-tool-execute-shell' because they share the same event+matcher), the global version silently overwrites the project version.

Expected behavior: syllago should either (a) scope names by location (e.g., 'before-tool-execute-shell' vs 'before-tool-execute-shell-project'), (b) prefer project-level over global, or (c) prompt the user to choose. At minimum, warn when a collision occurs.

This affects hooks (derived names from event+matcher) and potentially rules/skills/agents if global and project dirs have same-named items.

Reproduction: Create a project with .claude/settings.json containing a PreToolUse/Bash hook. Also have ~/.claude/settings.json with a PreToolUse/Bash hook. Run syllago add hooks --from claude-code — only the global hook's commands are stored.
close_reason: Already fixed in aa80bec: collision handling via uniqueItemDir + SourceScope metadata check. E2E tests for both hooks (TestAddHooksE2E_BothScopes) and MCP (TestAddMcpE2E_NameCollision, TestAddMcpCollision) exist.

title: Validate: Content hashing updates (T6, T7, T13, I2, I5)
description:
## What

Validation gate for content hashing updates bead (syllago-ghjwx).

## Validation Checklist

1. **VCS exclusions:** Grep for ".git/" in Section 7.5. Must appear in exclusion language with MUST. Also check .svn/, .hg/, CVS/.

2. **No over-exclusion:** Grep for ".DS_Store" and "Thumbs.db" in Section 7.5. They must NOT be in the exclusion list — they should only appear in Security Considerations as a publisher SHOULD warning.

3. **Sensitive files warning:** Security Considerations must contain MUST NOT language about credentials/API keys/.env in content directories.

4. **Integrity precedent:** The rationale for no .acpignore must reference cosign and/or in-toto.

5. **CRLF note:** An implementation note about CRLF must exist. Must contain `.gitattributes` example with `* text=auto eol=lf`. Must reference at least Go modules or Cargo as precedent.

6. **Symlink precision:** Section 7.3 step 1 must contain: "fully resolved absolute target path" and "descendant of the content directory's absolute path." Must address relative symlinks through `..`, directory symlinks, and transitive chains.

7. **published_at constraint:** Section 6.3.10 must reference RFC 3339 (not just ISO 8601). Must require Z suffix (not +00:00). Must require seconds precision.

8. **RFC 3339 reference:** Section 12.1 must contain a normative reference for RFC 3339.

9. **TV-MH unaffected:** TV-MH's published_at value (2026-04-01T14:32:00Z) already conforms to the new constraint. Confirm no test vector changes.

10. **No stale references:** Section 7.5's "No exclusion list is applied" must be removed or rewritten.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 10 checks pass
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: 10/10 checks PASS. VCS exclusions, CRLF note, symlink precision, published_at RFC 3339, and cross-references all verified.

title: ACP Spec: Content hashing updates (T6, T7, T13, I2, I5)
description:
## What

Update Section 7 (Content Hash Algorithm) and Section 6.3.10 for: VCS directory exclusions, CRLF implementation note with rationale, symlink resolution precision, and published_at timestamp format constraint.

## File

docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md

## Changes

### Change 1: VCS directory exclusions (T6 + T7)

In Section 7.5 (Content Normalization Rules, line 279), change:
> "All files in the content directory are included, including hidden files. No exclusion list is applied."

To:
> "All files in the content directory are included, including hidden files, with the following exceptions: version control system directories (`.git/`, `.svn/`, `.hg/`, `CVS/`) MUST be excluded from content hash computation. These directories are never content — they are repository metadata. No other exclusion list is applied."

Add a Security Considerations note (new subsection 11.8 or add to existing):
> "**Sensitive Files in Content Directories:** Publishers MUST NOT include sensitive files (credentials, API keys, `.env` files, private keys) in content directories. All files in the content directory — including hidden files — are included in the content hash and may be redistributed. OS-generated metadata files (`.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`) are included if present; publishers SHOULD remove them before publishing. ACP does not provide an exclusion mechanism beyond VCS directories — consistent with supply chain integrity tools (cosign, in-toto) that deliberately hash what exists rather than filtering during integrity computation."

### Change 2: CRLF implementation note (T13)

Add a new subsection or prominent note after Section 7.5 (or within it). Title: "Implementation Note: Line Endings and Cross-Platform Verification"

Content:
> "Section 5.1 requires publishers to use LF (0x0A) line endings. ACP hashes raw bytes with no line ending normalization — CRLF (0x0D 0x0A) content produces a different hash than LF content. This is by design: every modern supply chain integrity specification (Sigstore DSSE, in-toto, SRI, Go module checksums) hashes raw bytes rather than normalizing, because canonicalization adds attack surface and implementation divergence risk between signers and verifiers.
>
> On Windows, git's `core.autocrlf=true` setting converts LF to CRLF on checkout. Content checked out with CRLF will fail hash verification against hashes computed from LF content. Publishers and consumers on Windows SHOULD configure their content repositories with a `.gitattributes` file:
>
> ```
> * text=auto eol=lf
> ```
>
> This ensures consistent LF line endings across all platforms. Go modules, Cargo, and npm all rely on the same convention — LF in storage, platform-appropriate in working tree — with `.gitattributes` as the standard cross-platform solution."

### Change 3: Symlink resolution precision (I5)

In Section 7.3 step 1 (line 256), change:
> "Resolve symlinks whose target is within the content directory"

To:
> "Resolve symlinks whose fully resolved absolute target path is a descendant of the content directory's absolute path. Relative symlinks using `..` that resolve back inside the content directory are valid. Symlinks to directories MUST be recursively enumerated as if the directory contents existed at the symlink's path. Transitive symlink chains (A → B → C) MUST be fully resolved to the final target."

### Change 4: published_at RFC 3339 constraint (I2)

In Section 6.3.10 (line 186), change:
> "String. ISO 8601 timestamp of publication."

To:
> "String. Timestamp of publication, conforming to RFC 3339 with mandatory UTC offset expressed as `Z` (not `+00:00`) and seconds precision (e.g., `2026-04-01T14:32:00Z`). Millisecond or finer precision MAY be included but MUST NOT affect meta_hash computation when truncated to seconds. This constraint ensures a single canonical representation for identical timestamps, preventing meta_hash divergence from equivalent but differently-formatted timestamps."

Add RFC 3339 to normative references (Section 12.1):
> **[RFC3339]** Klyne, G. and C. Newman, "Date and Time on the Internet: Timestamps", RFC 3339, July 2002. https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3339

## Success Criteria

- [ ] VCS directories (.git/, .svn/, .hg/, CVS/) explicitly excluded in Section 7.5
- [ ] No other exclusions added — only VCS dirs
- [ ] Sensitive files warning exists in Security Considerations with MUST NOT language
- [ ] .DS_Store and Thumbs.db mentioned as publisher responsibility (SHOULD remove)
- [ ] Rationale references cosign and in-toto precedent for no-exclusion design
- [ ] CRLF implementation note present with .gitattributes example
- [ ] CRLF note references Go modules, Cargo, npm as precedent
- [ ] Symlink resolution uses "fully resolved absolute target path" + "descendant of" language
- [ ] Relative symlinks through .. are addressed
- [ ] Directory symlinks addressed (recursive enumeration)
- [ ] Transitive chains addressed (full resolution)
- [ ] published_at constrained to RFC 3339 with mandatory Z suffix and seconds precision
- [ ] RFC 3339 added to normative references
- [ ] TV-MH example's published_at (2026-04-01T14:32:00Z) already conforms — no TV changes needed
close_reason: VCS dirs excluded (.git/.svn/.hg/CVS), CRLF implementation note (Section 7.6) with .gitattributes example and ecosystem rationale, symlink resolution precision (absolute paths, relative .., directory symlinks, transitive chains), published_at constrained to RFC 3339 Z-suffix seconds precision, RFC 3339 added to normative references.

title: Verify Homebrew tap Aembit credential delivery on first release
description:
## Why
The release.yml workflow was updated to fetch HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN from Aembit instead of GitHub secrets, but it can only be tested by pushing a v* tag (which creates a real release). The Claude workflow was tested and confirmed working — same trust provider, same pattern — but the Homebrew credential uses a different server workload (github.com vs api.anthropic.com).

## What
Verify that the Aembit credential delivery works for the Homebrew tap update on the first real release after going public.

## How
1. On the first release (v* tag push), monitor the release workflow run
2. Check that the "Get Homebrew tap credentials from Aembit" step succeeds
3. Check that the "Update Homebrew formula" step pushes to the tap repo
4. Verify in Aembit dashboard that the credential access event was logged
5. If it fails: the step has continue-on-error so the release itself won't break. Fix the Aembit config and retry manually.

## Success Criteria
- Release workflow completes with Homebrew formula updated
- Aembit dashboard shows credential access for github.com server workload
- HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret can be deleted from GitHub

## Blocked By
First release after going public

title: Implement MCP config discovery and add pathway
description:
## Problem
`syllago add mcp --from claude-code` produces zero output and adds nothing. The MCP content type has no add/discovery implementation — it was never built. The code skips MCP in both discovery and add paths.

## Root Cause Analysis
1. **Discovery (`runAddDiscovery`, add_cmd.go:384):** Only calls `discoverHooksForDisplay()` to merge settings.json content. No equivalent `discoverMcpForDisplay()` exists. MCP configs are invisible in discovery.
2. **Add (`runAdd`, add_cmd.go:154):** Special-case branch only handles `catalog.Hooks`. No `catalog.MCP` branch. Falls through to file-based `DiscoverFromProvider`.
3. **`DiscoverFromProvider` (add.go:479):** Explicitly skips MCP: `if ct == catalog.Hooks || ct == catalog.MCP { continue }`.
4. **`--all` path (add_cmd.go:154):** Enters the hooks block for `--all` but inner condition only matches hooks, so MCP is never handled.

## MCP Config Location Matrix (per provider)
MCP configs live in DIFFERENT files per provider. Discovery must check all sources:

| Provider | MCP Files | JSON Key |
|----------|-----------|----------|
| claude-code | settings.json (global+project), ~/.claude.json, .mcp.json | mcpServers |
| gemini-cli | settings.json | mcpServers |
| copilot-cli | .copilot/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| kiro | .kiro/settings/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| opencode | opencode.json | mcp |
| zed | settings.json | context_servers |
| cline | VS Code settings path | mcpServers |
| roo-code | .roo/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| cursor | .cursor/mcp.json | mcpServers |
| windsurf | mcp_config.json | mcpServers |
| amp | settings.json | amp.mcpServers |

## Implementation Plan

### 1. New `FindMCPLocations` function (installer/scope.go or installer/mcp.go)
Returns all files where MCP configs exist for a provider, with scope labels:
- For settings.json-based providers: use `FindSettingsLocationsWithBase` + extract mcpServers key
- For dedicated-file providers: use `mcpConfigPath` 
- For claude-code: BOTH settings.json files AND dedicated files (~/.claude.json, .mcp.json)
- Returns []MCPLocation with Path, Scope (global/project), ProjectName fields

### 2. New `discoverMcpForDisplay` function (add_cmd.go)
Mirrors `discoverHooksForDisplay`:
- Calls `FindMCPLocations` for the provider
- Reads each file, extracts individual server entries using `installer.MCPConfigKey` + gjson
- Returns []add.DiscoveryItem with scope metadata
- No dedup — shows all scopes

### 3. New `runAddMcp` function (add_cmd.go)
Mirrors `runAddHooks`:
- Finds MCP locations, reads configs, extracts servers
- Writes each server to library: `~/.syllago/content/mcp/<provider>/<server-name>/config.json`
- config.json format: `{"mcpServers": {"name": {...}}}` wrapper (matches installer expectations)
- Writes metadata with SourceScope and SourceProject
- Collision handling: suffix with -2, -3 on name conflicts
- Respects --scope, --exclude, --force, --dry-run flags

### 4. Wire into existing code paths
- `runAddDiscovery`: add `discoverMcpForDisplay` call alongside `discoverHooksForDisplay`
- `runAdd`: add `typeStr == string(catalog.MCP)` branch alongside hooks
- `--all` path: ensure MCP is included when adding all content

## Existing Infrastructure to Reuse
- `installer.MCPConfigKey(prov)` — returns the JSON key per provider
- `installer.ExtractServerEntries(data, name, key)` — parses both nested and flat MCP formats
- `installer.mcpConfigPath(prov, repoRoot)` — returns dedicated MCP file path per provider  
- `installer.FindSettingsLocationsWithBase(prov, root, baseDir)` — finds settings.json files
- `converter.StripJSONCComments(data)` — needed for OpenCode JSONC format

## Files to Modify
- `cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go` — new functions + wiring
- `cli/internal/installer/mcp.go` or `cli/internal/installer/scope.go` — FindMCPLocations
- `cli/internal/metadata/metadata.go` — SourceScope + SourceProject (shared with hooks bead)

## Testing
- Unit test: `discoverMcpForDisplay` with claude-code provider having MCP in settings.json — verify servers found
- Unit test: `discoverMcpForDisplay` with dedicated-file provider (e.g., roo-code) — verify servers found
- Unit test: `runAddMcp` writes correct library structure (config.json + metadata)
- Unit test: name collision between global and project MCP servers — verify suffix + metadata
- Unit test: `--all` flag includes MCP servers alongside other content types
- Unit test: `--exclude` flag filters out named MCP servers
- Unit test: discovery mode shows MCP servers with scope labels
- Manual test: `syllago add mcp --from claude-code` in ~/demo-project — should find obsidian from project settings.json
- Manual test: `syllago add --from claude-code` — should show MCP servers in discovery output

## Success Criteria
- `syllago add --from claude-code` (discovery) shows MCP servers from all locations with scope labels
- `syllago add mcp --from claude-code` adds MCP servers to library with correct config.json format
- `syllago add --all --from claude-code` includes MCP servers
- MCP servers from different scopes are individually identifiable via metadata
- Name collisions handled gracefully with suffix
- All existing tests pass
- New tests cover MCP discovery, add, collision, and multi-provider scenarios
- Works for all providers in the matrix (at least claude-code, gemini-cli, and one dedicated-file provider tested)
close_reason: All 8 criteria now pass including test coverage. 5 new tests: TestAddMcpDiscovery, TestAddMcpWritesToGlobalDir, TestAddMcpScopeMetadata, TestAddMcpCollision, TestAddMcpDryRun. Plus 4 FindMCPLocations tests in installer. Full suite passes.

title: Task 1.2: Add content type selection step to wizard
description:
# Add Content Type Selection Step

## Why
Before walking through individual content types, users should select which types they want in their loadout. This reduces friction — most users want all types (default checked), power users can uncheck types they don't need.

## Description
New step inserted between provider selection and per-type item selection. Shows checkboxes for each content type supported by the selected provider, defaulting all to checked. Only types with available items in the catalog are shown.

Microcopy: "Uncheck any content types you want to skip."

Keys:
- Space: toggle individual checkbox
- a: Select All / Deselect All toggle
- Enter: proceed to first per-type item step
- Esc: go back to provider step

Mouse: clicking checkbox or type label toggles it.

## Success Criteria
- After selecting a provider, checkbox screen shows all supported content types with available items, all checked by default
- Space toggles individual, a toggles all
- Enter proceeds, Esc goes back
- Selecting 0 types + Enter shows "Select at least one content type"
- Mouse clicks toggle checkboxes
- Modal fits at 60x20 terminal (height capped to min(24, termHeight-2))

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: given provider supporting Rules/Skills/Agents but catalog has only Rules and Skills, verify only Rules and Skills shown, both checked
2. Unit test: a key toggles all on/off
3. Unit test: 0 types + Enter shows validation error
4. Unit test: Space toggles individual type
5. Unit test: Esc goes back to provider step
6. Unit test: mouse click on checkbox toggles it
7. Golden file: wizard at content type selection step (80x30)
8. Golden file: wizard at content type selection step (60x20) — verify modal height cap

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go (new step in wizard)
- cli/internal/tui/keys.go (if new bindings needed)

title: L6: Hook script symlink not resolved before containment check
description:
## Why
resolveHookScripts() uses filepath.Join + filepath.Rel to check that script paths stay within the item directory, but doesn't resolve symlinks first. A symlink in the item directory pointing outside could pass the string-based containment check but read from an arbitrary location.

## What
Add filepath.EvalSymlinks before the containment check in resolveHookScripts.

## How (general)
- After filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(itemDir, scriptPath)), call filepath.EvalSymlinks
- Then run the filepath.Rel containment check against the resolved path
- This matches the pattern used in dirsafety.go:49

## Success Criteria
- Symlinks pointing outside the item directory are rejected
- Normal relative script paths still resolve correctly

## Testing
- Unit test: script path is a regular file within item dir — succeeds
- Unit test: script path is a symlink to file outside item dir — rejected
- Unit test: script path with .. that resolves within item dir — succeeds

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L6
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

What to write: Table-driven tests (TestDeduplicateItems):
- Hook-script item with path in another item's Scripts list → suppressed
- Hook-script item NOT in any Scripts list → not suppressed
- Two items same (type, name, hash), lower confidence first → keep higher confidence, alias recorded
- Two items same (type, name, hash), equal confidence, syllago vs. claude-code → syllago wins (Decision #19)
- Two items same (type, name, hash), equal confidence, claude-code vs. top-level → claude-code wins
- Two items same (type, name), different hash → both returned as conflict pair
- Cross-type same-name items are NOT deduped (skill foo and rule foo are separate)
- Empty input → empty output
