title: Shared + per-provider override pattern for canonical hooks
description:
Consider adopting a pattern where the canonical hook config has shared hooks AND per-provider override sections.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Issue #1259 shows their per-provider frontmatter overrides DON'T always work — Google Antigravity trigger declarations are silently ignored. Issue #1181 shows opt-in/opt-out granularity is insufficient — Copilot always gets agent/runSubagent injected with no way to disable it.

SYLLAGO APPROACH: Don't copy rulesync's approach directly. Their overrides are scattered across frontmatter in individual files, making it hard to debug when they fail silently. Instead, consider a centralized override mechanism:

Option A: Per-provider sections in the canonical hook config (rulesync's pattern but centralized)
  { "hooks": {...}, "overrides": { "claude-code": {...}, "cursor": {...} } }

Option B: Provider-scoped hooks in loadout manifests (syllago-native)
  Since loadouts already target specific providers, the loadout manifest could declare provider-specific hook variations. This keeps the canonical format clean and moves provider concerns to the distribution layer.

Option C: Defer this entirely
  The spec defines canonical hooks. If a provider can't handle a hook, the adapter drops it with a warning. No override mechanism needed — users create provider-specific content when needed.

RECOMMENDATION: Start with Option C (simplest). Add overrides only when real user demand emerges. rulesync's issues show that override mechanisms create their own bugs.

title: Audit pull_request_target usage in pr-policy.yml
description:
## Why
pr-policy.yml uses pull_request_target which runs with repo write permissions against the base branch. Currently safe — only runs the vouch check action, doesn't checkout PR head code for execution. But once public, external contributors' PRs will trigger this workflow with write access to the repo.

## What
Verify the workflow remains safe for external PRs, or switch to pull_request trigger if possible.

## How
- Review: the current workflow only runs mitchellh/vouch/action/check-pr which reads PR metadata. It does NOT checkout or execute PR code. This is safe.
- The checkout step checks out the base branch (default for pull_request_target), not the PR head. Safe.
- Document this decision so future modifications don't accidentally introduce a vulnerability
- Consider adding a comment in the workflow file explaining why pull_request_target is used and the safety constraints

## Success Criteria
- Workflow is documented as safe for external contributors
- No code from the PR head is executed in the pull_request_target context

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for external contributor PRs to test this

title: Task 1.1: Diagnose and fix loadout creation bug
description:
# Diagnose and Fix Loadout Creation Bug

## Why
Loadouts created via the wizard don't appear in the TUI after creation. This is the most basic prerequisite — the feature is broken.

## Description
The doCreateLoadoutMsg handler (app.go:835) rescans the catalog and refreshes sidebar counts, but the loadout still doesn't show. Possible causes:
- Catalog scanner not finding the new loadout directory
- Path mismatch (writing to a location the scanner doesn't search)
- Sidebar/card refresh issue after catalog rescan

## Success Criteria
- Create a loadout via the wizard -> it appears immediately in the Loadouts card view
- Sidebar count updates to reflect the new loadout
- Works for both Project and Library destinations
- Toast shows success message with loadout name

## Test-Driven Development
Write tests FIRST, then fix:
1. Unit test: create loadout via doCreateLoadout() with a temp catalog, verify doCreateLoadoutMsg triggers catalog rescan that includes the new loadout
2. Integration test: full wizard flow (provider -> items -> name -> dest -> confirm) -> verify loadout appears in rendered output
3. Verify golden files for loadout cards view still pass

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (lines 345-410, 835-846)
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go
close_reason: Bug diagnosed and fixed. The handler code at app.go:932 was already correct (rescan + navigate to detail). Fixed Library destination path to use catalog.GlobalContentDir() instead of hardcoded os.UserHomeDir() path (single source of truth). Added 2 integration tests: TestCreateLoadoutHandlerNavigatesToDetail (full handler flow verifying detail nav, toast, sidebar count) and TestCreateLoadoutLibraryDestination (Library dest via GlobalContentDirOverride). All tests pass.

title: Preserve updatedInput and structured output fields in hook conversion
description:
WHY: Claude Code hooks can return structured JSON output including updatedInput (transparent arg rewriting), suppressOutput, systemMessage, additionalContext, continue, and permissionDecision. These fields are critical for security hooks. Our converter preserves the hook definition but doesn't model or warn about these runtime capabilities.

RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/audit/hooks.md (lines 30-31 — structured output fields)
RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/claude-code/hooks.md (exit codes and structured output)
RELATED: syllago-qumq (hook matcher regex handling) — could be combined

WHAT TO DO:
- Document which structured output fields each provider supports
- During hook conversion, if a hook's purpose implies arg rewriting (e.g., command rewrites tool input), warn when exporting to providers that don't support updatedInput
- Consider adding a 'capabilities' annotation to canonical hook entries

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Converter warns about capability mismatches for structured output
- Documentation of per-provider structured output support

TESTING: Unit tests for warning conditions
close_reason: HookOutputCapabilities map for 6 providers. SourceProvider propagation in canonical. OutputFieldsLostWarnings generates mismatch warnings. 8 new tests. All tests pass.

title: M1: Registry sync missing git hardening
description:
## Why
registry.Sync() runs git pull --ff-only without the security hardening that Clone() applies. Missing: GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1, core.hooksPath=/dev/null, --no-recurse-submodules. A registry could add a post-merge git hook in an update — on the next sync, that hook executes with the user's permissions. This is a code execution vulnerability triggered by normal usage.

## What
Apply identical git hardening to Sync() as Clone() already uses.

## How (general)
- In registry.go Sync(), add GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 to cmd.Env
- Add -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null before the pull subcommand
- Add --no-recurse-submodules flag
- Consider extracting a helper function that applies hardening to any git command

## Success Criteria
- Sync() applies the same env vars and config flags as Clone()
- Post-merge hooks in registries do NOT execute during sync
- Submodules are not fetched during sync

## Testing
- Unit test: create a test registry with a post-merge hook, verify it doesn't execute on sync
- Unit test: verify GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 is set in Sync() command environment
- Regression test: normal sync still works with hardening applied

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M1
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Shared hook converter config pattern
description:
Refactor hook adapters so most providers use a shared converter with a config struct rather than each adapter implementing full conversion logic independently.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Their shared converter (tool-hooks-converter.ts) works well for Claude Code and Factory Droid, but Cursor, Gemini, OpenCode, and Copilot each needed custom implementations anyway. Issue #1250 shows the shared converter's command prefixing logic had bugs with bare executables (npx, prettier, etc.).

SYLLAGO APPROACH: Don't over-abstract. The shared converter pattern is valuable but the config struct should be SIMPLER than rulesync's. Focus on what's actually shared:

Shared behavior (put in common converter):
- Event name lookup (canonical <-> provider)
- Timeout unit conversion (spec seconds -> provider milliseconds)
- Basic command validation

Provider-specific behavior (keep in adapters):
- Output format (JSON settings merge, standalone file, JS plugin)
- Path prefixing logic (each provider has different project dir vars and rules)
- Matcher support/stripping (varies per provider and per event)

The lesson from rulesync #1250: command prefixing is harder than it looks. Don't try to share path resolution logic — let each adapter handle its own paths. Share only the stateless mapping tables.

title: Install/uninstall individual MCP servers
description:
## Context

Depends on: syllago-8ihnl (explode MCP configs into per-server items)

Currently installMCP/uninstallMCP in installer/mcp.go operate on ALL server entries in a config.json at once. After syllago-8ihnl, each ContentItem represents a single server (via item.ServerKey), so install/uninstall must operate on just that one server.

## Provider-Specific MCP Architecture (DO NOT CHANGE)

The existing provider infrastructure handles all the complexity of where and how MCP configs are stored. This code stays untouched — the per-server change only narrows WHICH entries get merged/removed within the existing flow.

### Config file locations (mcpConfigPath, line 54-82)

| Provider | Config File Path | Scope |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| claude-code | ~/.claude.json | Global |
| claude-code | .mcp.json (project root) | Project (also checked by FindMCPLocations) |
| gemini-cli | ~/.config/gemini-cli/settings.json | Global |
| copilot-cli | .copilot/mcp.json (project root) | Project |
| kiro | .kiro/settings/mcp.json (project root) | Project |
| opencode | opencode.json (project root) | Project |
| zed | ~/.config/zed/settings.json | Global |
| cline | VS Code extension path (dynamic) | Global |
| roo-code | .roo/mcp.json (project root) | Project |

### JSON keys (MCPConfigKey + FindMCPLocations overrides)

| Provider | JSON key for server entries |
|----------|---------------------------|
| Most providers | "mcpServers" |
| Zed | "context_servers" |
| OpenCode | "mcp" |
| Amp | "amp.mcpServers" |

### Special handling

- **OpenCode**: JSONC comment stripping via converter.StripJSONCComments before parsing (readMCPConfig, line 200-208)
- **Claude Code**: FindMCPLocations checks THREE locations: settings.json files (global+project), ~/.claude.json, and .mcp.json
- **Provider config files may contain OTHER data** (e.g., Zed's settings.json has editor settings alongside context_servers; Claude Code's .claude.json has permissions alongside mcpServers). Install/uninstall must only touch the server key, not other content.

### Existing functions that stay unchanged

| Function | Purpose | Why unchanged |
|----------|---------|--------------|
| mcpConfigPath() | Returns target config file path per provider | Path resolution is independent of server count |
| MCPConfigKey() | Returns JSON key per provider | Key is the same whether installing 1 or N servers |
| readMCPConfig() | Reads + optionally strips JSONC | Reading is the same |
| ExtractServerEntries() | Parses config.json into server map | Still needed to get the specific server's config data |
| FindMCPLocations() | Finds all files where MCP configs exist | Used by TUI for status display, unaffected |
| backupFile() | Backs up before modification | Same flow |
| writeJSONFile() | Writes result | Same flow |

## What Changes

### 1. installMCP (line 260-324)

**Current**: Calls ExtractServerEntries → iterates ALL entries → merges ALL into provider config → records one InstalledMCP with all ServerNames.

**New**: Calls ExtractServerEntries → filters to ONLY item.ServerKey → merges that one entry → records one InstalledMCP with that single server name.

```go
// Current (installs all):
entries, err := ExtractServerEntries(rawData, item.Name, jsonKey)
for name, configData := range entries { ... }

// New (installs one):
entries, err := ExtractServerEntries(rawData, item.Name, jsonKey)
serverKey := item.ServerKey
if serverKey == "" {
    serverKey = item.Name // backward compat for flat format
}
configData, ok := entries[serverKey]
if !ok {
    return "", fmt.Errorf("server %q not found in config.json", serverKey)
}
// merge only configData for serverKey
```

**Backward compat**: If item.ServerKey is empty (flat format items, or items from before this change), fall back to installing all entries (current behavior).

### 2. uninstallMCP (line 326-381)

**Current**: Finds InstalledMCP by item.Name → removes ALL tracked ServerNames from provider config → removes the InstalledMCP entry.

**New**: Finds InstalledMCP by item.Name AND ServerKey → removes only that server key → removes that specific InstalledMCP entry.

### 3. checkMCPStatus (line 383-420)

**Current**: Checks if ANY tracked server name exists in provider config.

**New**: When item.ServerKey is set, check if THAT specific server key exists. Fallback to current behavior when ServerKey is empty.

### 4. InstalledMCP struct (installed.go, line 26-31)

Add ServerKey field:
```go
type InstalledMCP struct {
    Name        string    `json:"name"`                  // content item directory name (provenance)
    ServerKey   string    `json:"serverKey,omitempty"`    // specific server key installed
    ServerNames []string  `json:"serverNames,omitempty"`  // DEPRECATED: kept for backward compat
    Source      string    `json:"source"`
    InstalledAt time.Time `json:"installedAt"`
}
```

New installs populate ServerKey (single string). Old installs still have ServerNames (slice). Uninstall must handle both formats.

### 5. FindMCP (installed.go, line 96-104)

**Current**: Matches by Name only.

**New**: Add FindMCPByServerKey(name, serverKey) that matches both. Keep FindMCP for backward compat (used by uninstall to find legacy entries).

### 6. ParseMCPServerConfig — new function

```go
func ParseMCPServerConfig(itemPath string, serverKey string) (*MCPConfig, error)
```

Reads config.json, extracts the specific server entry from nested format, returns its MCPConfig. Falls back to flat parsing when serverKey is empty. This is used by the TUI detail view to show the correct server's command/url/env.

## Files to modify

- `cli/internal/installer/mcp.go` — installMCP, uninstallMCP, checkMCPStatus, add ParseMCPServerConfig
- `cli/internal/installer/installed.go` — InstalledMCP struct (add ServerKey), add FindMCPByServerKey
- `cli/internal/installer/mcp_test.go` — new tests + update existing
- `cli/internal/installer/installed_test.go` — test FindMCPByServerKey

## Testing

- Unit test: install single server from nested config (2+ servers) → only that server appears in provider config, others absent
- Unit test: install two servers independently from same config → both tracked as separate InstalledMCP entries
- Unit test: uninstall one server → other server remains in provider config and in installed.json
- Unit test: checkMCPStatus returns StatusInstalled for installed server, StatusNotInstalled for non-installed server from same config
- Unit test: ParseMCPServerConfig extracts correct MCPConfig for given key (command, args, env, url fields)
- Unit test: backward compat — empty ServerKey installs all entries (current behavior preserved)
- Unit test: backward compat — uninstall works for old InstalledMCP entries with ServerNames but no ServerKey
- Unit test: provider-specific JSON keys work correctly (test with "mcpServers" and "context_servers" at minimum)
- Integration test: install server A from config X, install server B from config X, uninstall A → B still present in provider config
- Existing MCP install/uninstall/status tests still pass

## Success criteria

- Individual servers can be installed and uninstalled independently
- installed.json tracks each server with its own InstalledMCP entry (using ServerKey field)
- No regression for flat-format MCP configs (single server, no wrapper)
- Backward compatible with existing installed.json entries (old ServerNames format still uninstalls correctly)
- Provider-specific config paths, JSON keys, and special handling (JSONC stripping, multi-location) are completely untouched
- ParseMCPServerConfig can extract config for any individual server for TUI preview
close_reason: Implemented and verified. installMCP/uninstallMCP/checkMCPStatus support per-server operations via item.ServerKey. ParseMCPServerConfig added. InstalledMCP has ServerKey field. FindMCPByServerKey added. All backward compat preserved. 5 new test functions.

title: Validate: Schema constraints & field rules (T5, T10, T12)
description:
## What

Validation gate for schema constraints bead (syllago-2tdz1).

## Validation Checklist

1. **Name constraints exist:** Section 6.3.3 must contain: character count limit (1-128), allowed character set, forbidden control characters with Unicode ranges, forbidden path separators.

2. **Schema example still valid:** The example name "code-review" in Section 6.2 must conform to the new constraints (only letters, digits, hyphens — it does).

3. **Authors note:** Section 6.3.6 must contain an informative note about non-normalization. Must show an example of different casing producing different meta_hash.

4. **Version note:** Section 6.3.4 must contain an informative note explaining integer versioning over SemVer. Must mention provenance vs package management distinction.

5. **Version behavior unchanged:** Section 6.3.4 must still say integer, starts at 1, resets on derivation. No behavioral changes.

6. **No over-constraining:** The name constraints must allow Unicode letters (not just ASCII). Names like "コードレビュー" or "révision-code" must be valid per the constraint text.

7. **Consistency:** All new notes use the "(Informative)" label consistently with other notes in Section 6.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 7 checks pass
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: 7/7 checks PASS. Name constraints, authors note, version note all verified.

title: L1: Symlink creation TOCTOU race in CreateSymlink
description:
## Why
CreateSymlink checks if the target exists with Lstat, removes it, then creates a new symlink. Between Remove and Symlink, another process could place a symlink at the target path. Requires attacker write access to provider config dir (low practical risk).

## What
Use atomic symlink replacement pattern (create temp symlink, then rename).

## How (general)
- Create a temporary symlink with a random name in the same directory
- Use os.Rename to atomically replace the target with the temp symlink
- os.Rename atomically replaces the target, closing the TOCTOU window

## Success Criteria
- Symlink creation is atomic (no window between remove and create)
- Existing symlinks are replaced without race condition

## Testing
- Unit test: concurrent symlink creation doesn't produce errors
- Existing symlink tests continue to pass

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L1
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Phase 2: Security Hardening
close_reason: All 12 Phase 2 security hardening tasks completed

title: Automated provider compatibility monitoring
description:
Detect when AI coding tool providers change their configuration formats, hook events, MCP schemas, or content type support. Currently syllago relies on manual discovery. Automate this.

DESIGN: Two-tier monitoring — changelogs/releases for early signal, docs for format details.

## Tier 1: Changelog/Release Monitoring (Early Signal)

Changelogs surface changes before docs are updated. Monitor these for keywords like "hooks", "mcp", "rules", "config", "settings", "breaking".

| Provider | Changelog Source | Type | Fetchable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog | Hosted docs | Yes (scrape) |
| Cursor | https://www.cursor.com/changelog | Hosted | Yes (scrape) |
| Gemini CLI | https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Copilot CLI | https://github.com/github/docs (PRs to copilot/) | GitHub repo | Yes (API) |
| Windsurf | https://docs.windsurf.com (check for changelog) | Hosted docs | Yes (llms.txt) |
| Cline | https://github.com/cline/cline/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Roo Code | https://github.com/RooVetGit/Roo-Code/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| OpenCode | https://github.com/opencode-ai/opencode/releases | GitHub releases | Yes (API) |
| Codex | https://developers.openai.com/codex (check for changelog) | Hosted docs | Yes (scrape) |
| Kiro | https://kiro.dev/docs/ (check for changelog/what's new) | Hosted docs | Unstable |
| Amp | https://ampcode.com/news | Blog/news | Yes (scrape) |
| Zed | https://zed.dev/releases | Hosted releases | Yes (scrape) |

## Tier 2: Doc Page Monitoring (Format Details)

After changelog flags a change, diff the actual format docs to understand what changed.

BEST RAW SOURCES (easiest to diff programmatically):
- Gemini CLI: raw.githubusercontent.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/main/docs/*.md (BEST — raw markdown)
- Copilot CLI: github.com/github/docs/tree/main/content/copilot/ (raw markdown in public repo)
- Windsurf: docs.windsurf.com/llms.txt → *.md raw URLs (BEST — llms.txt index + raw markdown)
- OpenCode: opencode.ai/config.json (JSON Schema — machine-readable config format)
- Codex: developers.openai.com/codex/* (stable versioned docs)
- Zed: zed.dev/docs/ai/*.html (stable versioned docs)

HARDER TO DIFF (SPA or no raw source):
- Claude Code: code.claude.com/docs/en/* (no raw markdown, llms.txt available as index)
- Cursor: cursor.com/docs/* (SPA, no raw source)
- Cline: docs.cline.bot/* (no raw API found)
- Roo Code: docs.roocode.com/* (Docusaurus, check for docs repo)
- Kiro: kiro.dev/docs/* (no versioning, unstable)
- Amp: ampcode.com/manual/* (some pages need auth)

## Per-Provider Doc URLs (Format References)

### Claude Code (code.claude.com)
- Rules: /docs/en/memory, /docs/en/claude-directory
- Hooks: /docs/en/hooks (26 events incl WorktreeCreate, TeammateIdle, Elicitation)
- MCP: /docs/en/mcp
- Skills: /docs/en/skills
- Agents: /docs/en/sub-agents
- Commands: /docs/en/commands
- Settings: /docs/en/settings

### Cursor (cursor.com)
- Rules: /docs/rules (.mdc with frontmatter)
- Hooks: /docs/hooks (20 events — most of any provider)
- MCP: /docs/mcp (uses ${env:VAR} format)

### Gemini CLI (GitHub + geminicli.com)
- Config: github docs/reference/configuration.md
- Hooks: github docs/hooks/index.md (11 events)
- MCP: github docs/tools/mcp-server.md

### Copilot CLI (docs.github.com)
- Instructions: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/add-custom-instructions
- Hooks: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/use-hooks (6 events)
- MCP: /en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/add-mcp-servers

### Windsurf (docs.windsurf.com)
- Rules: /windsurf/cascade/memories
- Hooks: /windsurf/cascade/hooks (12 events)
- MCP: /windsurf/cascade/mcp
- Skills: /windsurf/cascade/skills

### Cline (docs.cline.bot)
- Rules: /customization/cline-rules
- MCP: /mcp/configuring-mcp-servers
- No hooks, no skills

### Roo Code (docs.roocode.com)
- Rules: /features/custom-instructions
- Modes: /features/custom-modes
- MCP: /features/mcp/using-mcp-in-roo
- No hooks, no skills

### OpenCode (opencode.ai)
- Config: /docs/config/ + config.json (JSON Schema)
- Hooks/Plugins: /docs/plugins/ (25+ events via JS plugins)
- MCP: /docs/mcp-servers/
- Agents: /docs/agents/

### Codex (developers.openai.com)
- AGENTS.md: /codex/guides/agents-md
- Config: /codex/config-reference (TOML)
- Hooks: /codex/hooks (5 events, experimental)
- MCP: /codex/mcp
- Skills: /codex/skills

### Kiro (kiro.dev)
- Steering: /docs/steering/
- Hooks: /docs/hooks/ (10 events)
- MCP: /docs/mcp/configuration/

### Amp (ampcode.com)
- Manual: /manual (main reference)
- MCP: /news/lazy-load-mcp-with-skills, /news/mcp-permissions
- No hooks (has "checks" for code review)

### Zed (zed.dev)
- Rules: /docs/ai/rules
- MCP: /docs/ai/mcp (context_servers)
- Config: /docs/ai/configuration
- No hooks, no skills

## Implementation Sketch

1. Tracking file: .provider-monitor/baselines.json — stores per-URL content hash + last-checked timestamp
2. Script: provider-monitor.sh or Go command
   a. For each provider, fetch changelog/release page
   b. Hash content, compare to baseline
   c. If changed: extract diff, keyword-scan for relevant terms
   d. Create GH issue tagged "provider-change" with diff summary
   e. Update baseline
3. Trigger: GitHub Action on release (workflow_dispatch) or weekly cron
4. Human reviews issue, updates syllago if needed

KEY INSIGHT: Changelogs are the early warning system. Docs are the reference for implementation. Monitor changelogs frequently (weekly?), diff docs only when changelog flags a change.

NOTE: Docs lag behind code. A changelog entry like "Added WorktreeCreate hook event" may appear weeks before the docs page is updated. The monitoring system should flag changelog mentions even if the docs haven't changed yet.

title: Task 2.5: Add pane navigation for split view
description:
# Pane Navigation for Split View

## Why
Users need to navigate between file tree and preview panes. Tab behavior must stay consistent with the rest of the app (sidebar/content toggle).

## Description
Navigation model:
- Tab / Shift+Tab: toggles sidebar and content area (consistent globally)
- l or Right: from file tree, move focus to preview pane
- h or Left: from preview pane, move focus back to tree
- 1 / 2: switch tabs (Files | Install) from anywhere in content
- Arrow keys: navigate within focused pane (Up/Down for tree cursor or preview scroll)

### Focus State
Zone focus lives on detailModel (not App):
```go
type detailZone int
const (
    zoneFileTree detailZone = iota
    zonePreview
)
```
App decides sidebar vs content vs modal. detailModel decides which pane.

### Focus Indicator
Pane title in accent color when focused, muted when unfocused. No full border (saves width).

### Behavior
- Default focus on file tree when entering detail view
- Switching tabs resets focus to file tree
- Tab to sidebar, Tab back lands on last-focused pane
- Help bar: "l/right preview . h/left tree . 1/2 tabs . tab sidebar"

## Success Criteria
- Tab toggles sidebar/content (same as all other pages)
- l/Right moves tree -> preview, h/Left moves preview -> tree
- 1/2 switch tabs from anywhere
- Focus indicator visible (accent vs muted pane title)
- Help bar shows pane navigation keys
- Default focus on file tree on enter
- Focus resets on tab switch

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Tab toggles sidebar/content (NOT pane cycling)
2. Unit test: l from tree focuses preview, h from preview focuses tree
3. Unit test: 1/2 switch tabs
4. Unit test: arrows work within focused pane only
5. Unit test: entering detail focuses file tree
6. Unit test: switching tabs resets focus to tree
7. Golden file: tree focused vs preview focused

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go (detailZone, focus routing)
- cli/internal/tui/keys.go (new bindings if needed)
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (focus indicator rendering)
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

title: Hook import ignores project-level settings.json
description:
syllago add hooks --from claude-code only reads the global ~/.claude/settings.json, ignoring project-level .claude/settings.json hooks. Discovery (syllago add --from claude-code) correctly finds project-level hooks, but the actual add pathway skips them.

This is a duplicate/escalation of syllago-ydai (previously P2), escalating to P1 because it blocks the demo and means project-specific hooks cannot be imported.

Reproduction:
1. Create project with .claude/settings.json containing hooks
2. Run: syllago add hooks --from claude-code  
3. Result: only global hooks are added, project hooks are silently ignored
4. Run: syllago add --from claude-code (discovery)
5. Result: project hooks ARE shown in discovery but cannot be added
close_reason: Superseded by syllago-1srjf which has full implementation plan

title: Skills: Kiro canonicalize + render (frontmatter support)
description:
## Problem
Kiro adopted Agent Skills standard (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter) in Feb 2026. The converter treats Kiro as plain markdown via canonicalizeSkillFromMarkdown(), stripping any frontmatter. Kiro now supports name, description, license, compatibility, metadata fields. The render side outputs plain markdown with a prose header instead of SKILL.md with frontmatter.

## Required Changes
### Canonicalize
1. Remove kiro from the plain-markdown path in Canonicalize()
2. Route kiro through the standard YAML frontmatter parser (same as claude-code/cursor/etc.)

### Render
1. Create kiroSkillMeta struct with the 5 fields Kiro supports
2. Update renderKiroSkill() to output YAML frontmatter (not plain markdown)
3. Embed CC-specific fields (allowed-tools, context, agent, etc.) as prose notes
4. Keep hook warnings (Kiro supports hooks)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Canonicalizing a Kiro SKILL.md with frontmatter preserves name/description/license/compatibility/metadata
- [ ] renderKiroSkill() outputs YAML frontmatter with supported fields
- [ ] CC-specific fields embedded as prose notes (not lost)
- [ ] Hook warnings still generated
- [ ] Round-trip: Kiro skill → canonical → Kiro preserves supported fields
- [ ] Existing skills tests pass
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Dependencies
Depends on syllago-0tus (add license/compatibility/metadata to SkillMeta) for full field support

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/skills.go` (Canonicalize switch, add kiroSkillMeta, update renderKiroSkill)
- `cli/internal/converter/skills_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/skills-research.md — Issues #1, #2
close_reason: Kiro skills: canonicalize via standard YAML parser (not plain markdown). Render outputs 5-field frontmatter. CC fields as prose notes. 6 new tests. All pass.

title: Task 3.1: Split view for loadout Contents tab
description:
# Loadout Contents Split View

## Why
The Contents tab currently shows a static list of items. Users can't preview what's in a loadout without navigating away. A split view lets users evaluate contents in-place.

## Description
Reuses splitViewModel from Phase 2 (Task 2.4). Left pane shows grouped items, right pane shows preview of selected item's primary file.

### Left Pane
- Items grouped by type with headers: "Rules (2)", "Skills (3)", etc.
- Item names show source: concise-output (acme-registry) or (library)
- Type headers are non-selectable — splitViewModel skips disabled items on cursor movement
- First item selected on entry

### Right Pane
- Preview of highlighted item's primary file (same logic as Phase 2)

title: 5.5b: GREEN — Wire confirm items through handleDiscoveryDone
description:
Update handleDiscoveryDone in cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_update.go to process confirmItems, activate triage step, and set pre-check defaults so tests from 5.5a pass.

## What to do

### Step 1: Add analyzerToast field to addDiscoveryDoneMsg
```go
type addDiscoveryDoneMsg struct {
    seq              int
    items            []addDiscoveryItem
    confirmItems     []addConfirmItem
    analyzerToast    string  // NEW — non-empty when analyzer unavailable
    err              error
    tmpDir           string
    sourceRegistry   string
    sourceVisibility string
}
```

### Step 2: In handleDiscoveryDone, push analyzer toast if present
```go
    if msg.analyzerToast != "" {
        cmds = append(cmds, pushToast(msg.analyzerToast, toastWarn))
    }
```

### Step 3: Process confirm items
```go
    if len(msg.confirmItems) > 0 {
        m.hasTriageStep = true
        m.confirmItems = msg.confirmItems
        m.confirmSelected = make(map[int]bool, len(msg.confirmItems))
        for i, ci := range msg.confirmItems {
            m.confirmSelected[i] = ci.tier == analyzer.TierHigh || ci.tier == analyzer.TierUser
        }
        m.confirmCursor = 0
        m.confirmOffset = 0
        m.confirmFocus = triageZoneItems
    } else {
        m.hasTriageStep = false
        m.confirmItems = nil
        m.confirmSelected = nil
    }
    // Rebuild shell labels now that hasTriageStep is determined
    m.shell.SetSteps(m.buildShellLabels())
    m.updateMaxStep()
```

### Step 4: Update breadcrumb click handler in updateMouse
Replace existing breadcrumb block with stepForShellIndex-based routing:
```go
    if step, ok := m.shell.HandleClick(msg); ok {
        target := m.stepForShellIndex(step)
        if target == addStepExecute { return m, nil }
        if target != m.step && int(target) <= int(m.maxStep) {
            m.step = target
            m.shell.SetActive(step)
            m.reviewAcknowledged = false
        }
        return m, nil
    }
```

## Success criteria
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/tui/... -run TestHandleDiscoveryDone_TriageActivation` -> pass
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/tui/... -run TestHandleDiscoveryDone_NoTriage` -> pass
- `cd cli && go build ./...` -> no errors

title: Post-add success message: poor placement and warning overflow
description:
## Bug
After adding items via provider discovery (e.g., "Add provider → Gemini → add selected"):

1. **Success message placement**: The "Added [item]" message with the full list of added items renders at the very top of the welcome/landing page. When many items are added, this creates a long block of text that pushes the actual content down. It's not obvious and looks bad.

2. **Warning overflow**: After the success message, a large number of warnings scatter across the screen. The warnings are not contained or organized — they just dump all over the visible area.

## Expected
- Success message should be in a scrollable modal or summary panel, not inline at the top of the landing page
- Warnings should be grouped and contained (e.g., collapsible section, or "N warnings" with expand option)
- For bulk adds, a summary ("Added 12 items to library") with optional detail expansion

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — statusMessage / statusWarnings rendering in renderContentWelcome()
- The status message system renders a flat string + flat warning list — doesn't scale to bulk operations

## Context
This is the same statusMessage system we just improved (statusIsErr, keypress clearing). The core rendering works for single-item operations but breaks down for bulk adds with many warnings.
close_reason: Closed

title: Render status messages on registries screen
description:
## Phase 1b: Render status messages on registries screen

### Problem
statusMessage only renders in renderContentWelcome() (the category landing page). Registry add/remove/sync operations happen on screenRegistries, where the user sees zero feedback.

### Implementation
1. Change registries.go View() signature from `View(cursor int)` to `View(cursor int, statusMsg string, statusIsErr bool)`
2. Render status between breadcrumb and card grid:
```go
if statusMsg != "" {
    if statusIsErr {
        s += errorMsgStyle.Render(statusMsg) + "\n\n"
    } else {
        s += successMsgStyle.Render(statusMsg) + "\n\n"
    }
}
```
3. Update call site at app.go (~line 1906) to pass `a.statusMessage, a.statusIsErr`

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/registries.go` — View() method
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — call site (~line 1906)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] View() accepts statusMsg and statusIsErr parameters
- [ ] Error messages render with errorMsgStyle on registries screen
- [ ] Success messages render with successMsgStyle on registries screen
- [ ] Empty statusMsg renders nothing (no blank lines)
- [ ] Call site passes a.statusMessage and a.statusIsErr
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes (golden files may need updating)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not use strings.Builder optimization (deferred to accessibility pass)
- Do not add text-based selection indicators to cards (deferred)

title: Replace GitHub secrets with Aembit secretless credential delivery
description:
## Why
syllago's CI/CD pipeline currently stores two static long-lived secrets as GitHub repo secrets:
- CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (Claude Code Action authentication)
- HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN (Homebrew tap push during release)

Static secrets in GitHub are opaque (no audit trail of which workflow accessed them), can be read by any user with repo write access, and must be manually rotated. Replacing them with Aembit secretless credential delivery adds identity-based access control, centralized audit logging, and policy enforcement — the runner must prove its identity via OIDC before Aembit releases the credential.

This is also a compelling real-world use case for Aembit's GitHub Actions integration that Holden can reference for the company (staff tech writer at Aembit).

## What
Replace both GitHub secrets with Aembit/get-credentials action calls. Zero static secrets in GitHub.

## How (general)

### Aembit Configuration (per secret)
For each credential, create in Aembit:
1. **Client Workload**: GitHub ID Token Repository identifier = `OpenScribbler/syllago`
2. **Trust Provider**: GitHub Action ID Token type, repository match rule = `OpenScribbler/syllago`
3. **Credential Provider**: ApiKey type, storing the credential value
4. **Server Workload**: 
   - Claude: api.anthropic.com:443
   - Homebrew: api.github.com:443
5. **Access Policy**: ties all four together, activated

### Workflow Changes
Both claude.yml and claude-code-review.yml:
```yaml
permissions:
  id-token: write  # already present
# ...
- uses: Aembit/get-credentials@v1
  id: aembit-claude
  with:
    client-id: ${{ vars.AEMBIT_CLIENT_ID_CLAUDE }}
    credential-type: ApiKey
    server-host: api.anthropic.com
- uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@...
  with:
    claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ steps.aembit-claude.outputs.api-key }}
```

release.yml:
```yaml
- uses: Aembit/get-credentials@v1
  id: aembit-homebrew
  with:
    client-id: ${{ vars.AEMBIT_CLIENT_ID_HOMEBREW }}
    credential-type: ApiKey
    server-host: api.github.com
# Then use ${{ steps.aembit-homebrew.outputs.api-key }} where HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN was used
```

Store AEMBIT_CLIENT_ID_CLAUDE and AEMBIT_CLIENT_ID_HOMEBREW as GitHub Actions variables (not secrets — they're not sensitive, just identifiers).

### Pin the Aembit action by SHA
Before using, pin Aembit/get-credentials to a full commit SHA (consistent with all other actions in the repo).

## Success Criteria
- Both CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN and HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN removed from GitHub secrets
- Both workflows use Aembit/get-credentials to fetch credentials at runtime
- Claude Code Action still works on @claude mentions and PR reviews
- Release workflow still pushes Homebrew formula updates
- Aembit dashboard shows credential access logs for each workflow run

## Testing
- Trigger claude.yml via @claude mention on a test issue — verify it responds
- Trigger claude-code-review.yml via test PR — verify review runs
- Trigger release.yml via test tag — verify Homebrew tap update succeeds
- Check Aembit dashboard for access events after each test

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for the Aembit GitHub Action OIDC trust provider to work with GitHub's id-token

description:
File: cli/internal/errordocs/errordocs.go (new)
File: cli/internal/errordocs/docs/.gitkeep (new, placeholder for embed)

Create the errordocs package that embeds error documentation markdown files into the binary via Go's //go:embed directive.

Functions to implement:
- Explain(code string) (string, error) — converts code like "CATALOG_001" to slug "catalog-001", reads "docs/catalog-001.md" from embedded FS, returns content. Returns error if code not found.
- ListCodes() []string — reads all *.md filenames from embedded FS, converts slugs back to CODE format, returns sorted list.
- codeToSlug(code string) string — "CATALOG_001" → "catalog-001" (lowercase, underscore→hyphen)
- slugToCode(slug string) string — "catalog-001" → "CATALOG_001" (uppercase, hyphen→underscore)

Key implementation details:
- Use //go:embed docs/*.md directive on an embed.FS variable
- Create docs/.gitkeep so embed compiles even before doc files exist
- No external dependencies — only stdlib (embed, fmt, path, sort, strings)

Success criteria:
- Package compiles with go build ./internal/errordocs/
- Explain("CATALOG_001") returns error (no doc file yet) — expected at this stage
- ListCodes() returns empty slice (no doc files yet) — expected
- codeToSlug and slugToCode are inverse operations

Commit: feat(errordocs): create errordocs package with embed.FS and Explain function

title: Bug: syllago add only copies primary content file, drops supporting files
description:
When adding a skill (or other content type) that has supporting files in subdirectories, syllago add only copies the primary file (e.g., SKILL.md) and ignores everything else (workflows/, scripts/, references/, etc.).

This means:
1. Library copy is incomplete — skill won't work correctly without its reference files
2. syllago publish also only publishes the single file (since library is the source)
3. Users silently lose content with no warning

Reproduction:
- Have a skill with subdirectory files: skills/atlassian/SKILL.md + skills/atlassian/workflows/**
- Run: syllago add skills/atlassian --from claude-code
- Check library: only SKILL.md is present, workflows/ directory is missing

Expected: All files in the skill directory tree should be copied to the library.

Root cause: The add workflow (internal/add package) likely uses single-file copy logic rather than directory-recursive copy. Need to trace AddItems() and DiscoverFromProvider() to find where files are enumerated.

Impact: Any multi-file skill or content item is silently broken after add. This affects the entire add → library → publish pipeline.
close_reason: Fixed: added SourceDir field to DiscoveryItem, copySupportingFiles() in writeItem(), 4 new tests

title: Enhance 'syllago inspect' to show content and provider previews
description:
Enhance the existing inspect command instead of creating a new 'show' command.

Current behavior: shows metadata (name, type, source, path, description) and file listing.

New behavior:
1. Default: show metadata AND content (the actual file text, not just the filename)
2. --as <provider>: show the content converted to that provider's native format with a header labeling the format
3. Keep existing metadata display
4. For multi-file content (skills), show the primary file by default

Examples:
  syllago inspect coding-standards              # metadata + content
  syllago inspect coding-standards --as cursor  # content in Cursor .mdc format
  syllago inspect coding-standards --as codex   # content in Codex format

This replaces the need for a separate 'show' command and makes inspect the one-stop command for viewing content in any format.

Needs tests for: default content display, --as flag with multiple providers, --json output, multi-file skills, error cases (unknown item, unknown provider).

title: Rules: Kiro canonicalize (inclusion/fileMatchPattern) + render update
description:
## Problem
Kiro rules have evolved: they now use inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description frontmatter fields. The converter's canonicalizeMarkdownRule() ignores all of these. A Kiro rule with inclusion:fileMatch and fileMatchPattern loses its conditional activation entirely. The render side outputs plain markdown instead of proper Kiro frontmatter.

## Required Changes
### Canonicalize
1. Add dedicated canonicalizeKiroRule() function
2. Parse Kiro frontmatter: inclusion (always/auto/fileMatch), fileMatchPattern, name, description
3. Map inclusion:fileMatch + fileMatchPattern → alwaysApply:false + globs:[fileMatchPattern]
4. Map inclusion:always/auto → alwaysApply:true
5. Map Kiro description → canonical description

### Render
1. Update renderKiroRule() to output Kiro-native frontmatter
2. Map canonical alwaysApply:true → inclusion:always
3. Map canonical globs → inclusion:fileMatch + fileMatchPattern (join globs for single pattern)
4. Include name and description in frontmatter
5. Keep prose scope embedding as fallback for complex glob patterns

## Success Criteria
- [ ] canonicalizeKiroRule() parses inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description
- [ ] Kiro rule with fileMatchPattern canonicalizes to globs array
- [ ] Kiro rule with inclusion:always canonicalizes to alwaysApply:true
- [ ] renderKiroRule() outputs proper Kiro frontmatter with inclusion field
- [ ] Round-trip: Kiro rule → canonical → Kiro preserves activation semantics
- [ ] Existing rules tests pass
- [ ] New test cases for Kiro rule canonicalize and render
- [ ] `make build` succeeds

## Files to Modify
- `cli/internal/converter/rules.go` (add canonicalizeKiroRule, update Canonicalize switch, update renderKiroRule)
- `cli/internal/converter/rules_test.go`

## Research Reference
docs/research/rules-research.md — Issue #1 (Kiro import), Recommended Action #1 and #6
close_reason: Kiro rules: canonicalizeKiroRule parses inclusion/fileMatchPattern/name/description. renderKiroRule outputs proper Kiro frontmatter. 7 new tests. All pass.

title: 2.2b: GREEN — Implement analyzer field strip at import boundary
description:
Implement stripAnalyzerMetadata and wire it into writeItem in cli/internal/add/add.go so the test from 2.2a passes.

## What to do

### Step 1: Add DiscoveryItem fields
In the DiscoveryItem struct (add.go or types file), add:
```go
Confidence      float64
DetectionSource string
DetectionMethod string
```

### Step 2: Add stripAnalyzerMetadata function
Add to cli/internal/add/add.go (or a new add_security.go file):

```go
// stripAnalyzerMetadata zeros out scanner-computed fields on any .syllago.yaml
// that was copied from a source package. Prevents a malicious package from
// pre-setting confidence/detection_source/detection_method.
// Non-fatal: load/save errors are silently ignored (metadata.Save overwrites anyway).
func stripAnalyzerMetadata(destDir string) {
    m, err := metadata.Load(destDir)
    if err != nil || m == nil {
        return
    }
    m.Confidence = 0
    m.DetectionSource = ""
    m.DetectionMethod = ""
    _ = metadata.Save(destDir, m)
}
```

### Step 3: Wire into writeItem
After copySupportingFiles block in writeItem, add:
```go
    if item.SourceDir != "" {
        if err := copySupportingFiles(item.SourceDir, destDir, filepath.Base(item.Path)); err != nil {
            r.Status = AddStatusError
            r.Error = fmt.Errorf("copying supporting files: %w", err)
            return r
        }
        stripAnalyzerMetadata(destDir)
    }
```

### Step 4: Wire analyzer fields from item into meta
After taint propagation block, before metadata.Save:
```go
    if item.Confidence > 0 {
        meta.Confidence = item.Confidence
        meta.DetectionSource = item.DetectionSource
        meta.DetectionMethod = item.DetectionMethod
    }
```

### Step 5: Use SafeResolve in copySupportingFiles
In copySupportingFiles, before each file copy, add:
```go
_, err := catalog.SafeResolve(srcDir, relPath)
if err != nil {
    continue
}
```

Also skip .syllago.yaml in the copy loop:
```go
if filepath.Base(relPath) == metadata.FileName {
    continue
}
```

Add import "github.com/OpenScribbler/syllago/cli/internal/catalog" to add.go if not already present.

## Success criteria
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/add/... -run TestWriteItem_StripsMaliciousAnalyzerFields` -> pass
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/add/...` -> pass
- `cd cli && go build ./...` -> no errors

title: 6.1: SCAFFOLD — viewTriage() full split-pane rendering
description:
Implement full viewTriage(), renderTriageItems(), renderTriagePreview(), and helper functions in cli/internal/tui/add_wizard_view.go. Replace the stub from 4.4.

## What to do

### Step 1: Implement viewTriage()
```go
func (m *addWizardModel) viewTriage() string {
    var lines []string
    titleRow := m.renderTitleRow("Triage: detected content", true, "Next")
    lines = append(lines, titleRow)
    lines = append(lines, "")
    lines = append(lines, "  Include items to add them, or skip (skipped items reappear next time you add from this source).")
    lines = append(lines, "")

    innerW := m.width - 2
    leftPct := 30
    leftW := max(18, innerW*leftPct/100)
    rightW := innerW - leftW - 1

    contentH := max(3, m.height-9)
    itemH := contentH - 2

    leftLines := m.renderTriageItems(leftW-2, itemH)
    rightLines := m.renderTriagePreview(rightW-2, itemH)

    topBorder := mutedStyle.Render("╭─ Items " +
        strings.Repeat("─", leftW-9) + "┬─ Preview " +
        strings.Repeat("─", rightW-11) + "╮")
    lines = append(lines, topBorder)

    for i := 0; i < itemH; i++ {
        left := ""
        right := ""
        if i < len(leftLines) { left = leftLines[i] }
        if i < len(rightLines) { right = rightLines[i] }
        leftPadded := left + strings.Repeat(" ", max(0, leftW-2-lipgloss.Width(left)))
        row := mutedStyle.Render("│") + " " + leftPadded + mutedStyle.Render("│") +
            " " + right + mutedStyle.Render("│")
        lines = append(lines, row)
    }

    sep := mutedStyle.Render("├" + strings.Repeat("─", leftW) + "┴" + strings.Repeat("─", rightW) + "┤")
    lines = append(lines, sep)

    btnLine := "  " + zone.Mark("triage-cancel", renderButton("Cancel", false)) + "  " +
        zone.Mark("triage-back", renderButton("Back", false)) + "  " +
        zone.Mark("triage-next", renderButton("Next", true))
    lines = append(lines, btnLine)

    botBorder := mutedStyle.Render("╰" + strings.Repeat("─", innerW) + "╯")
    lines = append(lines, botBorder)

    return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
}
```

### Step 2: Implement renderTriageItems() with responsive column collapse
See plan lines 1226-1282 for full implementation with three width thresholds (>=30, >=24, <24).

### Step 3: Add helper functions
- tierDotLabel(t analyzer.ConfidenceTier) (dot, label string)
- tierAbbrev(t analyzer.ConfidenceTier) string
- shortTypeLabel(ct catalog.ContentType) string

### Step 4: Implement renderTriagePreview()
Delegate to m.confirmPreview.View(), split on newlines, pad/truncate to availW.

### Step 5: Add (detected) suffix in viewDiscovery()
When an addDiscoveryItem has detectionSource == "content-signal", append mutedStyle.Render("(detected)") suffix to the item name column.

## Success criteria
- `cd cli && go build ./...` -> no errors
- TUI renders triage step without panicking when hasTriageStep = true
- Discovery step view shows (detected) suffix on content-signal items

title: Fix canonical hook timeout unit inconsistency
description:
WHY: Claude Code stores hook timeouts in seconds, Copilot canonicalizer stores milliseconds. Round-tripping a hook through syllago produces wrong timeout values depending on source provider. This is a data corruption bug.

FINDINGS:
- CC-4: hooks.go:296-297 (Claude Code seconds), hooks.go:434 (Copilot ms conversion)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Define canonical timeout unit explicitly (recommend seconds, matching Claude Code)
- All canonicalizers normalize to the same unit
- All renderers convert from canonical to provider-specific unit
- Round-trip: Claude Code -> canonical -> Copilot preserves correct timeout
- Round-trip: Copilot -> canonical -> Claude Code preserves correct timeout

TESTING:
- Unit: Test canonicalize from each provider with known timeout values
- Unit: Test render to each provider produces correct unit
- Unit: Round-trip test across providers

DOCS (syllago-docs/): Document canonical timeout unit in format spec
close_reason: Canonical hook timeout defined as seconds, all providers aligned

No CODEOWNERS file exists. Without it, require_code_owner_reviews has nothing to enforce. Security-sensitive paths (workflows, installer, sandbox, signing) should require explicit review from maintainers.

## What
Create .github/CODEOWNERS with ownership rules for critical paths.

## How
Create .github/CODEOWNERS with at minimum:
- /.github/workflows/ @holdenhewett
- /cli/internal/installer/ @holdenhewett
- /cli/internal/sandbox/ @holdenhewett
- /cli/internal/signing/ @holdenhewett
- /cli/internal/updater/ @holdenhewett
- /cli/internal/registry/ @holdenhewett
- /SECURITY.md @holdenhewett
Then enable require_code_owner_reviews in branch protection.

## Success Criteria
- PRs touching security-sensitive paths require review from @holdenhewett
- CODEOWNERS file is checked into the repo

## Blocked By
Repo must be public — CODEOWNERS enforcement requires branch protection features

title: M8: Sandbox auto-approves low-risk config changes via denylist
description:
## Why
After sandbox exit, config changes are classified as high-risk (mcpServers, hooks, commands) or low-risk. Low-risk changes are auto-applied without prompting. The denylist only checks three hardcoded strings. A provider could add new executable content types that syllago doesn't know about, and they'd be auto-approved as low-risk.

## What
Invert the sandbox config diff logic from denylist to allowlist.

## How (general)
- In sandbox/runner.go and configdiff.go, change isHighRiskDiff to use an allowlist approach
- Define explicitly-known-safe keys that can be auto-approved (e.g., theme, editor settings)
- Everything not on the safe list requires user prompt
- Alternatively: display all changes and auto-approve after brief delay with cancel option

## Success Criteria
- Unknown/new config keys require user approval after sandbox exit
- Known-safe keys (non-executable settings) are auto-approved
- Users see what changes are being applied even for auto-approved changes

## Testing
- Unit test: config change to known-safe key — auto-approved
- Unit test: config change to unknown key — user prompted
- Unit test: config change to mcpServers/hooks/commands — user prompted (regression)
- Unit test: new arbitrary key not in allowlist — user prompted

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M8
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

description:
WHY: Claude Code supports 4 hook types (command, http, prompt, agent) but syllago only handles command. HTTP hooks lose url/headers/allowedEnvVars, prompt hooks lose prompt/model, agent hooks lose agent config. These hook types are increasingly used in enterprise setups.

FINDINGS:
- CC-6: HookEntry struct only has command fields (hooks.go:19-25)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- HookEntry struct has fields for all 4 types: command (existing), http (url, headers, allowedEnvVars), prompt (prompt, model), agent (agent config)
- Canonicalize preserves all fields for all types
- Render outputs correct type-specific fields
- Unknown hook types pass through with warning

TESTING:
- Unit: Test canonicalize for each hook type
- Unit: Test render for each hook type
- Unit: Test round-trip for each type

DOCS (syllago-docs/): Document all 4 hook types in canonical format spec
close_reason: All 4 hook types supported: command, http, prompt, agent. Type-specific fields preserved, warnings for non-Claude providers.

title: Task 2.1: Remove README.md infrastructure from codebase
description:
# Remove README.md Infrastructure

## Why
README.md is an unnecessary layer of indirection. Skills have SKILL.md, agents are markdown files, hooks are configs — the content IS the documentation. Removing README simplifies authoring and reduces code complexity (parsing, rendering, warnings for missing README).

## Description
Remove all README.md handling from the codebase:

- catalog/scanner.go: Remove loadReadme(), readDescription() calls for README.md, README.md warnings, ReadmeBody field population
- catalog/types.go: Remove ReadmeBody field from ContentItem
- cli/internal/readme/: Delete entire package
- catalog/scanner.go: Update description extraction to use primary content files (SKILL.md frontmatter, first paragraph of agent .md, etc.)
- scanner.go:362: Remove README.md from isMetaFile() skip list — README.md becomes a regular file in the file tree

Note: Removing README.md from isMetaFile() means any README.md files in user-authored content directories will start appearing in the file tree. Call out in PR description.

## Success Criteria
- ReadmeBody field removed from ContentItem struct
- readme package deleted
- No code references README.md as a special file
- Content descriptions still populated from primary content files
- make test passes (after updating dependent tests)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: scanner discovers content items without README.md files
2. Unit test: description extracted from SKILL.md frontmatter description field
3. Unit test: agent description extracted from first paragraph of .md file
4. Unit test: hook description extracted from config file
5. Update catalog/scanner_dir_test.go (currently creates README.md in fixtures)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go
- cli/internal/catalog/types.go
- cli/internal/readme/ (delete)
- cli/internal/catalog/scanner_dir_test.go
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

title: L2: Uninstall RemoveAll without scope validation
description:
## Why
Uninstall() calls os.RemoveAll(targetPath) where targetPath comes from provider.InstallDir + filepath.Base(item.Path). No validation that targetPath is within the expected install directory tree. If a PathResolver is misconfigured, RemoveAll could delete outside scope.

## What
Add containment check before RemoveAll in uninstall path.

## How (general)
- Before RemoveAll, verify targetPath starts with the expected installDir prefix using filepath.Rel
- Reject paths that escape the install directory
- Similar pattern to the containment check in hooks.go:321-326

## Success Criteria
- Uninstall refuses to remove paths outside the expected install directory
- Normal uninstall operations are unaffected

## Testing
- Unit test: uninstall with path inside install dir — succeeds
- Unit test: uninstall with path outside install dir — rejected with error

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L2
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Full TUI codebase review: efficiency, accessibility, best practices
description:
## Goal
Comprehensive review of the full TUI codebase (cli/internal/tui/) by Go CLI expert agents, evaluating against current best practices for:
- Efficiency and code organization (state management, rendering, TEA architecture)
- CLI best practices (error messages, user feedback, progressive disclosure)
- CLI accessibility (NO_COLOR, screen readers, keyboard nav, color-independent meaning)
- TUI accessibility (focus management, modal patterns, status regions, form inputs)
- TUI patterns and best practices (modals, async UX, navigation, card layouts)

## Requirements
- Spin up research agents for each topic area to gather current (2024-2026) authoritative sources
- Feed research into Go expert agents that review the actual codebase files
- Produce both high-level pattern findings AND specific file:line recommendations
- Review scope: all files in cli/internal/tui/ plus supporting packages they depend on

## Success Criteria
- Each topic area has a structured findings document with citations
- Actionable recommendations are tied to specific code locations
- Findings are saved to docs/ for reference during implementation
- Critical issues (accessibility failures, UX anti-patterns) are separated from nice-to-haves

title: Implement cryptographic signing for hooks
description:
Implement cryptographic signing for hook manifests and scripts using Sigstore/cosign or GPG.

## Context
Jordan's enterprise security review (docs/reviews/spec-design-audit-enterprise-security.md) identified content hashing as insufficient for enterprise trust — it verifies integrity but not provenance. The spec defines a signatures field; this bead implements the signing workflow.

## Why
Without signing, any compromise of a registry git repo or MITM on clone silently replaces hook content. Content hashing only detects tampering if you have an independent trusted hash. Enterprise deployment (200+ devs) needs cryptographic provenance.

## What to Implement (Plan Only)
1. signatures field in .syllago.yaml — detached signature storage
2. syllago sign command — sign a hook directory's manifest + scripts
3. syllago verify command — verify signatures against a configurable trust policy
4. Trust policy configuration — keyring of trusted signing keys
5. Integration with install flow — verify before install, warn/block on unsigned

## Success Criteria
1. Plan specifies exact file paths, function signatures, and CLI command interfaces
2. Plan covers both Sigstore/cosign and GPG approaches with a recommendation
3. Plan addresses key management (where do keys live, how are they distributed)
4. Plan includes test cases for: signed+valid, signed+tampered, unsigned, expired signature
5. Plan considers the solo developer case (signing should be optional, not required)
close_reason: Implementation plan written: docs/plans/implementation/signing.md. All 5 success criteria PASS.

title: L4: MCP backup is non-atomic and single-depth
description:
## Why
backupFile uses os.WriteFile directly (not atomic temp-then-rename). Only one .bak is kept — overwritten on each operation. If crash during backup write, the .bak is corrupted. Hooks already use a proper snapshot system.

## What
Extend snapshot-based rollback to MCP operations (matching hooks).

## How (general)
- Option A: Use the same snapshot system hooks use (snapshot.CreateForHook equivalent for MCP)
- Option B: At minimum, make backupFile use the atomic write pattern (writeJSONFileWithPerm)
- Option C: Keep .bak but make it atomic and add rotation (keep last N backups)

## Success Criteria
- MCP backup files survive power loss / crash during write
- Multiple sequential operations don't destroy earlier recovery points

## Testing
- Unit test: verify backup file is written atomically
- Unit test: verify snapshot exists after MCP install

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — L4
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Hook spec Tier 3: Windsurf split-event adapter + new provider adapters
description:
Windsurf uses per-tool-category events instead of unified before_tool_execute + matcher.

Encoding rules:
- before_tool_execute + shell → pre_run_command
- before_tool_execute + file_read → pre_read_code
- before_tool_execute + file_write/file_edit → pre_write_code
- before_tool_execute + MCP → pre_mcp_tool_use
- before_tool_execute + wildcard → all four pre-events
- after_tool_execute wildcard → all four post-events
Decoding: reverse (e.g., pre_run_command → before_tool_execute + shell matcher)

Windsurf-specific:
- Timeout: dropped (no configurable timeouts) — emit warning
- Blocking: implicit via exit code 2; field dropped
- provider_data.windsurf: rendered (show_output, working_directory)
- MCP granularity lost: pre_mcp_tool_use fires for ALL MCP tools

Opencode adapter:
- Generates JS plugin files (NOT JSON) — fundamentally different
- Named typed hooks with matcher via input.tool
- Must escape commands for JS template literals
- Consider whether JS generation is worth complexity or document as limitation

Additional providers needing research:
- VS Code Copilot: full event support per spec, bash/powershell platform, env vars
- Codex: 5 events (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop), experimental
- Factory Droid: PascalCase events identical to CC — easy win
- deepagents-cli: dot-notation events
- Cline, Roo Code, Amp: may not have hook APIs yet

Depends on Tier 2 (adapters off legacy bridge) for full support.
close_reason: Superseded by syllago-flwc6 (Phase 1) + syllago-rdoba (Phase 4)

title: Task E2E: Provider smoke tests (Claude Code + Gemini CLI)
description:
# Provider Smoke Tests

## Why
File-level tests verify that loadout apply puts files in the right places. But only real CLI tools can confirm the content is actually picked up. These smoke tests catch provider path changes, config parsing quirks, and permission issues.

## Description
Separate CI workflow that installs Claude Code and Gemini CLI, applies loadouts, and uses programmatic mode (-p flag) to verify content is loaded.

### CI Workflow
- Runs on release branches and manual trigger only (not every PR)
- Gates releases
- Separate jobs per provider
- Auth: SSO/service account (setup TBD)

### Claude Code Tests

title: Task 1.6: Add JSON validity check for settings file merges
description:
# Add JSON Validity Check for Settings File Merges

## Why
readJSONFileOrEmpty() doesn't validate that the file contains valid JSON before sjson operates on it. If settings.json is malformed (truncated, hand-edited with syntax error), sjson silently produces malformed output or returns a cryptic error. The snapshot protects against data loss but the error path is confusing.

## Description
Add json.Valid() check after reading the settings file in readJSONFileOrEmpty(). If invalid, return a clear error: "settings.json contains invalid JSON; fix or delete the file before applying a loadout."

## Success Criteria
- readJSONFileOrEmpty() checks json.Valid() before returning file contents
- Malformed JSON returns clear, actionable error message
- Valid JSON files continue to work as before
- Missing files still return "{}" (no change to that behavior)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: valid JSON file returns contents
2. Unit test: missing file returns "{}"
3. Unit test: malformed JSON returns error with descriptive message
4. Unit test: empty file returns error (not valid JSON)
5. Unit test: truncated JSON returns error

## Key Files
- cli/internal/loadout/apply.go (readJSONFileOrEmpty, ~line 463)
close_reason: Added json.Valid() check to readJSONFileOrEmpty() with clear error message. 5 unit tests covering valid, missing, malformed, empty, and truncated JSON.

title: Hook adapter: Codex (experimental)
Fix symlink escape in sandbox config staging
description:
In configdiff.go copyDir(), symlinks are preserved as-is during config staging. A malicious project config directory (e.g. .claude/) could contain symlinks pointing outside the project directory (e.g. '~/.claude/config.json -> ../../sensitive.json'). When the sandbox modifies and ApplyDiff copies back, the target outside sandbox scope gets overwritten.

Fix: In copyDir(), resolve all symlinks to their targets BEFORE copying. Verify targets are within the original source directory. Reject symlinks that escape.

Files: cli/internal/sandbox/configdiff.go (lines 336-349)
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix: install command ignores per-type path overrides from config
description:
## Problem

The `syllago install` command (`cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go`) constructs a PathResolver at line 90 but only uses `resolver.BaseDir()` at line 140, passing a flat `baseDir` string to `installer.Install()`. This means per-type config path overrides (e.g., `provider_paths.claude-code.paths.skills = "/custom/skills"`) have NO effect on `syllago install`.

The resolver priority chain is: per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default. Currently `install` only honors the bottom three tiers. The `sync-and-export` and `loadout apply` commands both use the resolver correctly.

## Root Cause

`installer.Install()` (cli/internal/installer/installer.go:163) accepts `baseDir string` — a flat directory override. It always calls `prov.InstallDir(baseDir, ct)` internally. There's no way to pass "use this exact install directory" without changing the function signature.

The resolver's per-type path returns a final install directory directly (bypassing `prov.InstallDir()` entirely), but `Install()` has no mechanism to accept that.

## Fix Approach

Add `InstallWithResolver(item, prov, repoRoot, method, resolver)` to `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` that:
1. Checks `IsJSONMerge` first (unchanged — merge types don't use resolver)
2. Uses `resolver.InstallDir(prov, item.Type, home)` to get the effective install dir
3. Computes the target path from that resolved dir (same logic as `CheckStatusWithResolver`)
4. Proceeds with symlink/copy/converter logic using the resolved path

Update `install_cmd.go` to call `InstallWithResolver` instead of `Install` with `resolver.BaseDir()`.

Keep existing `Install()` unchanged for backward compatibility (loadout apply and other callers that pass baseDir directly).

## Key Files

- `cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go` — line 140 is the call site
- `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` — `Install()` at line 163, `CheckStatusWithResolver()` at line 114 (reference for resolver usage pattern)
- `cli/internal/config/resolver.go` — `InstallDir()` method

## Success Criteria

1. `syllago config paths set claude-code --type skills --path /custom/skills` followed by `syllago install my-skill --to claude-code` installs the skill to `/custom/skills/my-skill`, not `~/.claude/skills/my-skill`
2. Existing `--base-dir` flag still works
3. Config-level `base_dir` still works
4. Per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default priority chain holds
5. JSON merge types (hooks, MCP) are unaffected
6. Unit test for `InstallWithResolver` covering per-type path override
7. L2 command test verifying `install` command respects config per-type paths
8. All existing tests pass (no regressions)
close_reason: Added InstallWithResolver() to installer.go, updated install_cmd.go to use it. 7 new unit tests + 1 L2 integration test. All 8 success criteria validated.

title: M5: JSONC comments destroyed during merge
description:
## Why
For OpenCode, JSONC comments are stripped before merge and the clean JSON is written back — comments are permanently lost. For Zed (which uses JSONC natively in settings.json), no stripping is applied at all, so sjson may produce corrupt output. Either syllago corrupts the file or destroys user comments.

## What
Handle JSONC-format settings files correctly for providers that use them (Zed, OpenCode).

## How (general)
- Option A (preferred): Apply StripJSONCComments universally in readJSONFile and document that comments will be removed. This is honest and predictable.
- Option B: Use a JSONC-aware JSON manipulation library that preserves comments during merge.
- Option C: For providers with JSONC settings (Zed), refuse to merge and ask the user to configure a dedicated MCP file. Document the limitation.
- At minimum: fix the Zed case where no stripping happens and sjson gets JSONC input (likely produces corrupt output)

## Success Criteria
- Zed settings.json with comments does not get corrupted by MCP merge
- Users are either warned about comment loss or comments are preserved
- All provider merge paths handle JSONC consistently

## Testing
- Unit test: merge into settings file with // comments — verify no corruption
- Unit test: merge into settings file with /* */ block comments — verify no corruption
- Unit test: merge into settings file with trailing commas — verify handled
- Test with actual Zed settings.json format

## Threat Report
docs/security/threat-inventory-2026-03-26.md — Section M5
close_reason: Implemented in security hardening session 2026-03-26

title: Validate: Security Considerations expansion (S1-S16, T8, T11)
description:
## What

Validation gate for security considerations bead (syllago-7c550).

## Validation Checklist

1. **Coverage audit:** Create a checklist of all 16 security findings (S1-S16). For each one, verify it is addressed somewhere in Section 11. Mark any gaps.

S1: Unsigned content trust confusion → covered in?
S2: No revocation model → covered in?
S3: False lineage via derived_from → covered in?
S4: Authorship impersonation → covered in?
S5: Content-type mismatch → covered in?
S6: Name squatting → covered in?
S7: Version rollback → covered in?
S8: TOCTOU gap → covered in?
S9: generated_by spoofing → covered in?
S10: published_at self-reported → covered in?
S11: File permissions excluded → covered in?
S12: Composition bombing → covered in?
S13: Symlink TOCTOU → covered in?
S14: Unicode homoglyphs → covered in?
S15: Extra fields injection → covered in? (may be addressed by T2 allowlist instead)
S16: Sigstore identity confusion → covered in?

2. **No existing text modified:** Sections 11.1–11.7 must be byte-identical to the pre-edit version. Diff against committed version.

3. **Tone check:** New text must not be defensive ("this isn't a problem because..."). Must be honest about limitations ("ACP does not prevent this because...").

4. **No normative requirements:** New Security Considerations text should NOT contain MUST/MUST NOT/SHALL/SHALL NOT. Security Considerations describes risks and mitigations, not requirements.
   Exception: If earlier beads (content hashing, conformance) already added normative security text, that's fine — this bead should only add descriptive/advisory text.

5. **Cross-references valid:** Any cross-references to other sections (e.g., "see Section 5.2.2", "see Section 7 Implementation Note") must point to sections that actually exist after all beads are applied.

6. **Deprecation deferral:** Must explicitly state that deprecation/yanking is deferred to registries with rationale.

7. **Offline Sigstore:** Must mention SSH as offline alternative for air-gapped environments.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 16 security findings accounted for
- [ ] Sections 11.1–11.7 unchanged
- [ ] Tone is honest and non-defensive
- [ ] No new normative requirements in Security Considerations (descriptive only)
- [ ] Cross-references point to real sections
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: 7/7 checks PASS. All 16 security findings covered, Sections 11.1-11.7 unchanged, tone honest, no improper normative requirements, cross-references valid.

title: Centralize MCP field capability metadata per provider
description:
Currently each provider's MCP render function handles field inclusion/exclusion inline. Refactor to centralize capability metadata so unsupported fields are stripped BEFORE reaching the converter.

RULESYNC VALIDATION: Issue #1340 confirms this is a real problem — Copilot CLI MCP sync drops valid fields (tools, url, headers). Only command/args/env/type preserved. Round-trip conversion loses data. Their per-provider field stripping is incomplete.

SYLLAGO OPPORTUNITY: Our canonical format already preserves provider-specific fields through provider_data. The gap is centralizing the metadata so we know WHAT each provider supports without scattering that knowledge across individual converters.

PROVIDER CAPABILITY MATRIX (verified against rulesync issues):
- Claude Code: project+global, no enabledTools/disabledTools
- Cursor: project only, uses ${env:VAR} format (see syllago-ezssl)
- Copilot CLI: project only, uses "servers" key, LOSES fields on round-trip (#1340)
- OpenCode: project+global, supports enabledTools+disabledTools, inverts disabled->enabled, "environment" not "env"
- Gemini CLI: project+global, simple passthrough
- Kiro: project only, kiroAutoApprove/kiroAutoBlock
- Codex CLI: supports enabledTools+disabledTools
- Cline: uses alwaysAllow
- Roo Code: uses alwaysAllow, disabledTools, timeout
- Windsurf: uses serverUrl for HTTP
- Amp: amp.mcpServers namespace, includeTools only

DESIGN APPROACH: Don't copy rulesync's narrow field list approach (that's what causes #1340). Instead, define a capability struct per provider that declares SUPPORTED fields. Anything not in the struct gets preserved in provider_data for round-trip fidelity, but stripped from the rendered output. This way we never lose data — we just don't render what the provider can't consume.
close_reason: Done: MCPTransports, ConfigLocations, HookTypes now on Provider struct. Frontmatter fields derived via reflection registry. All provider capability metadata centralized.

title: Validate: Meta hash & field handling (T2, I1, I3)
description:
## What

Validation gate for meta hash rewrite bead (syllago-8lz06).

## Validation Checklist

1. **Allowlist completeness:** Count the fields in the meta_version 1 allowlist. Must be exactly 12: authors, content_hash, derived_from, description, generated_by, meta_version, name, published_at, source_commit, source_repo, type, version. No more, no fewer.

2. **Exclusions explicit:** Confirm meta_hash and signature are explicitly stated as NOT in the allowlist. Grep for both terms in Section 8 — they must appear in exclusion language.

3. **Unknown fields rule:** Section 8 must contain normative text (MUST/MUST NOT) about unknown fields: MAY be present in meta.yaml, MUST NOT be included in meta_hash computation.

4. **Type mapping table exists:** A table mapping every field from YAML type to JSON type must exist. Count rows — must cover all 12 hashed fields plus signature sub-fields.

5. **published_at string rule:** The type mapping entry for published_at must explicitly warn about YAML timestamp parsing. Must use MUST-level language.

6. **Boolean coercion rule:** Normative requirement (MUST NOT) prohibiting YAML boolean coercion must be present.

7. **Absent fields rule:** Normative requirement about absent optional fields: MUST be omitted, MUST NOT be serialized as null.

8. **Anchors/aliases rule:** Requirement to resolve YAML anchors and aliases before serialization must be present.

9. **TV-MH unchanged:** Verify TV-MH's JCS JSON string and meta_hash value are identical to pre-edit values. The JCS JSON must be:
   `{"authors":["alice <alice@example.com>"],"content_hash":"sha256:9c9c3591140eae4e0f047060470af98da00629b668f152ac6d4846e64ff91d40","description":"Reviews code for quality and security issues.","generated_by":"claude-code/4.0","meta_version":1,"name":"code-review","published_at":"2026-04-01T14:32:00Z","source_commit":"abc123def456789","source_repo":"github.com/alice/code-review","type":"skill","version":3}`
   meta_hash must be: `sha256:ff9c3684845115f9d204b1cef0a8a1b43d7648663ccdea503331bf5623d097d9`

10. **TV-MH2 unchanged:** Same check for TV-MH2. meta_hash must be: `sha256:cc2faf328de4c45bd5d9816c3709e29fffdbcb9438dcf076213079f6be7aedc9`

11. **No old language remaining:** Grep for "Extract all fields" in Section 8 — must return zero results. The old approach must be fully replaced.

12. **Cross-references intact:** Any section referencing Section 8 must still make sense with the rewritten text.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 12 checks pass
- [ ] Validator is a different agent than implementer
close_reason: All 12 checks PASS. Allowlist complete, exclusions explicit, type mapping table correct, TV-MH/TV-MH2 unchanged, no old language.

title: Fix OSSF Scorecard token permissions
description:
## Why
Scorecard workflow fails with: "Resource not accessible by integration" when trying to list commits via GraphQL. The GITHUB_TOKEN doesn't have sufficient scope for the OSSF Scorecard action's GraphQL queries. This will work differently once the repo is public.

## What
Configure proper token permissions for the OSSF Scorecard GitHub Action.

## How (general)
- Option A (public repo): Once public, the default GITHUB_TOKEN with contents:read should work
- Option B (if still failing): Create a PAT with public_repo scope, add as SCORECARD_TOKEN secret
- Option C: Add explicit permissions to the workflow: contents:read, id-token:write
- Scorecard docs: https://github.com/ossf/scorecard-action#authentication

## Success Criteria
- Scorecard workflow completes without errors
- Scorecard badge/results are available

## Blocked By
Repo must be public for default token to work with Scorecard's GraphQL queries
